# Thread: Leptin Reset journal.....or 'Why is the neighbor wearing sunglasses at night?'
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1438
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Endless — 2012-08-24T17:39:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46726>

I've been reading, and reading, and reading some more for the past several months....now time to actually try to get through this reset. I really want to be on the other side, but the more I read the scarier it gets, with so many people dealing with AI issues and hormonal conundrums that seem to stretch it out for so long!

I'm reluctant to gain weight, when I am really anxious to LOSE 25 pounds. I'm reluctant to shell out the approximately $1000 I'll need to spend on testing, and if I'm lucky a consultation or two, and the other $1000 I will be spending on supplements and food. Reluctant only because I don't exactly have the money....if I spend it it will be adding to my debt load. Thinking of balancing the cost of the journey with the added financial stress, which is counterproductive......

Aaarrgghh. No more thinking. Just do the damn thing already.

Starting info:

Age 43

Height 5'1"

Weight 160

### Endless — 2012-08-24T17:56:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46732>

Okay, I'm counting yesterday as day 1. Was half-assing it before that so no need to document it.

Breakfast 7am: leftover halibut (5-60z), half an avocado

Lunch 1pm: can of mackerel fillets in water, 1 tomato and 1/2 cucumber from the garden with TBSP balsamic, avocado oil, and TBSP goat feta. Followed by 2 TBSP coconut cream concentrate (manna?)

Dinner 6:15pm 6oz sirloin steak with butter, I beef sausage, sauteed onion and zucchini (about 1 cup), 1/2 cup plain greek yoghurt with stevia, another 2 tbsp coconut cream.

Drank LOTS of water yesterday. Put on my glasses at about 8pm...had the computer and tv on, both dimmed, and few lights in the house. Went to bed at 10pm. Woke once to pee, don't know what time as I kept my eyes closed. Woke again at 4:45am, really hungry! My stomach was aching....I think I ate way too much for dinner. Woke at 6am

Breakfast was filling, I was not hungry before 1pm, then not starving, just a good appetite. I WAS pretty hungry for dinner, and then I over ate. Maybe I need to add a bit more fat to lunch.

### Endless — 2012-08-24T18:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46735>

Woke a couple times in the night...once to pee, and once because I was hungry. Any night waking makes me a little anxious because often I can't fall back asleep easily. My fire radio needed a battery change and that woke me up as well. Too much waking! I was a little groggy this morning, stayed in bed awake for about 20 minutes before getting up.

Breakfast 7:15am Thai style raw beef (prepped last night from 6oz sirloin and marinated overnight) and 1/2 avocado with 1/3 cup sauerkraut. Had a cup of dark roast Yerba Mate (blended with chicory) with a lot of coconut milk. Hope that's enough fat to keep me satisfied till this afternoon.

### Endless — 2012-08-24T20:13:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46764>

First CT....I know I'm supposed to do it at sunrise or after dinner, but I felt like doing it now...it's only 3 hours after breakfast. I did the face dunks, in 50 degree water.

16sec

24sec

32sec

39sec

29sec

37sec

38sec

I couldn't find anywhere in the quick start guide where it says how much total time to shoot for per session. I don't have a skin thermometer, so I took a little break in between dunks, too, about 30 seconds.

I like it!

### Endless — 2012-08-24T20:17:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46770>

So it occurred to me as I was gathering up the bedroom throw rugs yet AGAIN to wash off the bloodstains left by my cats breakfast carnage...I was the last holdout in my household to a raw food diet!

Well, semi-raw. My 3 cats, dog, and snake are all 100%, but I will continue to eat some of my food cooked for now. Not quite ready for the fish head smoothie....although that thai raw beef was really yummy. I'm going to make the rest of those raw meat recipes in the cookbook too. I'd like to shoot for one meal a day consisting of raw seafood or meat.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-24T20:58:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46777>

Hi Endless. Enjoy the journey!

Sounds like you are doing very well with the face dunks. When I'm in my 55F bath, I turn over and put my face in for a few seconds (about 20) and repeat 2 or 3 times. No need for a skin thermometer.

Testing right now would be nice, as a baseline for comparison later on. Maybe just vitaminD, and HS-CRP (cardiac). But you can also just see how your body responds to what you are doing. Depending on your previous diet and your current health, you could see results fairly quickly.

### Endless — 2012-08-24T21:07:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46781>

Thanks, Nonchalant!

I actually did sign up for the email special, and Jack suggested some tests for me as well as strong suspicion of Hashimoto's and perimenopause. I'm in Canada, so some of the testing is not as easy to get (or at least costs more) so I am in the process of trying to get as much as I can for the least $$, so I'll be able to afford to retest.

I decided to go ahead and start the reset anyway, and hopefully the thyroid/hormone issues won't hinder me too much! Fingers crossed.

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-24T21:42:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46796>

You'll do yourself more good than harm with the reset, so that's great that you started it. You have to follow the AI protocol, especially if you suspect Hashis. Cutting the eggs, I felt much better within a few days! I miss them bc it was cheap BAB, but I feel like a complete different person and have so much more energy, I wouldn't go back. I know it's more expensive to eat that way, but so worth it. Try to find a place in the States where you can pick up suplpements, it would be cheaper. Eat sardines and mackerel in cans, it really cheap. I eat horse meat in the morning since I can't afford (and can't find much) grass-fed meat. It's lean, no anti-biotics, and very cheap. I eat fish and selfish at night, loading my freezer when they are in special.

Embrace this journey toward a healthier you

K

### Endless — 2012-08-25T06:43:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-46964>

Thanks for the tips! I have been keeping my eyes open for good deals on seafood already....

Today I bought 4 fresh salmon for $30! I skinned, deboned and sliced 2 for jerky, and froze the other two. Also found scallops, prawns, mussels and 'seafood medley' in the freezer section on sale. I already have smelts, sqid and sole in the freezer, so with today;s haul I should be set for awhile as long as I use the canned stuff once in awhile.

Lunch was a bit of a bust.....I was planning to go for sashimi but ran out of time and had to leave for a work call. Picked up a package of smoked salmon at the grocery store along with my fish haul and ate it on the road.

Dinner was cod with 1/2 an avocado and 1/2 a cucumber, followed by 1TBSP coconut cream for dessert. I actually had a tiny glass (4oz) of red wine but could have done without.

Did my face dunks again tonight, water 48 to start, warmed to 55 by the time I was done.

29sec

35sec

43sec

42sec

43sec

37sec

39sec

I don't find them that hard....hope the rest of my body is as cooperative!

No snacking again today, no problem....although right now I'd love a mate with coconut milk!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47012>

Good morning!

Nate, I buy a commercially made raw food for them. The cats alternate lamb and chicken, and my dog gets chicken meal plus chicken necks for breakfast. In the fall I get her chicken feet and heads from when people butcher and she eats those frozen for treats. last year I got a big bag of lamb livers and hearts from my neighbor, but this year I will eat them myself!

The cats supplement themselves with mice (hence the bedroom floor carnage) and my snake eats live mice.

### Endless — 2012-08-25T17:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47018>

Last night was the darkest yet....no tv and I FINALLY got f.lux to install on my computer. I broke out the candles. Found myself not as sleepy as usual, I wonder if the face dunks had anything to do with it? Was in bed by 10, probably asleep by 10:30. Woke up to my stupid radio chirping again...I have to get a new battery next week at practice, this is unacceptable. Managed to wake up and change it, plus a bathroom break, with eyes closed and I did fall back asleep quickly so that's good.

Woke at 6, opened the blinds and went outside for a minute. 4C!!! No! It's too early! I'm not ready for summer to be over

Still waiting for the sun to come up so I can look at it..lol Wondering how this will all work in winter when it doesn't get light here until 8am! I can't sleep 16 hours a day, but that's how long it will be dark outside in midwinter.

I had a cup of mate with cocnut milk this am....I wonder if that's a no-no with my shrimp and avocado breakfast.

I'm spending a lot of time reading threads....and I'm absolutely impressed by the level of knowledge here! I can't even follow a lot of the conversations about dosages of this and that, and timing and readings....I guess once I get my own labs done and get started I might be able to join in. I'm trying to read the blogs and get caught up, but the webinars are out of my budget right now, it's either that or the shrimp!

I'm supposed to go to a music festival later but I sure don't feel like it. It'll screw up my light exposure too, even though it's outside.

Wonder how wearing my UVEX would go over.....

### Endless — 2012-08-26T20:22:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47295>

Well, that was not an ideal night.....

I made chicken curry for dinner...from reading it sounds like I may go off chicken at some time in the future, but for now I have a lot of it in the freezer so I'm going to use it.

Finally decided to go take in the music festival with some of my family. I started out wearing my orange lenses, but it was just too damn hard to see anything, so I took them off. It was a dark night, but the lighted areas were pretty bright (vendors, etc...) so I kind of screwed up the circadian rhythm for the night. I left about midnight, got into bed by 12:30 but couldn't sleep for awhile.

My lower back and hips were just aching last night! I'm noticing an increase in inflammation, my elbows are hurting too. I'm staying away from eggs, dairy (have used a bit of butter) and nightshades, hopefully it's temporary.

Managed to sleep till 8, and got my breakfast in on time, sitting outside in the sun.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/47735=438-P1090554.jpg)

Tried the Korean style raw beef this time. I like the Thai better so far, but it was pretty tasty.

This might be the last good beach day, so the mutt and I will spend some time at the river later. I don't know what the river temp is, but I'll have a long soak, maybe it'll help my sore back and joints!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-26T21:34:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47312>

I've noticed a steady and gradual change in what I actually "want" to eat. From a year ago I would have shed a little tear thinking "you are going to quit eating chicken for the most part, and then will eat liverwurst, and only eat seasonal veggies. . . .and on."

I think our tastes change over time so it's easier done than said!

### Endless — 2012-08-26T22:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47328>

I think you're right, Cavemam...I've already noticed a change in my tastes. I just hope the chicken stays palatable for awhile... I buy all my meat for the year in advance and I have 10 chickens in the freezer plus some livers!

I am getting SO frustrated trying to find a place to get the labs done!!!!! Why the hell does it have to be so much harder in Canada?

Once I saw the Life Extension site I wanted to order from them because they have it all, but I can't. So I checked with ZRT...they have some of it but not everything I want. Same with Rocky Mountain Analytical.

So I can order from Eudamonia on my practitioner account and get;

Female Hormone+Adrenal (saliva) Cx4, Dhea, E2, Pg, T for $325.00 What about prometrium?

And if I go for the Adrenal only, it's just Cx4....no Dhea for $195.00

ZRT has:

Female Blood Profile II : E2, Pg, T, Dhea, C x1, shbg, TSH, Ft3, Ft4, TPO....what about the thyroglobulin, reverse t3 and preometrium??

Cardiometabolic Profile: Insulin, hsCRP, HbA1c, TG, Ch, LL, HDL, VLDL

I don't want to shell out all this money if I'm not getting enough info for it

Rocky Mountain Analytical DOES have an Omega 3 score that sounds interesting if it isn't too expensive.

What Dr. Kruse suggested for me was COMPLETE thyroid panel, with TPO and anti-throglobulin and rT3, complete female hormone, Vit d, HS-CRP, cortisol/dhea.

I wonder if I could make an appt for a blood draw in Spokane and if they would do it....then I might be able to use Life Extension instead....

### Endless — 2012-08-27T06:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47440>

Oh, I hope so....I'm reading about the gut flora a lot and worried that I might be populated with a bunch of nasty little beasts!

Spent the late afternoon at the river....had my can of mackerel, cucumber and kombucha for lunch and then tried a little river CT. It wasn't very cold, about 65, but I got goosebumps so hopefully it was somewhat beneficial. Stayed in 25 min, to the extreme puzzlement of my dog.

My salmon jerky turned out great! I vaccuum sealed it and the dehydrated zucchini 'noodles' I made. They make a kick-ass lasagna...that is if I can ever eat tomatos or cheese again! It's all going into my SHTF stores anyway, for now.

Dinner was mussels on the half shell (came frozen like that) cooked in avocado oil, garlic and water. I also ate avocado and some sauteed mushroom and onion (in avo oil as well).

Eating out tomorrow night, hopefully it goes well!

### [email redacted] — 2012-08-27T15:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47470>

You are doing great!!  How do you do the salmon jerky?

Pg is progesterone, that's what you want to get tested. Prometrium is fake Pg (medication).

Could you get the Life Extension kit delivered to the States and pick it up? You are pretty close to the borders, aren't you?

If you could have those tests with a blood draw, it would be more accurate (except for Cortisol x 4). You need female hormones panel, full thyroid panel with anti-bodies (both), Hs-Crp, Vit D, Cortisol and DHEA. You don't really need the Omega 3 for now. I know it's a lot. I know it's expensive. But you HAVE to know what is going on. That way, you'll put your efforts and your money working (supp, BHRT) on the right things. You will also be able to monitor if what you are doing is working. I know it's stressfull to put all that money on those labs, especially when you are on a thight budget, but you have to do it for yourself and to feel better ASAP.

How does it work is your Dr prescribe you the blood testing? Would it be cheaper?

### Endless — 2012-08-27T19:38:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47501>

I've already gotten what I can from my doctor....she's not that willing to prescribe testing for some reason. All I can get through her for thyroid is TSH. I'm hoping to figure out a way to get the Life Extension stuff....

I made the salmon jerky on a whim...I read it on here somewhere and decided to make some. I cut my skinned and deboned fish into strips, marinated it in coconut aminos, ginger, garlic and a couple drops of stevia overnight, then dried it in the dehydrator. I took it out overnight so it wouldn't attract bears (it's out in my shed) and did a bit more the next day till it was very dry. It's tasty, I will make some more when I pick up some more fish!

### Endless — 2012-08-28T19:31:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-47805>

Things are looking up...I called Life Extension and they will fax me the requisitions for blood testing and I can go get the blood drawn in Post Falls Idaho, only 150 miles away! That's exciting.

I also have a call in to see a local doc who is open to using natural hormone replacement and will likely be more open to ordering more testing for me, but she's got a waiting list, so I don't know when I can see her. I might go ahead with the LE tests and hope she can get me some of them for follow-up testing later.

I forgot to mention, I did my first full body CT at the river on Sunday...I took the thermometer with me and the temp was not super low, only 65, but the water was moving so it seemed pretty chilly! I stayed in 30 min. I had goosebumps the whole time, which I seem to remember reading was good....

Went out to dinner last night with the family for my SILs moms birthday. The place we went was recovering from hosting a local misic festival on the weekend so limited menu (lunch menu). They had salads, wraps, rice bowls and sandwiches. No chance of getting a steak or anything. I ended up ordering a 'build your own' salad, trying to avoid the things I didn't want, but still ended up with peppers, tomatos, pumpkin seeds on it. The only protein available was chicken, so I ordered double chicken plus avocado. had some herbal tea while everyone else drank wine.

We'll see how it goes heading into this next week (PMS week) but so far I am not having any problems with cravings or snacking. I've eaten a bit more coconut cream than necessary a couple of times, but not excessive. I am still waking up kind of draggy and tired though. Making sure to see the sun asap, although I am up before it comes over the hillside.

Someone coming to look at my landcruiser today....hope it sells!

### Endless — 2012-08-29T04:44:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-48019>

Sold the truck! I am relieved but kinda sad....I was attached to it.

No lunch today....I ate breakfast (cod and avocado) at 7am, and wasn't hungry around noon (plus I was showing the truck). I got hungeer pangs around 2:30, and again an hour later, but I was out and about and there was nothing convenient. Supper at 6:30. Is that too long between meals, I wonder? I know many people skip lunch, but if I am hungry I'm thinking I should still be eating 3 meals. Better stash some of that salmon jerky in my car.

### Endless — 2012-08-29T18:32:55+0300

I hope I start waking up more rested....I seem to be really fatigued most mornings. Still dragged my butt out to walk the dog before work.

I couldn't face meat or fish this morning so I made a coconut milk smoothie with whey protein, egg yolks and MCT oil. A lot of fat...I feel a bit queasy from it. I have some big shrimp ready for lunch with avocado and cucumber.

I have to do something about Tuesday nights....the fire hall is REALLY bright, and I didn't get home till 9:30, past dark. I guess I'll just start wearing my UVEX and endure the joking that's sure to come of it.

### Endless — 2012-08-30T08:20:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-48311>

I will! They already think I'm strange so what's one more addition to my eccentricity.

I had a slightly embarassing moment today....I was helping my sister in law with some garage clean up when I suddenly smelled something, well, fishy. I asked her if I smelled strange to her or if I was tripping, so she sniffed my neck and said I smelled like fish, a little. OMG,why would that happen? I ate shrimp for lunch but I didn't get it all over me or anything.....now I'm going to be paranoid.

In other progress news, I seem to be almost craving free, and I'm not really hungry between meals...although I am still on 3 meals a day. I'm assuming that's a good sign!

I also noticed a wee bit of the 'litterbox' odor to my urine that I read about somewhere in the blog posts.....but I've been reading so much in the last few days that where I read it and what it pertains to have completely escaped me. Frustrating!

### Endless — 2012-08-30T18:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-48395>

### Endless — 2012-08-31T04:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-48504>

Actually, all of the cats were transitioned from dry food so they had already built up deposits. I use a powdered enzyme product in their water that's supposed to help get rid of the plaque, and I also use a gel that I put in their mouths. Both have helped a lot. They eat mice and the occasional bird, so I guess they get some bones to scrape against too.

As far as I know, raw diets in general are supposed to build up less than canned or dry.

### Endless — 2012-09-01T18:23:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-48749>

What I did wrong on Thursday:

\-dinner at 9pm

\-3 glasses of red wine

\-ate the mashed potatos with my steak

\-nighttime light exposure till 1am

\-bed at 2am

\-no CT

What I did right on Thursday:

\-breakfast and lunch timing and substance was good.

\-had fun! (that's gotta count for something...)

I went to the pride event being held nearby for the first time ever in this particular town, so I wanted to support it. Plus my SIL was doing a burlesque with a couple of friends so I wanted to see her performance. had a good time. I forgot to specify no potatos on my plate so could not resist them when they came. Got home late, slept only 4.5 hours, and felt like crap yesterday.

I still got up and did BAB (although all I could do was a protein/coconut milk smoothie, I was running late. Ended up eating a bag of salmon jerky at lunch because I was caught short again and could not wait long enough to get home and make something.

Bed was early last night, I slept well and woke up feeling pretty energetic!

Scallops and avocado for breakfast....I wish I could afford to eat these every day! Bought the sushi-grade ones to eat raw. They are so sweet!

### Endless — 2012-09-09T17:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-50490>

I might have to investigate the possibility of getting some more fashionable blue blockers....I realized last night that there will be times, especially in winter, when I am still out working after dark, often in awful bright lighting. Even driving will expose me. Last night I was still out at 8pm doing a job at a gas station...for the drive home (1hr) I put on my sunglasses. It's too dark to be ideal for driving with those on though.

Got this in the email this morning....makes me worry more about my fbg....

> Friday, September 7, 2012. The September 4, 2012 issue of the journal NeurologyÂ® published the finding of Australian researchers of an association between high normal plasma glucose levels and a decrease in brain volume in nondiabetic men and women. Although research has established an association between type 2 diabetes and cognitive impairment, the current study suggests an adverse effect on the brain for glucose levels considered by most authorities to be within a normal range. "Numerous studies have shown a link between type 2 diabetes and brain shrinkage and dementia, but we haven't known much about whether people with blood sugar on the high end of normal experience these same effects," commented first author Nicolas Cherbuin, PhD, of Australian National University in Canberra. "These findings suggest that even for people who do not have diabetes, blood sugar levels could have an impact on brain health."
>
>
>
> The study included 266 men and women between the ages of 60 and 64 enrolled in the PATH Through Life Study, which is a longitudinal study of aging. Fasting plasma glucose and other factors were measured upon enrollment, and those with glucose levels of 6.1 micromoles per liter (110 mg/dL, classified as impaired fasting hyperglycemia by the World Health Organization) or higher were excluded. Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans of the brain were conducted at the beginning of the study and four years later.
>
>
>
> Fasting plasma glucose levels ranged from 3.2 to 6.0 micromoles per liter (57.6 to 108 mg/dL) upon enrollment. Dr Cherbuin and his colleagues uncovered a significant association between a decline in volume in the brain's hippocampus and amygdala (cerebral structures affected by aging and neurodegeneration) and higher plasma glucose levels within this nondiabetic population. "Plasma glucose levels were found to be significantly associated with hippocampal and amygdalar atrophy and accounted for 6%–10% in volume change after controlling for age, sex, body mass index, hypertension, alcohol, and smoking," Dr Cherbuin and his associates write. "These findings suggest that even in the subclinical range and in the absence of diabetes, monitoring and management of plasma glucose levels could have an impact on cerebral health."
>
>
>
> When asked if there is a specific level at which plasma glucose would begin to be considered risky, Dr Cherbuin told Life Extension: "We found that the top two highest quarters (or quartiles) had significantly more shrinkage than the lowest one which might suggest a tentative cut-off of 5.1 \[micromoles per liter\]. However because overall the association with hippocampal atrophy across all glucose levels was relatively uniform, more evidence was needed to point to a specific cut-off."
>
>
>
> He concluded that "More research is needed, but these findings may lead us to re-evaluate the concept of normal blood sugar levels and the definition of diabetes."

### Endless — 2012-09-12T17:03:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-51196>

6.9 this morning!!!!(124). I feel very close to TOM and yesterday I was running late so ate something less than optimal...prawns battered in panko. They came with kimchee and rice, and I ate the rice. I can't think of any other reason for the high reading unless it's cycle related. That is crazy high.

I was also exposed to far too much light last night, at practice and afterwards at home where I have a houseguest staying. I'll break him into the routine today, he arrived late last night so it was not really the right time. I had a hard time getting to sleep.

Took a set of measurements the other day....I hope to see some changes in the belly region! No shape change, but I AM losing weight despite being near my period. I don't know how I could possibly not lose , I'm eating pretty low carb. I'm planning to enjoy an apple or two pretty soon, they are almost in season!

### PaulaRichards — 2012-09-12T18:18:26+0300

I have to say I am having the same problem with getting my Dr. to order the blood work I need. Did you go with LE? If you find a lab that does the testing we want let me know please. I want the exact same tests that you mentioned. I already had saliva testing done for hormones and cortisol. I'll try my Dr. one more time but if it's a no go I'll have to pay, which, in the end, increases my debt load too, but like ABetterMe said we need the testing to know where to focus our efforts.

Hope you're having a good day!

### Endless — 2012-09-14T03:02:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-51555>

Hi Paulalynn!

I just ordered the saliva tests from ZRT (my chiropractor was listed on their website, score!) but I think that I will be going with LE for the rest. I've registered for the account with them and I was told that I just have to order the tests online, print out the requisition forms and take them to the nearest labCorp location. For me that is in Idaho, but it's only a daytrip.

I've got an appt. with my doc next week and I will TRY to get her to order thyroid tests by telling her I suspect Hashimotos, but I must admit I'm not really that hopeful.

I did 15 min CT yesterday and 20 min today...the tap water is down to 56F. Yay me!

### Endless — 2012-09-17T17:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-52113>

TOM was not as great as I expected....more cramps and clotting than I've had in a long time. I don't know why I was expecting it to be better.

I have an appt with my regular doc on Friday, and I'm hoping that before that I can manage to get through some more of the blog to find some associations for my high FBG readings that might persuade her to help me with either some thyroid testing or hormone testing. I have company right now and have not had much time to get on and read...have not even read the new post yet. If anyone reads this and has any ideas on effective arguments for my doc I'd be thankful for any advice!

I managed 30 minutes of CT yesterday...I need to get one of those things to cover the overflow so my belly and boobs stay under...I flipped over for the last 10 minutes.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-18T00:18:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-52222>

> **Endless;52730 wrote:** TOM was not as great as I expected....more cramps and clotting than I've had in a long time. I don't know why I was expecting it to be better.
>
> I have an appt with my regular doc on Friday, and I'm hoping that before that I can manage to get through some more of the blog to find some associations for my high FBG readings that might persuade her to help me with either some thyroid testing or hormone testing. I have company right now and have not had much time to get on and read...have not even read the new post yet. If anyone reads this and has any ideas on effective arguments for my doc I'd be thankful for any advice!
>
> I managed 30 minutes of CT yesterday...I need to get one of those things to cover the overflow so my belly and boobs stay under...I flipped over for the last 10 minutes.

Serious CT will give you some erratic estrogen dumps and make you think your lady parts have gone wild. I had a couple of months that freaked me out because of the things going on down there. I stopped the CT to clear out for tests - and whoa, in 2 months all was back to regular and normal cycle .

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-52609>

Yikes...I guess I'd better stop then. I'm hoping to get my testing done fairly soon....

On that note, I found a great swimming hole at the river today (private) and got nekkid and sat in the river up to my neck for 20 min. Seems to be about the same temp as the tap water, maybe a degree or two higher so I'm going to call it 60. It was nice to sit there in the sun!

I went back to the grocery store where I found the cod livers and this time they had cod roe (pressed, canned) so I decided to try it. It was really yummy, sliced like spam and fried in a bit of butter (I know, not optiimal). Unfortunately I belatedly read the label to discover potato starch and tomatos. Oops.

\*\*\*\*...I don't really want to stop CT for the tests, but I guess I have to if I want to have an accurate idea of what's going on.

Did another 30 minute river CT yesterday, with my dog looking on and wondering what I was doing.

I took the plunge and signed up for a 30 min and a 60 min consult before the price goes up. I have 3 months to use them so I think it'll be worth it in the long run. Creddit cards sure come in handy sometimes.....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-53807>

I am so LOVING the river CT! It wont last much longer, as I can't see myself sitting in there when the weather turns. Right now the sun is still shining and when I get out I can sit on a rock naked and dry off. I checked the temp yesterday and it was 59. It would be ideal if I could continue as it naturally drops, but I have to walk 20 min or bike 10 to get there and if it is cold and wet outside I won't.

I downloaded the latest webinar but won't be able to listen until I have some time at home. I wish I could put it on my ipod!!!

### Endless — 2012-09-25T18:42:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-54143>

Another marathon work day about to begin and I still doidn't get a chance to listen to the webinar I will miss the Q&A as well tonight, I hope it will be recorded.

Wow, I am really missing CT the days I don't get a chance to do it! I still can't bring myself to do it in the evening, my body temp drops around dark and I am usually already cold.

### Endless — 2012-09-29T05:44:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-55155>

My computer is in the hospital.....guess it didn't like the glass of red wine I poured into the keyboard last night

I tried my first MHS today!! Actually it was 3 large whole sardines. It tasted fine, the only issue was the scales, which I obviously didn't get off very well, didn't blend at all so the texture wasn't great. I have to fix that.

Went to get my blood drawn for the TPO test...still pissed at my doc for being so stingy with the other test. There is ONE doc in town who is very into BHRT and tests for everything and anything...of course he is not taking any new patients...not even a waiting list.

in other news, I am down some inches...about 1 inch in each place I measured! And down about 10 pounds as well. I am pretty happy about that, although if I fluctuate on the road to getting my hormones in shape I will be ok with that.

I'm doing pretty well with the diet....no nightshades or eggs, no grain legumes or sugar (except wine). I'm eating seafood at least once a day. CT about 4-5 a week. I have had a houseguest for the past couple of weeks so light exposure has been higher than I'd like, but come Sunday i'll be back to the swing of things.

### Endless — 2012-10-04T20:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-56273>

I just have a normal blender, not a vitamix or anything fancy. With the sardines what I have to do is remove the scales...they don't blend!

Feeling crappy this morning, I ended up eating a tiny bit of quinoa last night, as well as mayo in the salad dressing. Had several glasses of red wine. I don't feel like doing anything, but I have a huge list of 'outside' chores to get done before I go to Edmonton and before the weather changes. Better drag my butt out and do some before I end up getting called to work.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-04T23:26:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-56327>

QVC had one as a special of the day a couple of weeks ago and for 5 "easy" payments. He whined so I ordered it. Right now he's only using it to blend the butter into his morning coffee. pfbbbt. Our old blender did that just fine. I want to try making soup in it but am thinking about a fish head smoothie. gack. Cannot believe I just said that.

Sorry you aren't feeling well. Might have been the wine. That usually does it to me which is why I seldom drink.

### Endless — 2012-10-04T23:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-56335>

Yes, pretty sure it was mostly the wine although there is a bit of digestive rebellion from the grain I think.

I think wine and I are coming to the end of our relationship, sadly. It makes me crave things that I don't want to eat, as long as 24 hours after drinking it! I was just walking down the street seriously being persuaded by my self talk to go eat pizza when I saved myself by ducking onto a sushi place and ordering sashimi and seaweed salad.

I can't afford to eat sashimi for lunch when I am out, I better start planning better.....

### Endless — 2012-10-06T00:22:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-56535>

Nice day in the garden..so far no call to work, yay! I got the carrots and beets harvested and moved a few perennials into one spot to overwinter. Started prepping the garlic bed, I want to plant it next week. Nothing left in there now except lettuce and chard!

I don't understand why I crave higher carb veggies in the fall and winter...winter squash especially. Its exactly opposite of what I should be eating now.

30 minutes CT in the tub today....river CT might be over for the year, although I might have tried it had I known it was going to warm up this much today. My tap water is down to 53 from 57 last week!

### LinD — 2012-10-06T00:51:11+0300

### Endless — 2012-10-06T18:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-56603>

I know, right?

I'm down in the 140s, woohoo! Well, 149, first thing in the morning naked and empty....but still. I'm happy about that.

BAB of sole fillets and bacon with sauteed zucchini and beet greens in tumeric. I am using some of my garden veggies while they are still here, soon enough I'll be down to avocados and seaweed.

I ate a single, perfectly ripe PEAR yesterday....OMG it was good. What a treat!

### Endless — 2012-10-08T00:35:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-56824>

My water temp is dropping fast! It was 51F today, the coldest yet. I submerged my entire back, and the front up to my solar plexus, minus the (smaller!!) belly island....I only stayed in for 20 minutes. I really noticed it being much colder, and I can see now why doing REALLY low temps requires someone else there. I live alone, so I will be unlikely to go too deep.

I stayed naked to warm up...it took longer as well. I sat in the sun a bit while I was doing it to help things along. Still goosebumpy after an hour and a half.

My heart has been pounding more noticeably since after the CT...wonder if it's related.

Got the garlic bed prepped and ready to plant tomorrow. I am contributing potatos, brussel sprouts, onions, carrots and beets to Thanksgiving dinner tonight. I might even eat a bite or two of them. I found an awesome recipe for roasted brussel sprouts with bacon, balsamic, toasted walnuts and a bit of maple syrup. Smells pretty good!

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-08T05:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-56859>

### Endless — 2012-10-09T18:33:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-57112>

> I love that I know what a belly island is.

I love that it's an island and not a penninsula!

Well, I blew it a bit for thanksgiving dinner.....ate a bit of the stuffing and also some of the chocolate cream pie my niece made. The rest of it was not bad but I did eat a few potatos and a bit of squash. Went two days with no seafood or CT at all...

I have a day off today, unexpectedly. I'm getting my winter tires on and planting garlic...other than that I should have time for CT and some basking in the sun

### Endless — 2012-10-10T08:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-57261>

I got back in the river today, on a whim....probably flashed a few people driving by on the other side but whatever. Went for a long walk with my SIL while waiting for my tire change and it was warm and sunny so I stripped down and jumped in. It's getting pretty cold!

I need to get some shrimp or at least some squid, I have been craving shellfish and haven't had any for awhile.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-11T02:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-57474>

I was 150,6 this morning, can't wait to see the 140s again!!

I hope you had a great Thanksgiving! I dont know why, but that's something that is not celebrated here by French speaking Quebecers....No feast for me, and my belly is happy

### Endless — 2012-10-11T03:47:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-57506>

My belly would be happier had I not eaten the ham or turkey...but I was not the chef. If I host it next year it will be oysters and crab by candlelight.

ANOTHER bonus river CT today!! I am getting seriously addicted. I only stayed in 15 minutes (alone and the temps are getting lower) but warmed up naked in the sun quickly. I never shiver....only goosebumps. Maybe when I lose some more of belly island that will change.

Driving to Edmonton Friday...I'm worried about my diet and CT for that week. It might be snowing there so I can always just underdress...not sure how to get any tub time in. My dad is allergis to raw shellfish so I won't be buying any of that while I am there either. Maybe face dunks and sardines will be the plan.

### Endless — 2012-10-20T09:59:58+0300

### Endless — 2012-10-22T18:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-59657>

Yikes...going away is more trouble than its worth sometimes I think. Still trying to get caught up. I managed my first CT in 10 days yesterday though, and stayed naked for a couple of hours afterward. 30min @ 50F in the tub, then I was really uncomfortably cold for the first 20 minutes out. Goosebumps stayed for hours but I was ok. Forgot to check my temperature before and after.

My gut is NOT happy....I didn't do TOO badly while I was gone but my flora seems to have turned to evil fauna....wicked gas and bloating. I've had raw sardine smoothies two days in a row now and kept it clean but things are still very unsettled down there.

I have an appt. with a doc that I think will be supportive this afternoon!!! So excited. I have been frantically trying to find the links people posted about the benefits of Metformin so I can print them out but I have to leave fro work. Search isn't showing anything. Darn.

### Endless — 2012-10-23T18:55:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-59835>

I am so relieved that I got in to see this new doc! She is interested in the CT and the LR, and wrote up some testing for me no problem. I am getting P, E2, T, HSCRP, B12, TSH, thyroglobulin, T3, T4, Vit D, DHEAS, fasting insulin and fasting blood glucose.

Waiting till day 21 to test, so 11 more days. My concern now is that I get some results back in time to book a consult before the end of the year! I bought a 1/2 hour and an hour consult at the old price, but they have to be used by the end of Dec. it doesn't help that I can't download the forms..just gives me an error message.

### Endless — 2012-10-24T04:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-59938>

It tried to snow today....pretty soon I might be able to roll around in the snow for my CT. I used to do that a lot, but it was after being in the wood fired sauna and working up a major sweat.

I hope my belly settles down soon. Just ate some ground elk simmered with some coconut milk and tumeric over my spaghetti squash from the garden and I'm having some issues. I even took a betaine HCL with it.

Wonder if a probiotic enema would do anything....

### Endless — 2012-10-24T18:21:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-59991>

Missing my chiropractor appt this morning because of work Hopefully I can reschedule for later today. I want to pick up my saliva test from their office and I really need an adjustment.

finished off my 'large sardines' with another smoothie this morning. I think I have it down now....run them under tepid water and rub off the scales, chop, add lemon juice, salt, water and parsley and blend until no chunks are left. It's a good replacement for my other fast option which has been whey protein. I'm still feeling some digestive upset but I'm hoping it settles soon!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-24T20:35:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60013>

How big are those sardines? The ones I've been getting come packed in spring water in a tiny "envelope-y" thing. They are BIG compared to the tinned ones and look more like kipper snacks. And they headless but whole. There's 3 to a pack. I just dump 'em on a plate, salt 'em and gobble 'em up. bones, skin, everything. Of course they've been processed ... are yours fresh?

You may want to ask your doc to add in the reverse T3 test too. I know I know my issues aren't everyone elses but why not get the whole picture instead of just part of it? Just a thought.

### Endless — 2012-10-25T03:49:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60072>

I would love to, Darleen, but as far as I can tell that test is not available in Canada. I'm thinking of doing a trip to Idaho to get it but for now I want to get this stuff done and get a consult in before it expires.

The sardines I bought are frozen whole and raw. They are about 6-7 inches long and still have heads. They are imported from Portugal and come about 10 to a bag. Once you get the scales off they blend up pretty good in the vitamix. I also have cooked sardines in cans but these are my version of the MHS, since I never see fresh mackerel.

I did NOT want to get in that tub today...in fact the water is down to 48F and I was not feeling up to it so I brought it up to 55, stayed in 20 min. I can see this being an issue as the weather continues to get colder. I have to crank the heat up in the house a bit before too, if I am already feeling chilly there's no way I'm getting in!

### Endless — 2012-10-25T07:18:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60087>

Oh, that reminds me...my dad gave me a Vitamix while I was in Edmonton!! He got one with airmiles because he needed to use some, and I commented on it so he gave it to me! I never expected to have one so I am excited. The sardines blend up so much nicer than in the old B&D.

### Endless — 2012-10-25T18:07:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60149>

148lbs this morning....I'm glad my trip didn't throw me too far off in that dept at least! I'm hoping that getting back to CT will spur some more loss. Especially if once I start messing around with my hormones I end up gaining.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-10-25T18:21:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60153>

I'm glad your new doc ordered those tests for you. Reverse T3 is available in Canada. My BHRT pharmacist said the problem is most docs won't order it, unless the other thyroid test indicate further testing is needed. So frustrating! But it sounds like you're getting the majority of important tests done. The only one my doc ordered for me was vit D (in addition to the tests "he" thought were appropriate like the lipid profile).

### Endless — 2012-10-25T19:40:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60165>

That's good to know, I think that I should be able to convince her to get me RT3 if things show abnormal this time around. Hell, I'll even pay for it if that's the issue!

I am lucky to have gotten in to see her,I know. I hope you can get your doc to come around!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-25T19:40:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60166>

And the problem is they look at the TSH and the T3 and if they are within "normal" parameters won't look any further. That is EXACTLY what I have been fighting for over 30 years. Then there's the "let's talk calories" and "are you exercising" BS. I wish I could be more insistent/forceful/adamant with my doc. He just blew me off when I asked about the RT3 with "oh we only order that if we suspect disease." Then he added the calorie bit. grrrrrr

I don't have any advice, unasked for or otherwise, about how to handle this with your doc. I just think that if someone is having difficulty losing weight and is being honest about their diet (like eating clean, low carb, no carb, paleo or epi-paleo) then the RT3 really should be checked just to be sure.

Feel free to use my story with your doc if you think it will help.

### Endless — 2012-10-26T05:18:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60265>

Thanks Darleen! I am printing out some stuff from Stop The Thyroid Madness to take in with me, I think that will help too.

I had an upset stomach this morning, not heartburn exactly, more like pressure and fullness thst never quite developed into nausea. Burping, too. It reminded me more than anything of when I had Giardia in Guatemala 15 years ago. I didn't eat lunch today and was not at all interested in food until about 5pm. Then I unfortunately made a dinner that didn't turn out that well so I was less than satisfied One of those days.....

### Endless — 2012-10-26T17:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60315>

Tried a new kind of fish in my smoothie this morning....atlantic horse mackerel, aka 'stickleback'. I kmow why they are called stickeback...they have a line of sharp pokey things along each side. I removed what I could see and blended the crap out of them but still there seemed to be some sharp things in it so I strained it through a colander. Ended up with more of a fish 'juice'. Oh well...maybe I will stick to the sardine/herring. taking the scales off is no big deal.

Fish juice wasn't filling enough, I was starving by 10:30! Held off eating till lunch and then I had a cheat meal....an apple, some imported raw milk sheep and goat cheese, and a venison smokie, followed by some goat milk kefir with stevia and vanilla. Yum! I am going to miss apples.

Had a doppler test today, I don't really think I have a heart problem, but best to rule it out. The more I read the more I think it is all thyroid related.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-26T23:12:00+0300

### Endless — 2012-10-28T19:01:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60599>

You guys are making me wish I wasnt so violently reactive to caffeine....the coffee sounds good.

I had a royal sh\*t-show of a tumble off the wagon yesterday. I had a crappy sleep and had to go to work in the morning, just for a bit, and on the way home I had an uncontrollable urge for pizza which I totally gave in to It was gluten free, but still. I ordered a 12 inch pizza and took it home and proceeded to lie on the couch ALL DAY and eat the whole thing. I didn't even wallk the dog.

The weather turning might have something to do with it. I don't mind cold but I hate damp. It's been both lately, and kind of gloomy. I'm sure I'll adjust soon.

The other thing that's annoying me is that my digestion seems to be getting worse instead of better. I have gas from both ends, and bloating, and a constant feeling like the gorge is rising in the back of my throat. I hate it. Not only that, but my Bristol Chart is WAY better when I cheat and eat crap, like I did yesterday....what's up with that?

Had a pound of (cooked) oysters and an avocado for breakfast. Now I feel yucky again.

### Endless — 2012-10-28T23:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60637>

I have accomplished exactly one thing today....25 minutes of CT. I actually have to add warm water to the tub to get it up to 50....not ready to go below that. I didn't get goosebumps while in the tub, and didn't have them for very long afterwards either. I'm not sure what that means, I have to go and re-read the CT series. I've been thinking that the goosebumps are a good thing. I don't shiver either. Hmmmm.

### Endless — 2012-10-30T06:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60872>

> Then why not just drink the HWC straight? I've done that. mmmmm yummy

I did this a lot on Atkins. I love it, but am also off dairy now so I'll drink a bit of coconut milk instead.

I've come to the conclusion that a lot of my resistance to the cold is emotional and habitual...I was outside this morning unloading my car with a hat and fleece jacket on, and when I came in I realized I was very warm. I decided to go for a walk with the dog in yoga pants and a sleeveless t shirt. (40f outside). I was not cold, but I was hesitant to leave the house without a jacket. I think I have been conditioned, or have conditioned myself, to believe that being cold is unacceptable and the possibility of getting cold must be avoided at all costs by always having a coat/blanket, whatever with me wherever I go.

I forced myself to stay naked at home yesterday, too. Again, I was not cold, but had to continually fight the urge to put something on because I was not entirely warm, either. The house is not cold, it is about 68. I am at least becoming more aware of this habit to cover up all the time.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-30T13:19:14+0200

Maybe you are becoming adapted??? You are supposed to "heat" up when adapting, not freezing your arse off. I am waiting for that day.

### Endless — 2012-10-30T17:40:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-60945>

I would love to think that is it, but I'm doubtful. I keep forgrtting to take my temp before I get in but the few times I have it has always been slightly down after. I am going to keep plugging away!

Got in 20 min in the tub this morning early...a first! I couldn't bring myself to go below 55 today though. I then went for a 30 min walk outside (40F) in my t shirt again. Should have worn gloves as my hands were painfully cold but the rest of me was ok. Still had to overcome that moment of resistance as I stepped out the door...now that I am aware of it I find it really interesting.

Had to skimp on the breakfast to get the ct in, so today is whey, mct, and coconut milk in the blender. I have some kick ass sauteed prawns in turmeric for lunch though!

Very mild here today, it's almost 50F outside right now. No wonder it was easy to walk in a tshirt. About to jump in 50F water....amazing how much colder that feels! This time I will remember to take my temp before and after.

### janagram — 2012-11-04T20:14:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-62058>

### Endless — 2012-11-04T22:42:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-62086>

It is good, darn it. I keep 'tasting' it to make sure.

Why is it that I can't get all my damn ducks in a row on the same day??? If CT is good my diet or my light sucks. I feel like I'm sabotaging myself sometimes.

This morning I was proud of myself because despite being startled awake by an emergency medical call and then exposed to bright light at 1:30am, I managed to get back to sleep (popped a melatonin) and sleep till almost 7! Then I had a MHS for breakfast and went for a walk in my Tshirt. THEN I did 30 minutes of CT at 49F. I'm feeling pretty darn proud of myself by now.

Since then I've proceeded to 'nibble' non-stop on the aforementioned beet salad, raw sheep milk cheese and bacon. Oh, and chocolate I can count on one hand the number of days I have eaten between meals since I started this. So why today, when I did everything else right? Pisses me off.

I did take my temp pre CT: 98.3

Immediately after: 97.6

30 minutes after: 97.1

90 minutes after: 99.2

Of course, it went up after the nibble-fest started. Not sure if that should count.

### Endless — 2012-11-05T07:02:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-62154>

Nibble-fest progressed into family dinner....I ate more of my beet salad, some beef stew my stepfather made (probably had flour in it, definitely potatos) and a small serving of the apple and lemon pies (but I didn't eat the crust). I overate.

Tomorrow should be a lean day....maybe BAB followed by sweet F-all!

I notice my hunger increases after CT even though Jack says it isn't. I'm sure he said if your hungry it means something is out of whack. To get around this I usually try to CT later in the day - but not too late as then I can't sleep cause I'm shivering/freezing!

### Endless — 2012-11-08T18:09:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-62914>

Yes, I suppose that is most likely....I have been almost afraid to CT after about 2pm because I know if I leave it too late I'll be too cold after. I'm looking forward to my test results to see where I'm off.

> (you're adorable too, endless)

Aw, thanks LOL

I had a long day yesterday so decided to treat myself to dinner out. I had a gift card somewhere so I went and had their dinner special, which was salmon. What a disappointment. I 'paid' $23 for something I could have made 300% better at home. Oh well, I guess I need that reminder occasionally.

Today I am back on the MHS train. I used 3 sardines this morning and this time I was careful to remove ALL the clear scales, plus pull off the little fins and hard 'cheek plates' before O blended them. MUCH better, no scales clogging the straw. I can't say I crave the taste, but I feel good after I eat (drink) my meal.

I scored 5 fresh lamb's livers and hearts for free yesterday from my neighbor. I cleaned and thinly sliced the hearts and they are now marinating for my lunch and or dinner today. I need to find a source for heart, I find it an easy organ meat to incorporate raw. Liver I don't know...I wondered about adding it to a smoothie or something but I don't know if I could do it. I sliced some of the liver to cook, instead. The whole family (me, cats, dog) were in the kitchen this morning eating scraps of raw heart together...

May I just step in here and say BLECH. RAW HEART? shudder not for this kid. More power to ya though.

Not eatin' raw fish. No way no how. I'll take my sardines out of the pouch or can thank you very much. But good on you for going there. Whatever works.

Liver? mmmmmmmmmmm but only lightly cooked. LIGHTLY being the operative word. Man I love liver. keep the fava beans and the nice chianti. I'll just eat the liver with some fried onions. oooo delish.

### Endless — 2012-11-10T06:58:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-63311>

I am hoping that my test results should be in by next week....I booked an hour consult with Dr K the last week of Nov, and a follow up 30 min appt the last week of Dec.

### Endless — 2012-11-12T20:00:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-63850>

Today is my 44th birthday. I'm feeling a bit sad and emotional....partly because of TOM arriving today I'm sure. But also because I am lonely.I miss being in a relationship, having someone who cares about me and who I can share things with. I don't really even have any friends that live near me....life as an introvert definitely has it's downside. SO, I've taken the dog out for a walk (my most loyal fan ), and am about to jump in the tub. Maybe the cold will snap me out of this funk. I made an appointment later today for a massage. Sadly, that's probably the only human contact I will get today....but I will enjoy it immensely, my back is feeling pretty stressed.

My brother and SIL took me out for dinner on Saturday night...had a good meal of filet mignon and scallops...I indulged in some fancy 'martini' type drinks and even had a poached pear for dessert. I enjoyed the drinks but it's not something I miss much...I'm finding them too sweet. Maybe I'll swing by the fish market after my massage and see if there are any oysters. I'll make myself a romantic dinner for one..lol

### Endless — 2012-11-13T22:19:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-64112>

The massage was intense, so I wouldn't say it was all that relaxing, but it is what I needed. My entire back was like wood. I should really go back in a few days for another session but I probably wont. Too much $$$ and I have trouble with setting that as a priority. Maybe once I get more on track with the LR and all that I will focus on the spinal stuff more.

> I hear ya sister! It's the catch 22 of introverts! It's just damn hard to make friends when you don't enjoy the initial superficial chatter required to get there. I've been there - I hope the ct and massage help!

I am having a hard time unlearning the shiny tough exterior I chose to hide behind as a child....everyone seems to think I'm super self reliant and don't need any help or anybody for anything. Of course, that's not true but it is my own damn fault for cultivating that.

I'm looking forward to my consult with Dr K....I need to get into some more specific goals and start to measure my progress so I don't start to backslide.

### Endless — 2012-11-14T17:20:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-64259>

Ok, funk or not I am back on the program...I indulged myself a bit over the weekend and up until yesterday, including the drinks on Saturday, gluten free pizza and a brownie the next day, some organic corn chips with salsa and refried beans, and some less-optimal chocolate than usual. Could be worse. On the upside, I did CT regularly and sorta kinda used my Uvex.

This morning was shrimp sauteed with turmeric and an avocado. I will not let this deviation become a full fledged slide. I still have a weight goal I would like to meet by the end of the year, and I want to be able to start working out by then as well because I am signed up for Tough Mudder in June (OMFG....)

### Endless — 2012-11-17T21:11:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-64988>

25min CT at 51F. Starting oral temp 98.0, afterwards 98.5 It's a gloomy wet day and all I feel like doing is curling up in a WARM place with a book. Getting in the tub was harder today.

Still feeling pretty low...I often have a hard time with my mood when the light levels drop....wonder if it's harder this year because I'm not doing anything about it, like using my SAD light.

### Endless — 2012-11-18T20:38:39+0200

Why aren't you? If it helps why wouldn't you?

I understand the gloomy thing. We've been overcast here most of the month but very little snow. It's just sorta cold and mostly dreary.

My water temp is the same as yours. I did 40 minutes total and am still shivering. It was tough getting into the water and I did a LOT of gasping. But I just made myself SIT and then made myself LIE BACK. brrrrrrrr.

What do you do for entertainment (i.e. to take your mind off of things)? I'm watching reruns of True Blood on my iPad. Keeps me from getting bored, helps me stay in the tub longer and keeps my mind off the cold water. Which, I have to admit, I start to enjoy by the end of the session. I always know when it's time to get out because I start getting antsy. Sure sign I've had enough.

ANYway. Get out that SAD light!!! Life's too short to be down in the dumps. hugs!!

### Endless — 2012-11-18T21:52:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-65202>

Thanks Darleen..I haven't been using it because I've been trying to avoid artificial light. I guess I should consider it again. I will also ask Dr K. when I have my consult.

I've also tried listening to the webinars but more often than not I get more agitated if I do that, instead of being able to relax!

I'm actually ok staying in the tub, but I dread getting out because I get so much colder...I haven't gone 40 minutes yet mostly because I'm afraid I'll take hours to warm up. I'll have to find something really riveting to watch for that.

Can't you use the SAD light during the day? Or doesn't that help? I have a lamp on my desk that runs about like a sun lamp for intensity. I'd turned it off months ago but now keep it on during the day when I'm at the computer. We don't have any natural light in this room. It's like a cave, LOL.

### endless — 2012-11-23T18:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-65439>

I'm just doing cold water....not sure if I will add ice, my water is in the mid forties anyway. I have no intention of going lower than that, at least for some time.I am not doing every day, or even every other day...I get 2-3 in per week. I am not able to bring myself to do evening baths because I take so long to get warm and I'm already chilly at night, so on really busy days I just don't do it.

I haven't been using the light in the day because of this forum...somewhere I read a thread in which Jack said that ANY artificial light, ANY time of day, was bad, and someone specifically asked about SAD lights and it seemed like a big no-no. Of course, I live much farther north than a lot of people here and in a place where it is often socked in, not sunny, in the winter so perhaps that makes it more acceptable, who knows.

I was rushing to get to practice on Tuesday night and cut myself really deep on a stupid can. So instead of practice I drove myself to the hospital for 6 stiches on my thumb. It's made working a bit of a challenge....

### endless — 2012-11-23T22:45:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-65485>

It seems to be healing up fairly quickly considering...today was much better!

I don't understand why we have to reset our passwords....I thought the whole point was to have the same password for everything. i can still log in and it shows me as endless, so I'm not resetting it until I know the reason behind it. I like my password.

I got my saliva tests back today, so I'm going to be faxing them to Jack for my consult on Tuesday. My estradiol is below the ref. range, and my DHEA is reallly low as well. Actually, everything is low. My cortisol is pretty low too, although it's highest in the morning (yay!) and goes lower for the rest of the day. I'm seeing the new doc tomorrow as well so hopefully will have some prescriptions by next week and get started on the hormonal optimization!

### endless — 2012-11-24T19:15:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-65524>

My appointment with the doc went ok...although she doesn't think there is a thyroid issue at all. My T3, T4 and TSH are all in range, and antibodies also, but I know Dr K will be able to see further into it. She is open to new info though and I am going to give her my Skype recording of my consult with Dr K to listen to afterwards.
For now she is suggesting progesterone supplementation and also possibly short term DHEA. I am going to be more diligent with my adrenal support supplements as my cortisol is a bit low. It is on the right schedule though, highest in the am and falling through the day which I am happy about!

Well, this morning I took the plunge below 50F....only lasted 10 minutes, and only my lower body. Tomorrow I will try to sink the upper half in. It's significantly colder!

I am visualizing my ass fat cells exploding, that helps me stay in there..lol

### endless — 2012-12-02T22:20:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-66189>

### endless — 2012-12-07T11:24:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-66680>

Wow, busy week! I've been trying to get serious with the CT and now I will do it even if I only have 10-15 minutes to spare. I just drop the temp for those quick ones. Today is one of those days, I don't have much time this morning before work.

I think my sleep is getting better! I slept 7 hours last night with only minimal restlessness. If I would remember to take my magnesium at night it might be even better.

I also broke down and ordered a 'Cool Fat Burner', despite the high shipping cost to Canada. I know I can use that later in the day when I would not jump in a cold bath. Maybe I'll be able to get two CTs a day in with it.

Back to see my new doc on Saturday, and get the results that were missing last time, and share my consult with her. I am trying to push my next consult forward by a couple of weeks since I will not be getting on the progesterone/DHEA until next week.

I just ate a can of mackerel for breakfast...it wasn't something I'm going to be craving, but it's 60g of protein. I will have to spice it up next time.

### endless — 2012-12-08T16:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-66841>

Just had another appointment with my doc and got the last stragglers of test results that I didn't get last time. HS-CRP is 0.3!!!! I am so excited about that! My fasting insulin is 46 (ref is <95), which is pretty good. I sent my doc the mp3 of my consult with Dr. K and she listened to it before my appointment and is totally on board with everything he said, even using a vaginal progesterone which she doesn't really do. She's going to call the compounding pharmacist on Monday morning and get a custom cream made up. Woohoo!

### endless — 2012-12-13T13:33:47+0200

Well, popped my first DHEA this morning....anxiously awaiting boundless energy and blissful sleep! lol...or not. Whatever I notice I am sure that taking this is a good thing for my body so I'm excited about it.

I MIGHT have had an awesome sleep last night if not for a 4am wake up to attend a medical emergency. I don't feel comfortable heading out in my UVEX (not to mention driving in them) so I am in the bright light.

### endless — 2012-12-13T19:48:45+0200

### endless — 2012-12-14T14:19:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-67515>

Day 2 of DHEA. Sleep was good, despite nodding off on the couch around 10 for an hour. I should have gone to bed, but trying to train myself to stay awake a bit longer in the evening.

Tried the fat burner vest this morning...it's ok. I need to experiment more. It doesn't seem to fit very well, but perhaps once I get used to using it that will change.

Raw sardine smoothie for breakfast today with lemon juice and seaweed. I feel so virtuous! lol Actually, it's because I'm out of bacon, or I'd have eaten a nice bacon/salmon fry instead.

I bought little boxes of Ferrero Rocher for some of the people who deliver to me and stuff....funny thing is, I'm feeling kind of guilty about giving them sugar as a gift. On the other hand, I doubt they would appreciate the sentiment behind a can of sardines!

### endless — 2012-12-15T12:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-67576>

Diane, I got my DHEA by prescription, I figured it was going to cost me more to make the trip ovber the border...but I plan on getting some that way soon. I am saving empty vitamin bottles to use for that!
Hi Caroline! I'm afraid that my journal is not very educational....I've been missing Souldanzer, Cavemam and Ealachan. I always learned something reading those ones!

Breakfast was a huge fail today. I had frozen bay scallops and planned to sautee them lightly and have them with some organic bacon and avocado. They were so salty I couldn't even eat them! I tried rinsing them, but they seem to be salty all through. I gave up and fried a couple eggs, and topped my protein up with a whey and kefir drink afterwards. I am soaking the scallops in water now, to see if that will desalinate them. If not, I won't be buying those again!

I've been thinking about some of the more 'radical' ideas coming up around here lately....going off grid, for one. I don't see it as a big deal, personally, because I've already lived like that. As a kid we were 40 miles from town on a semi-maintaioned logging road, no plumbing or power. We hauled water from the creek, had an outhouse and a wood stove, and propane and kerosene lamps for light. No tv, but we did pick up CBC radio.So I know I could do that again...I have a wood stove, and gravity feed water. I'm not going to give up my computer or tv unless or until I have to, but if I have to I will be ok with that. I could use some light options though....I'm going to keep my eyes open for some of the old glass kerosene lamps we used to use. Wouldn't hurt to pick up a couple of those and a jug of fuel to keep in the shed. I already have a small store of canned food and stuff like that, and my fishing gear.

Tonight is the Christmas party at our fire hall. I will try to eat clean (meaninig avoid potatos!) and limit myself to 2 glasses of red wine and NO SUGAR.

Oh, and my wolf urine arrived in the mail yesterday, so the pooch and I will spend some time hiking around the perimeter of the pasture putting up scent markers...hopefully the damn coyotes will take a hint and stay away.

### diane — 2012-12-15T15:02:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-67585>

Really? You can get DHEA by prescription? I thought we couldn't get it at all in Canada. I didn't even have the foresight to use old vitamin bottles - just brought it straight back LOL.
