### endless — 2012-12-15T16:57:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-67594>

I don't even bother with the quote thingy, I just do it by hand. I was getting too frustrated. Yes, you can get DHEA and pregnenelone both by prescription now, only for the last couple years I think. It's far more expensive, though. If I'm bringing things back from the states that isnt sold here or is sold only by prescription, I usually transfer it into some benign container, like a vitamin C, and stick it in my overnight bag....I don't want to chance having it taken away at the border.

I am totally paranoid about the stupid coyotes now. I chased two away this morning that were standing there barking, trying to goad my dog into chasing them. They took off up the hill when I took off in their direction. I took a bottle of wolf pee and hung dispensers in a line up the powerline access, and then I squirted the rest of the bottle on the trees and fenceposts at the back end of the field. I hope they take the hint and bugger off.

I just read Martin's journal and now I am craving oysters.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-16T00:07:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-67623>

Damned coyotes! Â I've only seen one since we moved here ... it was the mangiest looking thing I've ever seen. Â Ah the joys of living out in the wild. Â (and we're not even all THAT wild here!) Â 

I do have to laugh though ... we went overnight camping a few years ago up to Paint Rock Lake. Â We got there before the weekend so had the place pretty much to ourselves. Â I dropped right off (always sleep the BEST out in the mountains) but was awakened a few hours later by what I thought was a bunch of rowdy, drunken cowboys partying hearty. Â DH was already awake. Â "Coyotes," was all he said. LOL

### endless — 2012-12-17T13:02:03+0200

I had an epic lazy Sunday yesterday, topped off by a completely non-paleo dinner. I made a veggie quesadilla with rice flour tortillas. Also had some hummus. I enjoyed it immensely, but still felt guilty about it. I made up for it with a halibut and avocado breakfast.

I'm not really wanting to give up the avocados, although clearly they are not in season here....EVER. lol I depend on them for a lot of my fat.

### endless — 2012-12-19T22:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-68021>

I took a day off today....from work, and from most of my protocol. Did my BAB as usual, but for lunch I actually had a WHEAT bun. I don't know how long it's been, but I was at a sushi/burger bar for lunch and just couldn't resist. It was a nice soft squishy sourdough one. I might be slightly redeemed by the fact that it was an ahi tuna burger....raw fish on a bun. lol Talk about your good and evil. Whatever, it was good and I enjoyed it, and I will now return to my bread-free existance.

Had an intense 90 minute massage today as well, because my body has been in rebellion lately. I'm so tight and stiff that I required a lot of pummelling. So now I'm minimally less tight and stiff, but I feel like I've been run over by a semi. I have one more gift certificate to redeem, so I made another appt for 2 weeks. In the meantime I need to get serious about stretching more. Not to mention the massage therapist I have is mighty attractive...

CT makes me stiff....I don't know if it's because the cold is making me tense up or what, but I always have upper back, shoulder and neck stiffness the next day. No CT today. If anything I will have a warm epsom salt bath before bed.

Of course it's snowing like crazy all day, so after my massage and lunch I had to come home and move snow. just the thing to relax my back...(insert sarcastic smily)

There is an abundance of raw meat in my field....if I only had a way to harvest it! How do they know when the season is over??? Didn't see any of them around until after the end of it.

### endless — 2012-12-20T13:27:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-68066>

My nephew did his first face dunks last night...he's 8!

Wow, will wonders never cease??? I FINALLY have a normal looking reply window, with font I can read, and emoticons that work! I guess submitting a ticket was the way to go, after all! Still no 'quote' button, but whatever....

I woke up to another 8 inches of new snow, which I was planning to quickly deal with before work before it warms up and gets heavy...but of course the stupid snowblower isnt working

I swear that thing seems to run well for one good session and then it needs a new belt or some other piddly thing. So instead I spent an hour pushing snow with the big scoop. So much for the nice massage I had yesterday. I'm back to being stiff and sore. I'm hoping to get some CT in today, maybe it'll help. I feel kind of crappy after my cheat food yesterday. That's a good thing, it'll help me remember why I don't eat that stuff anymore. Plus I've gained back 2 pounds in the last week or so...Don't really want that to continue either.

I spent some time outside with my dog, but mostly just puttered around the house and watched a movie on tv.Â 

Back to business as usual today....I'm going to try to get my CT in before I head off to work. I should really have done it yesterday when I had lots of time, but it was one of those days where I was extremely NOT into being cold.

I'm finding myself slowing down on the seafood, simply because I need to conserve some money right now. I really don't want to cancel my silver membership here, which is another thing I've consiered temporarily to cut costs. I just can't justify buying a bunch of seafood when I have a freezer full of elk and GF beef. I have been eating it raw, mostly...except the ground, I won't eat that raw unless I grind it myself. I linger longingly over the jumo sea scallops in the store though....

I do often eat a can of sardines for lunch, in the car. Just have to remember to never again buy the unsalted ones...yuck.

I felt the onset of an upper respiratory thing all day yesterday...by last night I was sure it was inevitable. I've been taking osha root, usnea lichen, goldensela, and oregano oil, as well as olive leaf extract. That and my raw dinner and breakfast seem to have helped a lot. I made a version of hot buttered rum last night too, with dark roast yerba mate, chai spices, cinnamon, butter and a smidge of rum. I'll do it again this morning but skip the rum. Don't really need it.

).

I've only gained two pounds back but my measurements are bigger again, now I think I understand why. I have slacked off in every area except CT. Had grand plans tonight, to sear some ahi and make a fancy marinade....by the time I got home and fed the critters and other such chores, I was feeling inert. I ate a few pork rinds and some of my coconut oil 'fudge' and called it good. I have raw tuna in the fridge for breakfast instead.

I was eating a tin of sardines in the car for lunch today...so I was parked, and cracked my door to drain the liquid out of the tin. One of the sardines fell out! Forgot to mention, I went to the doc again the other day...she's funny, I asked her what her opinion was on what I should do about something, and she wanted to know what Jack thought! I had to tell her that while I appreciate her willingness to follow his recommendations for me I really was interested in her opinion as well! Too funny.

Anyway, she wrote me a lab rec. for my next day 21....including IGF-1, even though she said she's not sure they'll actually do that one. My vitamin D has gone up from 80 in April to 122 in November! (That's nmol/L, ref range 75-200). I'm pretty happy with that

### diane — 2013-01-02T12:50:46+0200

Although I did have to pay for the Vitamin D panel and the IGF-1 - but those are Ontario rules, I think. Those 2 are important to me, so I was happy to pay for them. Yet still scratching my head as to why we would have to pay for a Vitamin D panel in CANADA in DECEMBER. LOL. Think of how many people it could help if they just realized their Vitamin D was low! Every year in the past I have felt the effects of Seasonal Affective Disorder - and last year was the first that I didn't!

### endless — 2013-01-03T00:34:17+0200

I had scallops and avocado for breakfast, and then GF beef with avocado and tomato for lunch. For the first time I was not the slightest bit hungry for dinner. Well, maybe the slightest bit. I ate an ounce of raw milk cheese and a couple little squares of my coconut oil 'fudge' and that seemed to do it. I guess we'll see if I wake up starving! I'd like to get to the skipping lunch stage of the Leptin reset....today I ate a lot at lunch but most days I eat just a can of sardines or similar.

Tried to cook squid again the other night....didn't work out so well. I could use some tips if anyone has any favorite ways to cook it!

### endless — 2013-01-04T17:31:46+0200

Thanks, Ashryn!

My neighbors are probably starting to wonder about me, what with the sunglasses and the shovelling snow INto the house.....lol!

So I woke up to TOM this morning, I think.....not sure though because it seems to have stopped. It's only cycle day 26. This is the first cycle on all the HRT, so I'm wondering if that's a good thing or a bad thing. I am usually regular at 28-30 days. I guess I'll see if it's just spotting before I record it as the start of a new cycle. I've never been a spotter, so if it is that's a new thing.

I am feeling resistance to the tub today. Did face dunks this morning for the first time in a while, water temp 44F. It was tough, I kept getting immediate aching over my sinuses and having to come up. It seemed to go away as I kept at it, though. I'll try to use the vest today at some point, too. Don't think I'll be doing any full immersion today.

I have work to do elsewhere but I'm stuck here waiting for the damn phone company to show up

They won't even give a ballpark on what time he MIGHT show up, so if I leave I might be SOL.

### endless — 2013-01-05T15:14:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-69461>

Well, phone company did NOT show up...I wasted a lot of the day waiting. I didn't even want to get in the tub for fear of having a knock on the door. Come to think of it, I probably should have done just that. Had a sub-optimal evening....didn't eat properly, fell asleep on the couch with tv on, didn't get up and go to bed till 1am. I slept till 7:30 though, with a wake or two in between.

Morning has been an exercise in avoiding the two things I should be doing...CT and walking the poor neglected dog. I started out by trying to do some computer work that I abandoned last night...got to the stage where I'm supposed to plug my USB in and it was missing! Located it under the stove (damn cats...), noticed that under the stove was disgusting, as well as under the fridge. Finished the computer work, pulled out the stove and fridge to remedy the situation. Finished that, noticed the windowsills were moldy from condensation...went around the entire house and sprayed 15% peroxide on them all to kill the mold, then around again to wipe dry. This led into treating the houseplants with peroxide as well to kill fungus gnat larvae in the soil, and replace the sticky traps in each plant.

Tub is still sitting there full of cold water, dog has given up and gone back to bed. I guess I'd better get on at least one of those original tasks!

### endless — 2013-01-06T18:19:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-69647>

I have always liked them...never considered having one until my ex gave me one as a gift for quitting my job..lol
He's about 4 feet long but not very big around. Not big enough to strangle me

So TOM after 1 month of BHRT hasn't been that impressive. 25 day cycle, I had a face break out and some major irritability around week 3, and now (day 3 of cycle) heavy and crampy. I'm hoping for better results next month. I'm usually pretty regular between 27-30 days, haven't had one this short for about a year. I'm not exactly sure what to expect though so I'll just document and wait till next month. My doc is going out of town for awhile because her brother is dying of cancer...she will be away about 3 weeks. I moved my follow up consult with Dr K into Feb so I will have time to get new labs done on day 21 and have the results back. I re-listened to the recording of my consult and I think he was mistaken about my Vit D level...he said it was great, but I'll bet he was thinking it was in US. It was 84 nmol/L, which is only 33.6 ng/ml. It's gone up to 122 nmol/L (49 ng/ml) but it's still not optimal from what I understand. I'm getting it drawn again in 3 weeks so hoping for another rise!

I had a massage this morning. My massage guy is really good. It's not exactly enjoyable, because I have some pretty bad chronic areas, but it's effective. This time he worked on my ilio-psoas muscles a bit...yeowtch!! I sit a lot, driving for work, and it has made them very tight. It's a vulnerable place to have worked on, because it's accessed from the front, right at the groin. I just have an instinct to protect that area, for some reason. It's like the back of the knees.

I'm going to be doggy-sitting my brother's two mutts for a few days while they are away...my dog will be thrilled to have the company!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-06T19:32:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-69658>

Loved the pic of the kitties...too funny! And the pic of the canadian winter...yup, that's what it was like here today. I drove my DS to the ski hill and I could barely feel my ears after a few minutes of being outside...NO, I did not ski! Back home I went. Whimp!

### endless — 2013-01-08T12:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-69866>

I am feeling so sluggish lately....I hate it. I have been eating pretty clean (had a stevia soda last night), using my uvex, eating BAB on time...I guess as usual the weak link is CT. I'll see if I can make it happen this morning before I get a work call, but I really do have to walk the mutts first. I have my brother's two here as well and if I don't get them out before I leave my house will be a disaster when I get home!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T16:02:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-69904>

Krista I hear you on tough it is to get the CT done. For me, a firm mental grip and refusing to even think about it is the only way I get it done. I also only do it every other day preferring to take a cold shower on off days. Since that's how often I wash my hair, lol, it works pretty good!

On CT days I check the clock as I finish up my BAB and tell myself OK run the tub at X:XX which is 30 minutes AFTER I finish eating. That way I know I'm loaded up on good fat and ready to go. At the 30 minute mark I make myself stop whatever I'm doing and go turn on the cold water. I don't think about how cold it is or what else I need to be doing that day. My focus is simple ... fill the tub, grab the iPad and turn on HBOGO, ditch the robe and GTFI the tub. The minute I hesitate ... then I'm lost. So I don't. Focus. Eat, fill the tub, get IN.

HTH's! Cause it took me a loooooong time to get to this point and it would be so easy to just stop doing it because it's uncomfortable.

### endless — 2013-01-08T16:44:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-69922>

I think if I could always do it in the morning I'd be fine, but I have this pesky job.....

Days that I'm not rushing out the door, I do something similar. I got lucky today, and filled the tub once I got back from walking the mutts because I had nothing waiting for my attention that couldn't wait. Watched an episode of Californication in there, about 30 minutes. Unfortunately, most days I barely have time for BAB, let alone CT. And if I am at work ALL day, and get home after 5, forget it. I can't get in the tub that late. I do need to get better about using the CFB on those evenings though. I wish I had every day free till noon, I think I'd be able to do the tub every day easily if I had that time available!

I'm really hoping I don't get called out today...my rear snow tires are pretty well shot. I didn't notice because the roads have been pretty bare lately, and also because I just had the damn things put on in October. They should not be wearing this fast, so obviously something is wrong. Maybe my alignment is out. I am taking it in tomorrow morning early, and the less I have to drive until then, the better!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T21:27:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-69989>

Well bummer on the tires. and they are so blasted expensive these days.

On the CT ... even if you have to do it at night, same rules apply. Eat a fat laden dinner, give yourself 30 minutes then fill the tub. Don't think. consider it an exercise in meditation to keep all those negative thoughts at bay. Aren't I just a help??

### endless — 2013-01-08T21:46:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-69998>

I will get to the night time CT sometime, but for now I have about a 3pm cutoff. We actually talked about this in my consult and Jack actually suggested a WARM bath at night (which I have actually not done yet, but he agrees that CT should be earlier in the day for me, for now.)

I can see myself starting to CT later when the light cycle gets a bit longer and it's a bit warmer. If I ease into it, by next winter I'll be totally ok with it. In the meantime I will still be using my CFB for later CT sessions.

### endless — 2013-01-11T00:15:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-70260>

I am finding this week pretty trying.....so far I've managed fairly well to maintain a somewhat positive attitude but it is not effortless.

Having car trouble...my rear winter tires are basically shot while my front ones are still dandy, which makes no sense since they are exactly the same age. I'm not happy with the dealer's take on the situation so have been searching forums and going to tire shops and mechanic shops for different perspectives. I'm getting more info but either way I'm looking at $$$.

Also, after 14 years free of corrective lenses (since my Lasik in 1998) I need glasses again. My eyes are not nicely deteriorating together, they are headed in totally opposite directions, one farsighted and one near. This has been great so far, as they are able to compensate, but now the difference is large enough that it is causing me eyestrain and I really need glasses for night driving. I better hit the face dunks every day! Have no idea what glasses cost these days.

I went for a walk with my SIL today, and then as I was leaving their house I backed up into a snowplow blade that my brother had left in a STUPID spot in his driveway....shattered my right corner bumper cover in the back. Stupid plastic car.....I'm used to driving something made of steel. Anyway, it'll be a duct tape repair. I'll be damned if I'm paying $1200 to replace it when it has no practical purpose.

So hey, it's been an expensive week!! I had been toying with the idea of springing for the Q&A tonight, but not a chance. I only hope that someone who has access will graciously share their info afterwards.

On a happier note, I managed 30 mins in the tub today, and am still doing well managing every second day lately. Tomorrow I'm up and busy early so most likely won't be in the tub. Sunday should be manageable!

### endless — 2013-01-11T11:39:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-70295>

I guess I will break out the CFB this morning...no tub time in the forseeable future. I have a 3 hour 'web training' to sit through and I don't think I can do it from the bath

I'll take an underdressed walk first with the dag, at least.

Just ate 1/2lb of raw elk for breakfast. I added some avocado because it's so lean....I am becoming very partial to raw! I have a fair amount of ground elk and beef in the freezer that I will have to use cooked at some point, unless I can trade it for fish or something. I'll hit up a friend or two who fish a lot and see if they want some game...hmmmm. Not sure about eating freshwater fish raw. I ate what I think was Kokanee (landlocked lake salmon) raw last week, but trout? I'll have to experiment.

I think those darn coyotes are lurking around again. I'll have to go 'mark' my territory soon.

I'm not on my 8th day of TOM

It's been stop and start for the last 2 days. I'm really hoping this is a temporary adjustment period (no pun intended...)

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-11T17:45:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-70339>

I envy you the elk...makes me remember all the moose we were given in Newfoundland...too bad I cooked it all back then! We can get venison here in Vermont, but I have to pay a lot. Haven't found a friendly hunter yet..(I was given one liver this fall though)

Endless...keep getting in the tub as much as you can....I'm really starting to feel what the benefits of CT have to offer ....it's worth all the initial futzing around in the beginning....(end of pep talk)

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-11T18:07:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-70343>

### endless — 2013-01-12T13:18:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-70402>

Hey, pep talks always welcome here! You may envy my elk meat, but I envy your CT hole!

Paula, it's my Cool Fat Burner vest thingy that I bought....I thought I could use it later in the day and for times that tubbing isn't practical. I'm still not doing it as much as I'd hoped, but working on it!

No CT at all yesterday...by the time I finished my 3 hour webinar training I was so late for the rest of my work calls that I had to rush out the door. Didn't get home till late, and was ravenous, so I caved on the way home and bought a pre cooked Safeway chicken for dinner. I ate half of it just standing at the counter, didn't even get a plate! I enjoyed it, but I do see my poultry days being fewer and farther between.

Then headed out to my brother's place, where they (foolishly!) allowed my niece to have a party for her 15th birthday. There was a small amount of alcohol allowed under supervision, with all parents aware and all kids getting picked up by their parents by 1am. Unfortunately there were a handful of boys who smuggled in a bottle and polished it off on their own, so the three adults ended up dealing with 4 extremely sick young men until their folks arrived and hauled them away. They are going to be some seriously hurting units this morning! Hopefully it will dissuade them from such stupid behavior in the future. I was pleasantly surprised by the rest of the kids though...they were for the most part very responsible and respectful, and helpful when the puking started.

There seems to be an unspoken consensus among the parents around here, that the kids can have supervised parties, indulge in a beer or two, and not get in trouble. The parents of the sick boys (why is it always the boys????) weren't too happy with their kids, but they were happy that they were in a safe place when it happened, rather than out at some bush party where anythnig could happen. I think I'll pass on being a chaperone for the next one, though...either that or make sure somebody searches the boys and their stuff on the way in!

Sunny this morning...I'm tempted to try Darleen's buck nekkid basking, but it's the weekend and my neighbors are working on their barn. I can't do that to them...lol

### endless — 2013-01-13T11:58:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-70502>

I just listened to the January webinar and now I'm all fired up again after a couple days of feeling lackluster about this whole thing....I think having the monthly webinar to look forward to is perfect, for me anyway. If I start to lose motivation I get a 'hit' to get me going again. My name is Krista and I am a Krusaholic.

I am definitely going to supplement with everything suggested aggressively for a bit and see what I notice. I woke up this morning with really sore calf muscles...almost too sore to stretch. All I did was walk up and down a long flight of steps on Friday night about 30 times (while helping with the teenage debacle at my brother's place). Then the first thing I hear this morning is how Acetyl L Carnitine clears lactic acid and excessive muscle pain with exercise indicates low levels! Good enough for me.....guess I have some shopping to do.

The link to download the MP3 isn't working...as soon as it is I will download that to Dropbox so I can listen in my car on my Iphone.

### ashryn — 2013-01-16T09:29:11+0200

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-16T09:30:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-71020>

I finally tried the MHS yesterday. I was always scared of breaking the blender or something. So I tried it...This morning I cooked something else...and when it was finally ready I just couldn't help but have an MHS instead. I put the cooked stuff in a container for another time if I feel for it...lol.

For now I am going to stick with my Mag oil, epsom salts for magneisum sources (and seaweed and chocolate has mg too). I know that there is threonate etc. but for other supplements I am sticking to Vitex and ox bile and vit D3. It's very expensive and I'm on a very tight budget. I think the MHS might be better for my budget in the end (wow score)! I will not give up some of my supplements just yet even if I feel very good eating the MHS. So far it is literally the only thing that is not causing me bloat afterwards. It just goes down so smooth and easy---I don't have to try. I hope this effect continues and that I'm not 'just' feeling good because I'm past my TOTM...\*cross fingers\* My bbt is higher than previous months even after TOTM. For example last few days it's been 36.48 or 36.5 or today it was 36.58.

I am using a sleep mask now (as of last night). I think this will end up being a better choice.

I do face dunks in ice water in the morning and at evening (sunset) and/or night. I take showers before bed now. (The shower I started last night). When I go outside in the morning I decide I simply cannot sleep in until the sunrise (close to 8). I always get up ~6 and I can't help it. Instead I am going outside in bear feet in my back yard and looking up at the sky and lying on the ground/snow. I hope this gives enough earthing.

I just want my sleep to be solid and sound and then I know I'm in good health!

At least my bbt's are higher these days (before they were either 36.2 for months OR in December it was 35.9!) I don't know if it's the Vitex helping or what, but I will continue the Vitex for a few months at least to see how things go before I dare cut it out. I took (a tincture form) of Vitex in July after 3 months of no cycle- got it then I stopped Vitex + seafood...thought I was good to go. 6 months later I didn't have my cycle. December was a dreadful month long of PMS . Started Vitex at end of December got TOTM on Jan. 8. It was not the most comfortable TOTM but it was a day shorter just like in July with 6 days instead of 7 and there was much less clotting on day 2. It was like half of what it was in previous times before May 2012.

Also what's good about the MHS is that it's fast and easy...lol. I'm tired or preparing and cooking food right now. hehe.

p.s. I'm drinking more green tea and forgot to mention that I do take green tea extract too as one of my supplements.

I have not had coffee for the month of January. I want to add it back in because of all the beautiful single origin coffees that exist. It's like a luxury that no cake or pie can ever come close to. I believe also that when I was home for Christmas that I was drinking beautiful Cuban coffee but from a coffee maker that my parents have---I would think that is very mould laden. I don't know if I had trouble with my own coffee using french press or metal filter/pour over. I will test again soon enough it's just that I'm scared now. hehe I want my sleep to be good.

### endless — 2013-01-19T16:52:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-71647>

Yeah, the 'Big Bruise Theory'...lol (from me falling on my face tripping over the earthing cord.)

So I'm confused....been lurking over on MDA on the 'super secret potato project' thread. They are eating potatos, sweet and white, to the exclusion of everything else (most of them, anyway...) Results are everyone is losing weight, having better FBG readings, and some are in ketosis. What is that about? I wonder what that does to your hormone panel?

This is an excerpt from that thread....it seems so crazy when you compare it to what we're all trying to do!

> From watching everybody of this, and the other threads, I would like to propose the following guidelines for a "Potato Reset"
>
> 1\. Plan on eating mostly potatoes for 5-7 days (90%+ of calories)
> 2\. Eat between 2 and 4lbs of potatoes daily.
> 3\. Other foods allowed (but optional!): 1/2 cup sauerkraut, 1 cup bone broth, 1 tsp max daily coconut oil, olive oil, or butter,
> 1-2oz lean meat daily. Unlimited coffee, tea, and water.
> 4\. Spices, salt, pepper, and vinegar OK.
> 5\. Lifting of Heavy Things discouraged, think 'de-load week'
> 6\. All normal supplements OK to continue
> 7\. IF'ing, especially by skipping breakfast encouraged.
> 8\. If you need to lose more weight, alternate the "Potato Reset" diet with healthy Primal Blueprint in 5-7 day increments.
> 9\. Not recommended for people who eat every 2-3 hours, have glucose issues, or have eating disorders.
> 10\. Highly recommended for people who have their hunger under control, have been eating PB style for 6 months or more, and are having trouble losing weight with the normal approaches.
>
> This is given as a reason it works:
>
>
> > Hyperlipidrotons: Zero fat
> >
> > The theory why people are calling this a "diet hack" isn't because of calories. That would just be normal dieting. The reason why potatoes are the chosen food is because they're basically zero fat and come with high quality protein, something very rare in a vegetarian protein source, and a lot of nutrients. The theory is because your body needs fat to manufacture insulin, if you are eating ZERO fat, it has to pull fat out of storage to manufacture insulin.Because white potatoes are so highly insulogenic and create such a massive, high GI response, it needs A LOT of insulin - which requires a significant portion of fat. Since you aren't eating fat along withit, it is forced to go to adipose tissue as a source - and needs quite a bit - to make all that insulin.
> >
> > So when you start combining potatoes with outside sources of protein and fiber - like egg whites, fish or vegetables - you are destroying the"hack." You are greatly reducing the insulin spike you're supposed to get, which lessens the immediate fat need from your fat cells to manufacture insulin. The whole point is because potatoes are so massively insulinogenic. If you add stuff, it doesn't work. It has to be all potatoes.
> >
> > You could surely do it with white rice too, but it's not recommended because there's virtually no protein (certainly no high quality protein) and far less nutrients. Potatoes were chosen because of the very high quality protein, the nutrient density, the high glycemix index and the fact that someone can survive a very long time eating simply potatoes - it is nearly a complete food - you will become ill much faster eating just white rice than eating just white potatoes.
>
>
> But it would destroy any progress one had made towards becoming a fat burner, wouldn't it?
>
> Even more:
>
> The real magic of potatoes may be in it's resistant starch.
> \- Scientists are racing to figure out a way to isolate the resistant starch in potatoes so they can make big $$ adding it to other foods.
> \- The main benefits of the resistant starch is satiety (no duh), and blood sugar control.
> \- To maximize the resistant starch, you need to cook and COOL the potatoes. This starts a process called retrogradation that turns regular starch into resistant starch. The retrograded starch is treated and digested as short-chain fatty acids (fat) in the large intestine--how bizarre is that?
> \- Vinegar also aids in the conversion.

I'm going to look up this resistant starch thing, just out of curiosity. I have to admit that potatos are my most tempting cheat food. Maybe I can find something to justify eating them a bit in season....

> This is an excerpt from that thread....it seems so crazy when you compare it to what we're all trying to do!
>
> > From watching everybody of this, and the other threads, I would like to propose the following guidelines for a "Potato Reset"
> > 1\. Plan on eating mostly potatoes for 5-7 days (90%+ of calories)
> > 2\. Eat between 2 and 4lbs of potatoes daily.
> > 3\. Other foods allowed (but optional!): 1/2 cup sauerkraut, 1 cup bone broth, 1 tsp max daily coconut oil, olive oil, or butter,
> > 1-2oz lean meat daily. Unlimited coffee, tea, and water.
> > 4\. Spices, salt, pepper, and vinegar OK.
> > 5\. Lifting of Heavy Things discouraged, think 'de-load week'
> > 6\. All normal supplements OK to continue
> > 7\. IF'ing, especially by skipping breakfast encouraged.
> > 8\. If you need to lose more weight, alternate the "Potato Reset" diet with healthy Primal Blueprint in 5-7 day increments.
> > 9\. Not recommended for people who eat every 2-3 hours, have glucose issues, or have eating disorders.
> > 10\. Highly recommended for people who have their hunger under control, have been eating PB style for 6 months or more, and are having trouble losing weight with the normal approaches.
>
> This is given as a reason it works:
> Hyperlipidrotons: Zero fat
> The theory why people are calling this a "diet hack" isn't because of calories. That would just be normal dieting. The reason why potatoes are the chosen food is because they're basically zero fat and come with high quality protein, something very rare in a vegetarian protein source, and a lot of nutrients. The theory is because your body needs fat to manufacture insulin, if you are eating ZERO fat, it has to pull fat out of storage to manufacture insulin.Because white potatoes are so highly insulogenic and create such a massive, high GI response, it needs A LOT of insulin - which requires a significant portion of fat. Since you aren't eating fat along withit, it is forced to go to adipose tissue as a source - and needs quite a bit - to make all that insulin.
> So when you start combining potatoes with outside sources of protein and fiber - like egg whites, fish or vegetables - you are destroying the"hack." You are greatly reducing the insulin spike you're supposed to get, which lessens the immediate fat need from your fat cells to manufacture insulin. The whole point is because potatoes are so massively insulinogenic. If you add stuff, it doesn't work. It has to be all potatoes.
> You could surely do it with white rice too, but it's not recommended because there's virtually no protein (certainly no high quality protein) and far less nutrients. Potatoes were chosen because of the very high quality protein, the nutrient density, the high glycemix index and the fact that someone can survive a very long time eating simply potatoes - it is nearly a complete food - you will become ill much faster eating just white rice than eating just white potatoes.

But it would destroy any progress one had made towards becoming a fat burner, wouldn't it?
Even more:
The real magic of potatoes may be in it's resistant starch.
\- Scientists are racing to figure out a way to isolate the resistant starch in potatoes so they can make big $$ adding it to other foods.
\- The main benefits of the resistant starch is satiety (no duh), and blood sugar control.
\- To maximize the resistant starch, you need to cook and COOL the potatoes. This starts a process called retrogradation that turns regular starch into resistant starch. The retrograded starch is treated and digested as short-chain fatty acids (fat) in the large intestine--how bizarre is that?
\- Vinegar also aids in the conversion.
I'm going to look up this resistant starch thing, just out of curiosity. I have to admit that potatos are my most tempting cheat food. Maybe I can find something to justify eating them a bit in season....

I have said many times........it ain't about the food.......it is about the timing of the carbs, Soon you will understand that nuance and why this potato thing can work for small bit of time.

Day 2 of 38 degree CT....I enjoy my 30 minutes in the tub (ok maybe not the first 5 minutes so much) but afterwards SUCKS! I always wondered why I didn't shiver after CT...apparently I don't shiver at the temps I was doing before. I am surely shivering now! For some reason having my skin that cold in the water doesn't bother me, but when I get out....I've never actually experienced involuntary shivering of my quads before this.

I'm keeping my feet in for the first 2-3 minutes but that's all they can take...maybe if I put some wool socks on.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-20T14:32:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-71860>

Socks do help. It's great you have a deep enough tub to fully submerge. Mine isn't nearly as big as I thought. If I fill to almost overflowing I can get MOST of me under water but I have to keep my legs out and scootch down which puts my neck at an odd angle. 45 minutes isn't tough once I get past that first 2 minutes of ohmygodIcannotbreathethisissodamnedcold! Then I'm ok.

I'm going to try your trick of covering the overflow drain with a baggy and duct tape today. That cover I bought leaks so after 45 minutes I've lost a good 2-4" of water.

Great job, BTW!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-71937>

Darleen, actually I find the warm up totally different with this temp. First I am seriously uncomfortable, shivering and moving around a lot (like Diane, running around the house!) but I find that goes away more quickly than it did at higher temps. Or maybe my body is just starting to kick in some heat...never thought of that actually! But I stay marginally colder for hours afterwards....like goosebumps on my arms. Even if I take a warm shower after (which I did after an hour of warm up today...had to go to work) I still feel colder. Odd.

Oh, and my tub is not deep at all, just a standard old steel 5 foot tub. I sit up, legs extended, for a few minutes, then prop my heels on the edge and lie down. Luckily I am only 5'1" so I don't have to bend my legs, my head catches on the back rim of the tub and my heels on the front edge! If I fill it up REALLY full I can get everything under except the boobs. No more belly island, I can get the whole squishy area under now!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-20T22:30:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-71956>

5'1"! You shrimp, you! LOL. I need at least a 6' tub and deeper than what I have. There's a LOT of me to fold under the water. Could be worse. My "baby" sister is 6' tall, our brother 6'5". I was the runt of the litter. When all 3 of us were together it was "Land of the Giants."

### endless — 2013-01-20T23:59:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-71963>

Mmmm, shrimp

Yep, I come from a long line of hobbits. Not really...although I do have hairy toes if I neglect to wax them! I figure that is a benefit now....as long as I am grounded I am well within the 'safe zone' since I am so close to the ground!

I've decided to go with a grounding rod which I will place outside the unused door that leads into my bedroom. The cord will be long enough to take into the bathroom with me if I want to ground while in the tub...at least until I can get into the river again. Fairly reasonably priced and simple. The magnetic thing...we'll see. I can't affore to spend $1600 on a mattress pad at this point. Not if I want to continue to pay for membersip here and buy consults and buy supplements and pay my rent.

### caroline — 2013-01-21T03:51:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-71973>

Maybe we could all rob a bank together?? At least we have our priorities straight!

### endless — 2013-01-21T14:16:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72073>

Good idea, Caroline! Might have to....

So I skimmed the EMF 2 preview. I need to spend time on it, which I do not have at the moment....I appreciate hearing about HOW Dr K came to his realizations and WHY these things affect us, but honestly, I am all about the WHAT can I do to ameliorate it. EMFs are here to stay, so how can I minimize the risk to me, my loved ones and my environment. There are some simple and obvious answers, and some not so simple. I spent a lot of last night figuring out a few of mine.

I'm going to dig a patch of snow and path to it, so I will have a bare patch to use for grounding outside. I will probably do this in wool socks or felt boot liners until the worst cold is past. I can put a chair out there and read in the sun with my feet on the ground.

I'm going to install a ground rod outside the bedroom that I can attach to a wrist strap for sleeping or CT. I'd rather do that than plug into an outlet.

I discovered the breaker that turns off power to my bedroom, and last night I turned it off before I went to bed. No power running behind my head in the wall while it is off!

I unplugged my wireless router before bed. I keep my wireless printer unplugged all the time unless I'm using it.

There are no transformers, substations or celll towers near me. I will rent a meter to determine if the high transmission lines on the other side of the field are far enough from my house away. If not I will figure that out then....

There's more, but the biggest thing is I AM NOT GOING TO STRESS ABOUT IT. I already use protective devices on my cell phone, portable phone, and around my computer....I get a lot of comments from people that doubt they work, since there is no 'proof'. Well, the way I look at it is if they work, great! If they don't, I'm sure they are not harming me and I am supporting a small business

I AM going to look into further protective solutions for my car. I have it narrowed down to a few choices and hopefully that will help with all the time I spend there.

### endless — 2013-01-21T14:20:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72074>

Day 3 of 38 degrees. I'm thinking that this is probably the coldest my tap water will get.....unless we get a super cold snap. I'm trying to appreciate it! Damn, the river is going to be warm like a bathtub for me this summer!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-21T16:05:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72098>

LOL, that's what I keep thinking. I finally decided to put the thermometer away. It's just COLD that's all I need to know.

### diane — 2013-01-21T18:38:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72141>

Krista - yes, I definitely think there's a difference between getting stressed about something and trying to do better. For me, all these feelings and quest for change has been leading to something - and I think that all of this is just validating what I've been thinking and feeling. But I don't get stressed about getting in the tub or not, or eating the MHS or not. I do it because I like how it feels - and if it feels like it's working - who cares what anyone thinks.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-21T22:39:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72234>

Diane .. YES!!! It isn't hard to do when I feel like I'm doing so much better than I was even a couple of months ago. I feel the progression even if I've bucked Jack and his ideas most of the way. Yeah, I'm a wah wah baby pants most of the time, LOL. But here I am, taking baby steps then giant steps.

MHS? they're comin'
slurpin' oysters ... on the horizon
nude sunbathing in JANUARY? check
More CT ... yowza!
earthing ... you bet

I may drag my feet a bit but everyone here helps pull me forward. And the end result is I just moving forward. No point in stressing. Just -- one foot in front of the other.

### caroline — 2013-01-22T00:58:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72241>

You have made so much progress Diane .... I am so proud of you.

### endless — 2013-01-22T01:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72244>

I am wiped....I wish I had the fortitude to go outside in the GD freezing cold and earth on the ice, but I just don't. My legs ache, my hips ache, my back aches....I worked hard today and I can tell. I did manage my CT this morning so I feel pretty good about that. I am taking a day off CT tomorrow.....have an acu appt and I am going to have a WARM magnesium/peroxide bath tonight in preparation. CT will resume Wednesday.

Oh, and I worked in a McDonalds restaurant all day.....seriously expected to be tempted by deep fry aromas....didn't even notice it. I did not smell anything appetizing the entire 6.5 hours I was there. That's new...even though I have not eaten McDs for years I usually still jones for the fries if I smell them. Not this time.

Not only that, not hungry at ALL for dinner. Breakfast was tuna fried with onion, celery and bacon and an avocado. Lunch was 3 'power sticks' (gluten free pepperoni like things...) and a tiny bag of salt and vinegar potato chips. OH.....duh....that's why my legs and hips ache so much! Yes, I'm a f'in dummy. I guess I'll never be trying that potato diet. Potatos, although I love them , immediately cause me pain. Unfortunately I am an idiot who seems to constantly forget this.

One of my cats peed in my clean laundry basket. Perfect end to a Monday. I'm going to have a glass of wine now

### caroline — 2013-01-22T01:30:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72248>

I think red wine is the best remedy for just about anything - maybe hug a tree while you are sipping

### caroline — 2013-01-22T01:32:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72249>

### diane — 2013-01-22T09:12:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72271>

### endless — 2013-01-22T22:16:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72418>

I thought wood was not a good conductor...maybe living wood is different!

I took a day off from cold today. Had an amazing and much needed acupuncture appointment and ran some errands in town, including picking up the TriField meter. So needless to say I've been measuring everything in the house! I was amazed how strong the field is coming off the electric baseboards! First change made....I have been using only the baseboard in the kitchen which is right below the table where I am when I use the computer etc..(it's more like my desk, really) I am literally less than a foot from this baseboard for a couple hours a day. So, that baseboard is OFF and the living room one is on, which is on the opposite side of the room from where I sit.

I will have to wait a few days till I have time to put the snowshoes on and head outside for some readings there, but so far the only problem area in the house is the stub wall between the kitchen and living room that has the electrical panel on it. The background reading in the house appears to be about 0.2 in the bedroom to 0.3 in the kitchen. The readings when I turn ON the dryer or the hairdryer are off the charts.

It's amazing how many YouTube videos there are on EMF pollution and solutions. It's been a big deal for years....funny how so many people are just learning about it! I borrowed this same meter from my ND (then my boss) 15 years ago to test my house then..that's when I stopped using a digital clock within 5 feet of the bed!

My cats are annoyed at the change in baseboards. Maybe now they will stop sprawling over my computer to bask in the heat!

Thanks for sharing your experiences. I'm glad to see your kitty wasn't emitting EMF!

If my memory serves me from waaaaay back when I took a course in magnetic fields, the strength of a field decreases in proportion to the distance squared. So if you are 2 feet away, the field is 1/4 (25%) as strong. At 10 feet, it is 1/100 or 1%. As long as you don't spend time near your electrical panel, I would think that isn't a big worry. The baseboard heaters on the other hand...

### endless — 2013-01-26T19:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72745>

I had a somewhat productive morning...I plugged something into every outlet in my house and re-labeled the main panel. This place has been renovated and changed since the original electrical was put in so they were not properly labeled. Now I know which breakers I can safely flip off at night without affecting the fridge or freezer, or turning off my snake's heating pad. Everything else can go off at night, except the one baseboard.

I've been having a new symptom of...something, I have no idea really. I wake up to pee in the early morning, anywhere from 3-5am. Then I go back to bed and more often than not, in about 10 minutes I start feeling the need to have a bowel movement, more so if it's 4am or later. I've never had to go that early before....I hope it's not my cortisol peaking too early.

### endless — 2013-01-27T19:02:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72889>

Yesterday I had a repeat of the odd 'heart' (or chest, anyway...) discomfort I had been experiencing a few months ago. It feels like something heavy is pressing on my chest, from about my sternum to my lower throat. Very disconcerting. I have been having terrible upper back and neck problems though, and I know that several of my ribs on the left side are out of place so I have no idea where these sensations are actually coming from. I wake up with numb little fingers and ring fingers from the nerves in my neck being irritated....
The thing yesterday bothered me because I felt it strongly right after getting ot of the tub. I was in for 30 minutes at 38-40F, and out for about 5 when I started to feel odd....heavy chest and breathing, and my lower back felt very wierd too. I got into a warm shower, just in case it was from too .long in the cold. I didn't take my temp, but my skin was red, not pale...maybe I should shorten my CT sessions just in case.

Last night I slept well, other than the numbness in my arms/fingers. I fell asleep on the couch watching a movie (with Uvex on) but was woken at about 10:30pm to attend a drunk and hypothermic person for about an hour and a half. I was exposed to some bright light and adrenalin before coming home again. I turned off all the breakers except the critical ones, and went to bed. I used my red headlamp to read for 15 minutes or so and managed to fall asleep fairly quickly. I was afraid I'd be up all night from the full moon!

Off to a massage appoinment now...I hope I get some relief from this back pain!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-27T19:10:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72890>

Krista ... I have problems occasionally with numb fingers on waking when I've been lying on my back. I have life long issues with my neck and the only way I've found to deal with them is to head to the chiropractor. It's been off-and-on again lately and I'm finding that, for me, it's major tension in my lower back that's causing the issue.The chest thing is a little worrisome. Is it muscular or deep? Is your digestion off? When I had severe heartburn I actually thought I was having a heart attack. I finally figured it out ... I'm a little slow sometimes.

### caroline — 2013-01-27T23:18:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72914>

Gosh Krista - that heart thing is very worrisome. You are on your own I think? All these really low temps that you guys are doing scare me. Didn't Dr. K say to be really careful of going too low and to have a spotter? I have to go back and re read C/T...

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-27T23:29:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72918>

Caroline, too low is like frigid super duper CT. We aren't doing temps or times anywhere near that. The big fear is cooling your core too low. Since Krista only does about 30 minutes at 40+ temps she should be OK.

### endless — 2013-01-27T23:51:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-72922>

Darleen, I see the chiro every two weeks without fail...this is something that's come up fairly recently and I don't know why....he has had a hard time adjusting me lately because my upper back is so tight.

> The chest thing is a little worrisome. Is it muscular or deep? Is your digestion off? When I had severe heartburn I actually thought I was having a heart attack. I finally figured it out ... I'm a little slow sometimes.

I can't really tell,that's what is so frustrating about it! When it was happening before I had an ECG and some other test (like an ultrasound?) and both were normal. My digestion is fine...My dad has AFib and has episodes of tachycardia that require shocking his heart back into proper rhythm, so I've been somewhat freaked out about that happening to me as well. I've had palpitations since I was in my early 20s, so I'm used to that, but this pressure thing is different. My hunch is it is caused by pressure on the nerves in my neck and upper back affecting the organs in those areas, but who knows?

Caroline, I am barely below 40 degrees, and I doubt that is the cause, but I am going to keep an eye on it, for sure.

Had a GREAT massage today, he really worked me over and my neck is better now. I know my traps and pecs will be sore to the touch tomorrow but that will remind me to try and keep them relaxed. Apparently all the areas I am having tension are along the Gall Bladder meridian according to Chinese medicine...also related to agitation, impatience and frustration. Hmmm....

### endless — 2013-01-31T17:38:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-73460>

I agree Caroline...but I feel hopeful this morning I had a warm bath with a shit ton of epsom salts in it, but didn't stay in long because my neck was just not comfortable. I got out and went to bed, reading a bit of the grounding book by my red headlamp light but even that was hurting me (holding the book) so I gave up and turned out the light. I put the grounding strap on my wrist.
I am totally convinced this morning...slept all night, not waking up in pain with numb fingers and sciatic pain like I have been every night. I am still stiff and can feel where my spine or muscles are not 'right' but this is a huge improvement! I seem to have lost a lot of inflammation overnight. I'm not taking this thing off till I have to leave the house! Luckily I have a lot of cord I can trail around behind me....

I watched a YouTube video last night first and then proceeded to butcher my beef heart. It requires a very sharp knife to do properly, if you're skinning the whole thing. Got it done and now I am going to look up some recipes....

Thanks Lyndra!

I seem to have conveniently neglected the CT for the last few days, other than face dunks. Back in there this morning, as soon as the dryer is done. I don't go in the bathroom anymore when it's running after measuring the emissions!

Yesterday the Oyster Man was in town so I bought 2 dozen oysters and 4 pounds of mussels. I ate half of that for dinner last night...I think I will split it up today, the oysters for lunch and the mussels for dinner. Breakfast today was some marinated heart skewers, broiled, and some sauteed mushrooms. I ate the heart raw the last two days but the less tender pieces I cut into strips and marinated in some vinegar and garlic and olive oil, and it was nice and tender when broiled!

The grounding strap is working ok, but it is a PITA to drag the cord around the house. The snap comes undone when the cord fetches up against something. I might consider keeping the straps for periods when I am stationary, at the computer or watching a movie or something. ### endless — 2013-02-03T02:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-73881>

Putting these here for safekeeping....

> VDR/Taq and VDR/Fok (vitamin D receptor): The panel looks at more than one portion of the vitamin D receptor, the Taq as well as the Fok sites. While the Fok change has been related to blood sugar regulation, changes at Taq can affect dopamine levels. For this reason it is important to look at the composite of the COMT and VDR/Taq status and make supplement suggestions based on the combined results at these two sites. The focus on changes in the Fok portion of the VDR is in regard to supplements that support the pancreas and aid in keeping blood sugar in the normal healthy range.

> MAO A R297R (monamine oxidase A): MaoA is involved in the breakdown of serotonin in the body. Like dopamine, serotonin is another neurotransmitter in the body. It is involved with mood, and imbalances in serotonin levels have been associated with depression, aggression, anxiety and OCD behavior. Since Mao A is inherited with the X chromosome and is considered a dependent trait it may not show standard inheritance characteristics in males. Since the X chromosome in males can only come from the mother, this means that the fathers Mao A mutations (or lack there of) does not play a role in their son's Mao A status. For females, since one X chromosome is inherited from each parent, the genetics tend to reflect the Mao A status of both parents.

> MTHFR A1298C, C677T, 3 (methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase): The MTHFR gene product is at a critical point in the methylation cycle. It helps to pull homocysteine into the cycle, serving to aid in keeping the levels in a normal healthy range. Several mutations in the MTHFR gene have been well characterized as increasing the risk of heart disease, as well as cancer, and may play a role in the level of the neurotransmitters serotonin and dopamine.

> MTR A2756G/MTRR A66G, H595Y, K350A, R415T, S257T, 11 (methionine synthase/ methionine synthase reductase): These two gene products work together to regenerate and utilize B12 for the critical long way around the methylation pathway, helping to convert homocysteine to methionine. High levels of homocysteine have been implicated as risk factors in a number of health conditions including heart disease as well as Alzheimer's disease. As is the case for COMT and VDR Bsm/Taq, the MTR and MTRR composite status is also important. Mutations in MTR can increase the activity of this gene product so that it leads to a greater need for B12 as the enzyme is using up B12 at a faster rate. The MTRR helps to recycle B12 for use by the MTR. Mutations that affect its activity would also suggest a greater need for B12.

> BHMT 1,2,4,8 (betaine homocysteine methyltransferase): The product of this gene is central to the 'short cut' through the methylation cycle, again helping to convert homocysteine to methionine. The activity of this gene product can be affected by stress, by cortisol levels and may play a role in ADD/ADHD by affecting norepinephrine levels.

> CBS C699T, A360A (cystathionine-beta-synthase): The CBS enzyme basically acts as a gate between homocysteine and the downstream portion of the pathway that generates ammonia in the body. The types of CBS mutations that are identified on this SNP panel cause this "CBS gateÃ¢€Â￾ to be left open, this 'open gate' is not a neutral situation. The 'open gate'Â￾ can allow support that is added for the rest of the methylation pathway to be depleted, including any B12 that is used to address MTR and MTRR mutations. While there are some positive end products that are generated via the downstream portion of the pathway such as glutathione and taurine, there are also negative byproducts such as excess ammonia and sulfites. By virtue of increased CBS activity, these sulfur groups that were complexed as part of the methylation cycle can now be released into the system as sulfites which are toxic to the body and put an additional burden on the SUOX gene product.

Speaking of 'woo' (well, not here, in Darleen's J) I found this on FB and I wonder if these solar flares can affect us like this....it too is electromagnetic energy, isn't it?

> The Effects of Recent Solar Flares
>
> Many people are feeling agitated and un-easy recently. The solar flares can break apart old patterns of thought that are no longer needed so new ones can emerge. This can make us feel hyper and exhausted all at the same time. It can also wreak havoc with technology such as computers.
>
> We began noticing many new symptoms. Time and space appear to on a wobble even more erratically than before. We seem to be losing track of time easily. We lose words when we are speaking.The day simply disappears. We are not sleeping very well again. There is lots of tossing and turning in the night and many are having unusual dreams. We are feeling huge power surges in the body followed by energy drops. There were many reports of nausea, and body aches and pains and dizziness. Many feel deep grief. Some feel like they were walking on water (ungrounded). Others said that when they closed their eyes at night, everything is spinning. There have been visual disturbances, inner ear issues, ringing in ears, throat and thyroid issues, cold feet and even a weird symptom of having tongue dryness. Are you experiencing any of this? This isn't anything to be afraid of ... just be aware of what it is and get extra rest and drink lots of water. This too shall pass.

### endless — 2013-02-05T01:46:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-74189>

I cannot for the life of me figure out what causes me to go so wildly off track sometimes!

Yesterday I didn't get CT done, but I made a great bone broth, rendered the fat from my beef heart into tallow, managed various other productive things, and then everything went to hell! Unfortunately I had some organic corn chips in the house, which gave me the bright idea to make myself some nachos for dinner (just chips, cheese, green pepper and onion). Oh, and refried beans. I proceeded to eat a HUGE amount of this, followed by laying around on the couch watching the Walking Dead marathon until too late WITHOUT my uvex or anything. WTF?? I didn't even think of it. By the time I went to bed my stomach was distended from my bra line down to my undies. Not sure if it was the beans or the corn, but it was uncomfortable and wildly unattractive

I have GOT to do a thorough purge of all my cupboards. If it's there, I'll find it in a weak moment. I probably totally undid all the benefits of my two day oyster and mussel feast with that fiasco.

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-05T05:27:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-74236>

Endless, I feel for you. It is so easy to have what one shouldn't if its in the house. I've been known to buy something and then have to eat it in a hurry so I don't spread out the problem for days and weeks. If it has to be eaten in a hurry then the others in the house have to eat it too, so it reduces the damage I do to myself. Increases theirs but they don't think its a problem for them so I reckon its OK till they want to eat clean.

perhaps there is something about this solar energy thing (the scientist in me cringes at saying this, but I'm expanding my idea of science). I'm finding it hard to stay focussed and achieve what I need to achieve, though last night I had one good hour when I finally got to sort out my fridge. I had bought far to much meat and hadn't got it as far as the freezer and it was in danger of spoiling. But all saved.

Its odd how we can just forget things - like the uvex - when its so important to us.

### endless — 2013-02-11T18:33:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-75398>

I need to get that book too. I considered that it might just be the 'beginning of the end', but it's just too close to the start of the BHRT to be a coincidence. I went from a regular 28-31 day cycle, to 25, 23, and 14. I thought it might just be breakthrough bleeding at ovulation but this is a full on period. I have to admit to being kind of discouraged right now....I feel like I've been doing more 'right' than wrong and during my consult with Dr K he sort of thought that a bit of a hormone boost was all I needed...and now all hell has broken loose! At least it explains my cravings last week....I thought I was coming up to ovulation and instead I was about to bleed.

Between that and my neck/back thing I am feeling pretty shitty today.

One interesting thing though...yesterday after my acupuncture/massage treatment I was at home resting and in pain...my head and neck and upper back STILL aching after the treatment..and I remembered to slip on my grounding strap. The pain in my head went away, and reduced in my back. I have to remember to do that more often, not just in bed.

Krista

I still haven't broken the code on my cycle - when ever I just do BHRT -its short (23-24 days). When I do BHRT and CT its long (26-27days). I have break through bleeding, migraines, and crazy cystic acne - I'm not exactly sure what's going on... I guess I shouldn't complain b/c my TOTM is only 4 days and no PMS.

I really think I'm swinging in to menopause - courtesy of my workplace...

### endless — 2013-02-12T03:55:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-75485>

Hi Vicky, no I didn't get the magnet thing...think I will wait for the EMF Rx just to see what Dr. K has to say, and see if I can drum up any feedback from any other users first! No, the grounding stuff I bought was very inexpensive...it's from this place (well, I used the Canadian distributor but same products) [http://www.earthing.com/product\_p/sobbk.htm](http://www.earthing.com/product_p/sobbk.htm)

In addition to the body band kit I bought a grounding rod with a 40ft cord, because I didn't want to plug into an outlet. So far I am noticing it helps with pain relief. I wear it to bed, it's way cheaper than a sheet and works fine!

> I still haven't broken the code on my cycle - when ever I just do BHRT -its short (23-24 days). When I do BHRT and CT its long (26-27days). I have break through bleeding, migraines, and crazy cystic acne - I'm not exactly sure what's going on... I guess I shouldn't complain b/c my TOTM is only 4 days and no PMS.

I don't really have a major EMF exposure daily, and nothing else has changed drastically....even back when I started CT I didn't have a change in my cycle. Looking at latest labs next week, so hopefully I'll get an idea of where I'm at!

Thanks Caroline! Post some pics of the hot men on the beach, that might help

### endless — 2013-02-16T19:25:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-76126>

January was so great for CT...I managed to do it 17 days out of the month. So far in Feb I've managed TWO. Three as of today. I don't know what changed (other than my schedule) but it's been very difficult to fit in. I'm busier in the mornings for one, so if that business keeps me away all day it doesn't happen. I still can't do it later in the day. I'm hoping for some clarity when I get my new lab results...I feel like instead of helping I totally buggered up my hormones with the BHRT. I need to start over.

I'm hopeful that spring might really be near...it's wildly early to say that, but the snow is shrinking quickly and there's nothing big in the forecast....I could go for an early spring. I'm excited to get back in the river, and that won't be possible till after the spring runoff.

### endless — 2013-02-16T20:36:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-76133>

Observation....I've made it into the tub lately but not at 38 degrees. I've had to up it back to 48. My skin gets pink but not red, but I don't get that strange sensation in my chest that I was getting at the lower temp. I still don't have a skin thermometer so not sure how cold it's actually getting. I think I'll try to keep it at 45 for a while and see how that goes.

### endless — 2013-02-18T18:39:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-76328>

Friday I noticed breast tenderness, like I usually get after ovulation. Saturday I had intense pain in my left ovary....a cyst, maybe. Today, wet discharge and lesser throbbing on the same side. I'm going to assume these all point to me being mid cycle, despite my odd bleeding schedule. I am currently on cycle day 9 if I count from the last bleed, or 23 if I count from the one before that. Regardless, this is how I normally feel (pre BHRT) around CD 15-18.

In other news, I retired the cordless phones and now have two corded. next up is the wifi. I'm not sure if I have to get a new router. When I plug my computer into the one I have, it is still broadcasting the wifi signal, because my phone picks it up. Have to figure out what I need to do.

### endless — 2013-02-19T21:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-76535>

I didn't get my appointment this morning...there was a mixup. I did get my latest labs to review and will try to get in later today. The Vitamin D is not there, hopefully she can call for the result before my consult on Sunday.

Not much exciting....my DHEA has risen from 2.1 to 3.5 in 3 months....still really low!

TSH is down slightly again...2.93 in April, 1.25 in September, 2.28 in November and 2.08 now. Seasonal, maybe?

IGF-1 is 199 (range 116-298). Never tested that before so I have no idea if it's good bad or otherwise.

Sex hormones all lower than last time, despite supplementation...even T, which I have not been taking. Could be because I tested on day 21 but my cycles were going off the rails and I bled a few days later....

Cholesterol the same, but HDL is higher and LDL is lower. Triglycerides slightly higher.

I hope to get on the right track again after my consult with Dr. K! I probably won't have another for quite awhile so I have a lot to ask.

Off to work.

Aw, thanks everyone! I love being a firefighter, but I am sure not loving that dress uniform! Hoping that next time I have to wear it I will look much less like a bratwurst.

I called my ISP and they told me how to disable the radio signal on my wireless router, so I am happy. It does not require any financial outlay. Once that is done my house will be a wireless free zone (I still have a wireless printer, but it is always unplugged unless it is in use).

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-21T23:56:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-76942>

### endless — 2013-02-22T18:29:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77061>

Well, I thought I had it under control.....apparently the guy I talked to wasn't quite accurate. He said that I could plug directly from my incoming line into the computer, but that I had to authorize my computer somehow on the network. That would be fine, but I also have another work computer that needs to have access at times, and that solution only authorizes one computer.

The other solution was to remove the antennae from the router, which he said would reduce the range to only a foot or two. I could live with that...so I did. Unfortunately my router didn't have removeable antennae, so I just yanked them off. It did reduce the range, but not by nearly enough. It still reaches about 12 feet. SO, I'm thinking I need to get a new router that either does not have wireless or that can switch the wireless off. I'll look into that today.

Heading to the chiropractor again this morning...I'm really having a hard time with this neck thing. Yesterday the pain just got worse and worse until by the time I went to bed I had to take a couple of ibuprofen just so I could bear it. I didn't sleep very well but it's much better this morning (just like yesterday...) and that I attribute to being grounded all night. The inflammation seems to increase during the day. Ice didn't even seem to help last night.

### endless — 2013-02-22T18:47:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77064>

Oh, and I tried wrapping the router in tinfoil....didn't make any difference. I used my iphone to test the distance at which the signal was still received. Thought that was interesting!

### endless — 2013-02-22T22:34:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77109>

Ok, I am now officially wireless-free! I had to buy a new router but it wasn't expensive. It has wireless capability, so if I really need to I can turn it on, but otherwise it is off. I verified this with my IPhone. I might flip it on occasionally to print, until I get rid of the wireless printer.

Snowing like mad here today....I should be out rolling around in it I suppose but I'm choosing to get stuff done around the house until I get called away for work!

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-23T00:41:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77147>

We got a little sun today for a change. Man I am SO ready for Spring. Hope you get some home time and don't have to work. It's friday after all.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77246>

Wow Krista, your tatt is beautifully complex! Now I am curious to see the finished version. can you have someone take a picture?

Mine is tiny in comparison. Only about 2.5 x 4 inches...but at least it's very colorful . Colorful enough that I don't wear white blouses to work any longer because it shows through. Last time I did three people stopped me to say I had a stain on the back of my shirt.

In regards to the latest controversial thread, I have read most of it. Fear and change sometimes brings up a lot of negative feelings. I'm going to keep on my current path while exploring and learning more on the EMFs. There are changes I've already made at home, but like many, I work in an office environment.
