### endless — 2013-02-23T17:22:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77269>

> Too much ink for me though. I HATE needles. I still want something airy and light and girly. I also am going to wait until my weight is back down at least 50 pounds before I do it.

yes, wait until you are ready....a good artist will work with you to come up with a design that's perfect for you. In the meantime, if you see anything you like or that has elements that you like, save it in a folder. I gave my artist two pics and a rough idea, and he came up with this for me. And as for the pain....I actually kind of like it It's true, you get addicted to it somehow...that's why people usually keep getting them once they start!

I was trying to explain to my sister in law how I've started to crave the pleasure/pain sensation of a cold bath....just like a tattoo needle. She totally understood when I put it that way.

Fitness, I will see what I can do about a current pic! I'm curious to study it myself actually, I rarely see it. I want to see what I'm looking at for freshening up, maybe adding a few things.

I'm getting tired of this neck thing. Another day of increasing pain and immobility from about 4pm on. I'm using an enzyme anti-inflammatory, icing like crazy, grounding, even putting magnesium oil mixed with DMSO on the area and it's still bad. The chiro says it's just muscle spasms but I'm starting to wonder if there is something going on with a disc...it feels like nerve pain. I have one more acupuncture booked tomorrow and if that doesn't help immensely I will have to consider going to someone else for some investigation.

### diane — 2013-02-23T17:44:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77275>

Krista - at least it sounds like you had a clue when you went to get yours done. I told the artist I like hearts and skulls. He showed me this design he had with a skull in a flower, which I liked. When I came back to get it done, I guess I envisioned that I would see a complete drawing with colour as to how it would look. Nope, it didn't work that way. As a matter of fact, I had no clue what the bloody thing was going to look like. Talk about going on trust! HAHA! Yes, I do trust people - but my gut told me that it was ok (thank god it turned out LOL). After the first session, I just had the black lines done, and I still didn't know what it was going to look like. Yep, a little panic set in then. But after the colour - I was so happy. It's like a piece of artwork on my body! Thank god he was a talented artist!

I want to get another one with my dog's names. Just the script - no paws or anything cheese like that (maybe they do exist where they're not cheesy but I haven't seen them). Just need to go and talk to someone - cause I can't figure out exactly how I want it to look or where I want it!

### endless — 2013-02-26T04:16:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77709>

So, from what I know (secondhand) the hood barely hurts at all. Remember, it\`s not the place with all the nerves...as far as cleaning, my SIL sells a saline solution or tells people to make their own up and use it frequently for the first week.

Now, Ashryn\`s nipple experience sounds great! I know others who would disagree about the pain and healing. I think that one varies a lot with nipples....if you\`ve breastfed, for example, your nipples tend to be less sensitive. A friend of mine didn\`t have complete healing for almost a year (meaning that she continued to have \`crusties\`form, not pain). My ex had his done when he lost a bet on a hockey game and he was not impressed with the pain involved!

Having said that, I still toy with the idea of doing mine. I hate my nipples, and keep thinking that I might like them better afterwards. Still have not decided though.

Pretty sure you are kidding Darleen but please don\`t take your cat\`s valium...it might be the same but last summer my mom had a late night accident and took her dog\`s morphine and got really sick. Only my mother, I tell ya.......

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-26T04:30:52+0200

I almost hesitate to ask but you know me. Filters off. HOW on earth can you "hate" your nipples? I mean I really like mine  cause they're just so much fun.

No babies, so no breast feeding so probably ULTRA sensitive in regards to that big ol' needle. Howsa bout I come to Canada and we get your SIL to do both of us at the same time. We can hold hands and squeal together then commiserate together. Of course, you would have to go first.  "Cause honestly the only way I would ever do it is if someone jumped off that cliff with me. ### endless — 2013-02-26T04:35:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77714>

Okay, so my consult....

As one would expect, it was heavy on the current stuff. My main takeaway is that I can't get into fat burning because I am electron deficient. I have low DHEA and slightly high FBG and insulin. My location means that I get only a few months in which I can gain electrons from sun exposure and eat carbs....the rest of the time I need to follow a high fat epi paleo diet, CT and ground, and utilize tanning beds until my body is replenished enough to get into fat burning mode.

We discussed how I need to protect myself in my work environment and around the fire trucks, and ways to do that. Tin foil was mentioned I have a few other ideas too. I'm to monitor my progress by watching my labs...when DHEA rises and insulin and FBG drop I'll know I am improving.

I'm kind of excited to do some tanning, actually....just waiting for my latest Vit D level as my last one (Nov) was a bit lower than I need to protect myself for the tanning bed.

### endless — 2013-02-26T04:43:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77716>

> I almost hesitate to ask but you know me. Filters off. HOW on earth can you "hate" your nipples? I mean I really like mine cause they're just so much fun.

I like the sensitivity of them, but I've never liked the way they look....I have 'puffy' nipples, so my areolae extend all the way to the end of the nipple, giving my breasts a round shape with no 'point'. When they are erect, they are extremely tiny (more mouse parts) and 'underdeveloped' looking. Maybe if I'd had a child and breastfed they would have changed shape. They are also really pale in color....almost the same as my skin. I swear that when I look in the mirror it almost looks like I don't even HAVE nipples.

My SIL swears that she can pierce them in a way that makes them extend normally....I'm still on the fence.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-26T20:50:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-77845>

Well. Hmm. Mine kinda disappeared along with my hormones but since I've been on the Wiley Protocol for 19 months now they are a deep dark pink. There's a hint for ya. Tanning should help that too. the pointy thing? All I can suggest is finding someone to help you with massages :wink:wink: and maybe that will fix 'em.

Here's a hint on the tanning beds as well. I started at minutes and got PINK. So took it back to 7 for the next 2 sessions then 8 for 3 sessions then 9 for 2 sessions. I'm now up to 10 and that's where I'm staying. 3 times a week. Did the standing session for 6 minutes on Saturday and will do one again friday. Tomorrow will do the lie down for 10.

I did some searching on the net and found that 10 minutes in a tanning bed is APPROXIMATELY = to 2 hours in the sun. So I figured 3 times a week was more than enough. We'll see what my labs have to say. Oh and you might want to get some sunscreen for your nips. Don't want to burn THEM.

### diane — 2013-02-27T15:40:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78002>

Good thing a volunteer fireman is smoke free! LOL (my poor attempt at humour). But congrats - I smoked for a few years ago, but got disgusted with it and quit. Now if I could get disgusted with M&M's. LOL.

### endless — 2013-02-27T17:57:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78020>

I have quit several times over the course of my smoking career....the last time for a couple years before starting again because I got into a relationship with a smoker. Unfortunately that was not the only toxic thing about THAT situation. I think I can confidently say that this is my LAST 'quit'. I highly doubt that I will ever smoke again this time.

Caroline, actually it was not as hard this time as it has been in the past. I had an episode of tachycardia after smoking one night that scared the crap out of me. My dad has it, has had to have his heart shocked to stop it and regain normal rhythm, several times. I guess he has AFib. There are several others in the family with it, and it started to come on in middle age. I decided that I was not interested in joining the ranks and I didn't smoke again after that experience. I know (especially now)that there's a lot more to it, but smoking has always caused palpitations for me. If I ever have a fleeting wish for a smoke all I have to do is remember sitting here alone with my phone in my hand waiting for me heart to stop racing. It was terrifying.

Having said that, yeah, it was still hard! Luckily I didn't gain a bunch of weight, that would have made it much harder.

### endless — 2013-02-27T17:58:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78021>

Kelowna is about a 3-4 hour drive....not far at all! What are you doing there, Diane?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78026>

### endless — 2013-02-28T00:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78138>

I had the day off yesterday, so I took full advantage and made appointments that I have a hard time making on work days. Got my hair cut and went to see a guy I've been seeing for about a year. His business card says 'natural healer', so I'm not entirely sure what all modalities he uses. Usually he uses a pendulum and determines organ function and what deficiencies show up.

I've been trying everything to get this spinal/rib issue under control and nothing has worked so I called him up and asked if he could help. I spent the whole time (hour and a half or so) on the massage table. He does sort of an energy-bodywork, just locating and pressing on a spot while something else is happening. I have no idea how, but somehow he manages to move energy. You can tell when he's doing something because he will be yawning or breathing out differently. I know, it sounds bizarre but I swear to god I left there yesterday improved by about 90%. It's pretty interesting. I know a lot of people don't go in for the so-called 'woo woo' stuff, but I go with what works.

Anyway, I managed to convince my co-worker to work another day today so I could take an extra day off No real plans, just thought I needed a day to relax with no agenda, and to stay out of the car. I'm just focusing on letting my neck relax for the day. I took the pup for her walk this morning without worrying about the time...I managed to make myself a seafood soup for lunch with tumeric and bacon, I did my CT.....not much sun available to bask in, but oh well. I looked for some info on the tanning bed models used in my local salon but couldn't find anything. Other than the fact that they are electronic ballasts I have no idea if there are other variables that affect the amount of EMF.

I was kind of surprised that my doc seems completely CW about the sun and UV....I gave her my taped consult to listen to and she was concerned about the UV exposure. I was relating to her about how many people here have said they no longer burn in the sun but didn't really know all the reasons behind it...is it all about Vitamin D level, or does Omega 3/6 ratio figure into it as well....anybody remember?

Also, I got my period AGAIN.....another 14 day cycle! And that's without the progesterone....I was annoyed because I was just about to start progesterone for my luteal phase, and now I have to wait again. I think this time I will start it a bit earlier, like day 12, and just power on through if I start bleeding again...

My doc is going to South America for 2 months, so I got her to refill my prescriptions for a long time so I can maybe change things up if I need to while she is gone. She gave me a lab req. so that I can go a couple weeks before she gets back and get things rechecked. I'm really motivated to get my DHEA level up. I was discouraged that in almost 3 months of supplementing 50mg a day I only crept up from 2.1 to 3.2 (ref 1.4-14.7). Now going to focus on the tanning bed and see how that affects things.

So glad you are feeling better - that must be such a relief. Personally - I like woo woo. I found it really interesting what JK was saying about the crystal. I was in a woo woo shop the other day for the first time and she had all kinds of stones/rocks etc. etc. and they have all kinds of practioners for various things.

A new friend's sister is a clairvoyant and I want to see her but I am really scared. That really surprised me - I thought I would be up for that in a heartbeat.

### lioness7 — 2013-02-28T00:46:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78145>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=78139)
>
> Well shoot. I just looked up Gamma Dynacare labs, and there are none in BC. Still searching for the elusive RT3.....

You cannot get rt3 done in Canada anymore....the labs stopped sourcing the test two years ago. I read this on gamma's site when it happened. I think all the labs pulled it. Most docs don' t even know what it is. But there is a way to approximate rt3. I was shown that if you take t3 &t4 and plot each on a separate line graph of the lab range, if one is ok the results should line up. If they are too far apart consider rt3 issues...primitive but better than nothing.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-28T19:27:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78363>

I am SO glad you're feeling better!

Once you start the tanning bed I think you'll be hooked. It feels REALLY good. I'm sticking with 10 minutes after thinking about it. That's about equivalent to 6 hours in the sun per week (at 3 sessions/week for 10 minutes) so that seems plenty. When I can use the real deal I'll stay out longer but I don't think I need that much more exposure to any potential EMF's or dirty electricity. I AM excited to see if my D levels have come up any.

### endless — 2013-03-01T07:07:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78469>

I went for my first tan today, actually! I found myself waiting for a business to open so I could do some work, so I went in and they had room so I did it! I only did 5 minutes, in the mildest bed they had, but it was nice enough. I turned the face lamp off after only a couple minutes, it seemed really hot.

I've decided that I will stick to the lay down beds, even though I prefer the capsules. I remember the last time I tanned, before a kayak trip in Mexico, YEARS ago, I used the stand up capsules, and I enjoyed it...they were so warm and windy inside. BUT...all those fans are adding extra EMF that isn't necessary. When I tried the emf meter in my car, the level jumped way up when I turned on the defrost/heat. I think it was the fan...just like how a hair dryer is insanely high. In the bed, they provided an external fan that you could switch on if you want that blows in from the foot end....I didn't use it. I think I found the lowest EMF way to do it with what I have available, anyway....

I'm hoping I can sneak in another 5 minutes tomorrow sometime.

### endless — 2013-03-02T04:33:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78572>

I didn't feel claustrophobic, but I did have one of my 'what if' moments about the plexiglass or whatever it is under me breaking and cooking my flesh on the bulbs. Don't ask....I have a morbid monkey mind!

I was kind of hoping to get another one in today, but it didn't work out that way...I had two work calls in opposing directions involving lots of driving instead. ### endless — 2013-03-02T20:18:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78661>

I have been trying unsuccessfully for awhile now to incorporate more seaweed into my diet....so I decided to go back to the seaweed water idea. I tried it a few months ago and didn't like it at all, but yesterday I stuck a big piece of kombu into a half gallon canning jar and filled it up. It was better this time, for some reason. I kept drinking out of it and refilling it for the day and night, and then started a new jar. I'm not eating the seaweed after I soak it, but maybe I'll figure out a way to use it. For now it goes into the compost and eventually the garden.

I'm going to attempt to make it into the tanning salon again today.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-02T20:23:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78663>

Just my personal N1 is I go every other day 3 times a week. And at the end of 94 minutes I'm a lovely light golden brown. Don't need a sticker to see the color change either. I got white sidewalls from only doing the lie down bed. So I've done the standing twice now and am aiming for twice a week lie down and once, standing. And I have to say ... I LIKE IT (the tanning bed thing). Just paid for another 200 minutes. I'm hoping that will get me far enough into Spring that I can transition to natural sunbathing. boy my neighbors across the creek and on top of the hill are going to get an eyeful until what's left of the trees leaf out.

Which reminds me. I should probably ask Jack. Grounding ... can one lie on a towel or does it have to be directly on the grass/ground? Grass makes me itchy.

Or I suppose I could read the damned earthing book. But I'm about 1/3 through Jack's and don't want to split my attention.

### endless — 2013-03-03T05:22:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78711>

Grass makes me itchy, too. I would rather lay on a big towel. Maybe I'll just spend my naked time gardening, then I won't have to lay down at all!

I popped into a different salon today for another 5 minute tan. This one was cheaper, only $1 a minute. The young girl working there had absolutely NO information availabole about the beds, so I decided to take a chance. I'll find out about the ballasts during the week when the owner is in. I did the lie down one again, didn't turn on the face area, but I still feel it a bit...my face was a bit red afterward. Luckily I had just picked up my skincare stuff so I put some Vitamin C serum on. My face is so bloody sensitive! It felt good to lie there in the warm, I do enjoy that.

Oyster day in town today, I picked up 2 dozen and ate one for dinner. I'll save the other for breakfast. These ones are SO good! They are almost a little bit sweet, or something. They taste different than the last bunch I bought from him, anyway.

### diane — 2013-03-03T05:29:41+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-03T18:43:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78758>

And yeah, Diane ... timing is everything. I try to go midday which is when I would go out in the sun for some vitamin D therapy.

### endless — 2013-03-03T19:55:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78762>

Wow, Darleen...both salons I've been to have multiple brands of lotion specifically for tanning, and people have their own private stashes that they keep there on shelves with their names on them. It seems like a big part of the revenue stream, wonder why your place doesn't do it? I'm thinking that most of them must have some sort of self tanner in them, or some other crap....they all promise a 'fast deep tan'.

I'm thinking that my T is too high....been looking around online (bad idea, almost always!) and some of the symptoms are definitely cropping up. The 'testiness', some acne, and my weight is climbing again for no obvious reason. While I have not noticed any hair on my face or nipples, I noticed an increase of sparse hairs creeping outward on my upper thighs....I haven't waxed down there for a LONG time, but I don't remember it being so far outside the 'bikini area' before.

Wondering if I need to switch to the 7-Keto. I can't get that here so it would mean a trip to the US which I can't really afford right now. In the meantime I'm dropping my dose from 50mg to 25mg to see what that does. Not happy about it though, my levels are so low I hate to slow down my recovery!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-03-03T20:20:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78769>

I definitely had the testiness. My T was low way back when I did the testing and after months on supps for Pg and adrenals I added the supp to support T. I wasn't on it long when I had extreme testiness but I didn't realize what was causing it so I continued with the supp for at least another month. Fnally I mentioned it to my pharmacist and he told me I didn't need it anymore. Luckily after a couple of weeks the testiness went away.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-03T20:30:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78772>

Lack of sleep causes me to be a major bitch. I'm not gonna sugar coat it and call it being "testy" either. Even now if I miss the least little amount of sleep I get majorly grumpy. So I would ask ... how's your sleep ... before I started ditching supps. I doubled l my dhea intake from 25 to 50 with another 15 of the cream a couple of times a week AND I increased my T cream to 5 "lines" a couple of weeks ago. pfbbt. Might as well be trying to fill a swimming pool with thimble. Which is why I"m seeing the NP on Tuesday and getting some labs ordered. I also had doubled my pregnenalone, am doing the tanning thing, the sunlight in the eyes first thing in the AM thing ... still eating BAB and still getting in seafood several times a week. way low on the carbs as well. Although recently I've been adding some back in. Had cole slaw last night. so something is still outta whack.

My point being, don't ditch the dhea just yet. If it were me, I'd take a long hard look at what's going on in the big picture.

My vitamin D level was likely in the crapper back then. Current Vitamin D level is only 52. I spent about 3.5 hours sunning on a deck yesterday sans clothing (11 am to 2:30 pm) and didn't even turn pink. I loved it and so did my DH - although he liked the hormonal affect

### diane — 2013-03-04T00:07:12+0200

### endless — 2013-03-04T03:54:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78869>

Actually, Darleen, my sleep has not been great lately, but it's less about the quality and more about the timing. I struggle to stay awake at night and often fall asleep too early, and the wake at 4, 4:30, 5am....and I can be a major bitch as well, but this is different. It's like when Paulalynn was talking about getting pissed off at the dog for wagging it's tail too loud. That's what I am experiencing....irritation, short temper, for no good reason!

I am on 200 mg pregnenalone and 50 mg DHEA. No progesterone until I can fit it back into my whacko cycles. I am dropping the DHEA to 25 mg just to see if I notice anything....if not I will try upping it instead. The only other thing that has changed for me is drinking seaweed water instead of regular water....I can't see that causing any of my symptoms...I wish I could get my T tested sooner! I have to wait for my doc to return from her 2 month trip...she doesn't have a locum filling in.

Fitness, I think the beds have improved over the years..I remember having a reaction to one years ago as well. I'm sure they were all magnetic ballasts back then as well. I wish I had a sunny deck to lie out on! I can't wait till the sun comes back here.

Diane, I have done 2 as well....have not noticed any color yet though! I'm going again tomorrow.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-04T20:51:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78935>

Krista ... believe me when I tell you I understand the bitchiness. Lack. Of. Sleep. As a reminder I spent 20+ years with pretty bad insomnia. The year following my hyst I literally ... LITERALLY, AND I'M NOT EXAGGERATING ... slept only 2 hours a night. Didn't matter WHEN I went to bed. 2 hours and I was up and unable to sleep again. That was the year I became a total zombie. I went from bitchy to EXTREME bitchy and cried at the drop of a hat. I cannot believe I survived that. The answer for me was Pg. It didn't make it 100% better but I finally was able to sleep more than 2 hours. Full on BHRT, the paleo and now epi-paleo diet, plus plenty of DHEA and now the tanning bed have brought me back to good sleep. Some times it's absolutely stellar.

Tanning beds ... YMMV. I didn't begin to show any color change for several weeks. And it's barely noticeable. But then, I'm pretty pale because I have spent my entire adult life out of the sun.

### endless — 2013-03-04T23:25:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-78973>

I'm hoping I reach 'stellar' someday soon, when it comes to sleep! I have faith that it'll happen. I'm sure looking forward to getting back on the P and hoping to see an actual difference!

I'm reflecting back on the changes I've made over the past year....well, almost year. I would like to have a little more evidence that everything I am doing is resulting in positive changes....but frankly, aside from some initial weight loss I have not really noticed a lot, other than increased tolerance for cold. I know that eating raw feels good, as does CT (Kinda) and I've known for years that EMFs are bad, although I've not been extremely proactive about reducing them until recently. I think reading about other people's 'wins', especially those who have faced much more serious health challenges, has motivated me, and made me think more about certain aspects of my life and habits. But if I am to look at it honestly, I am largely the same as I was one year ago. At least as far as I can tell....who knows, my rockstarishness might be all internal at this point.

I continue to work slowly through the blogs, but I still find I get the most benefit and clarity from webinars and from reading other people's explanations on the forum. What's bothering me lately is the direction this forum has taken. I get that if people are ONLY here to disturb the shit, that measures should be taken to nip that in the bud, but silencing anyone who questions too much or offers up other information for discussion sits wrongly with me. Dr. K's response to someone named 'Owl' in the suggestion forum sits uneasily with me as well. I'm not sure what to make of it. I'm keeping my mind and my ears open for now.....

Off to bask in 5 minutes of electrons.

### endless — 2013-03-05T18:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-79116>

Well, another tanning session under the belt...I enjoy laying there in the warmth. I did 6 minutes this time, still in the lowest intensity bed. I have not noticed anything as far as improved sleep or energy, but I am hopeful! I know the sun improves my mood so hopefully this will, too.

I am once again faced with the sad reality that I have to give up wine. I've tried, several times, to have a glass over the past couple of weeks, and failed. Meaning, as I drive home from work I am looking forward to enjoying a glass with my meal, but once I open it, I realize I have absolutely no desire for it, so back in the cupboard it goes. Last night I was over at my brother's place and did have a couple of glasses while visiting, and I am regretting it a lot, now. My sleep was absolutely craptastic. I feel completely unrested today. I think I layed there awake from about 4am on, but even before that I was waking a lot. Finally gave up and got up at 6. I guess I can add that to the list of changes in the past year....alcohol tolerance has disappeared.

I'm drinking dulse water today...hopefully the seaweed will help rehydrate my poor silly brain.

### endless — 2013-03-06T17:38:01+0200

I've been looking up products that seem interesting for EMF protection....I want to get something for my car besides using clothing to shield myself. Just not really the right time for me to spend $300 in it all....well, more actually since it'll be coming over the border. I want the car protection asap, but it seems silly to order more than once. I guess I'm still on the fence.

Rather gloomy out there this morning. I'm trying to decide what my day will look like, I habe some work to complete that will involve about a 300km loop on mountain roads, either today or tomorrow.Best check the weather and pick the best day I suppose.

I had planned a good make-up sleep for last night after the crappy one the night before. I even took a melatonin, which I never do since Dr. K recommended not using it. Of course as soon as I took it and got into bed my radio went off and there was a medical call. I was instantly wide awake and on alert...I didn't respond, there was 4 people going so I could bow out, but it took me a lot longer to get to sleep because of it. I'm glad I didn't go though, I may have been up all night. Sounded like a bad one, with someone bleeding from the jugular and police involved. I'm glad there were enough other responders available, I wasn't keen on attending that one anyway.

Waiting on my SIL, who swore she'd be up to walk this morning at 7:30...I have my doubts! If she doesn't call soon the pup and I will head out on our own. Dog walks are priority number one around here.

### endless — 2013-03-10T20:13:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-79975>

> A. Irish Moss (chondrus crispus, carrageen) is full of electrolyte minerals — calcium, magnesium, sodium and potassium. Its mucilaginous compounds help you detoxify, boost metabolism and strengthen hair, skin and nails. In Eastern medicine it is traditionally used for a low sex drive because it helps support T3 levels to convert LDL to progesterone and not to cortisol.
>
> B. Wakame (alaria, undaria) is a high-protein, high calcium seaweed, with carotenes, iron and vitamin C. It is used in Chinese medicine for hair growth and luster, and for skin tone because it optimizes thyroid function.
>
> C. Kelp (laminaria) contains vitamins the fat soluble vitamins A, B, E, D and K in high quantity, and is a major source of vitamin C from the sea. It also happens to be rich in many minerals found in land based plants. This is why it made my top ten supplement list so long ago in the blog series. Kelp proteins are high quality and present in abundance for a sea plant. It is a brown marine plant, kelp contains sodium alginate (algin), an element that helps remove radioactive particles (think Fukishima Iodine) and heavy metals from the body. Kelp can works as a blood purifier, relieves arthritis stiffness, and promotes adrenal, pituitary and thyroid health. Kelp’s natural iodine can normalize thyroid-related disorders like obesity and muscle fatigue seen in fibromyalgia cases. Clinical pearl: people with herpes virus of any type: It is a demulcent that helps eliminate herpes outbreaks after they have occurred. Kelp is nutrient rich and a small amount gives a large clinical result often.
>
> D. Hijiki is a mineral-rich, high-fiber seaweed, with 20% protein, vitamin A, carotenes and calcium. Hijiki has the most calcium of any sea green, 1400mg per 100 grams of dry weight.
>
> E. Kombu (laminaria digitata, setchelli, horsetail kelp), has a long tradition as a Japanese delicacy with reported nutritional healing value. Natural healers use it is a decongestant for excess mucous and to helps normalize blood pressure. Kombu has abundant iodine, carotenes, B, C, D and E vitamins, minerals like calcium, magnesium, potassium, silica, iron and zinc, and the powerful skin healing nutrient germanium, which is a pretty rare element in the human diet. Kombu is a meaty, high-protein seaweed. It is higher in natural mineral salts than most other seaweeds. I often add a strip of kombu to my bone broths and seafood broths.
>
> F. Nori (porphyra, laver) is a red sea plant with a sweet, meaty taste when dried. It contains nearly 50% balanced, assimilable protein, higher than any other sea plant. Nori’s fiber makes it popular for sushi wrapping. I do not eat any rice because its miRNA directly affects our DNA expression. Nori is rich in all the carotenes, calcium, iodine, iron, and phosphorus.
>
> G. Sea Palm (Postelsia Palmaeformis), American arame, grows only on the Pacific Coast of North America by Oregon and Washington State. It has a honeyed, salty taste that makes it tasty vegetable. I like it with my cauliflower rice recipe form the e-cookbook, or as a summer or autumn salad topping.
>
> H. Bladderwrack is loaded with vitamin K and it is an excellent adrenal adaptogen and it helps sensitize us to insulin to because of its K2 like effects. It is often used today by Native American cultures broths and in sauna/steam baths for degenerative arthritis and inflammatory joint conditions.
>
> I. Dulse (palmaria palmata), a red sea plant, very rich in iron. The last two sea veggies are my favorites because they have the most iodine of any plants on our planet. I was made aware of this sea vegetable when I was researching the Geritol solution for global warming. It also has abundant protein, and vitamin A. What really makes it a specially balanced nutrient for a lactovegetarian paleo diet is that it contains 300 times more iodine and 50 times more iron than wheat products. Tests on dulse show antiviral action against the herpes virus.
>
> J. Arame (Eisenia bycyclis), is one of the ocean’s richest sources of iodine. It often contains more iodine than seafood does! Because of its high iodine content it can have major effects on women’s progesterone to estradiol ratio’s naturally. If you are estrogen dominant you might consider using this as become a staple in your broths and recipes. Many natural healers use arame to help reduce breast and uterine fibroids, excessive bleeding, fibrocystic disease of the breasts, and ovarian cysts. It is also quite helpful in cases of PCOS with acne and excessive facial hair. It also contains a lot fat soluble vitamins and phytohormones, to normalize peri menopausal and menopausal symptoms. Arame is often associated with soft wrinkle-free skin turgor and tends to enhances hair growth and make your hair have an incredible sheen. The reason is because this veggie increase your free T3 to a dramatic degree when it is used regularly in your diet.

My Kombu water has a syrupy texture, even after refilling the jar several times. The taste is fresh and 'oceany'. The Dulse tints the water a bit, and becomes very thin and tasteless after soaking. Adding too much makes the water a bit too salty for my taste. The third seaweed I used is not in a marked bag. Defiinitely kelp of some kind, but much larger and a different color than the Kombu. It has almost a slightly bitter taste for the first jar, but mellows after that. Haven't tried Hijiki or Wakame yet, although I have them.

Tanning has been every second day since starting on Feb. 28th. Up to 7 minutes now, but won't have a chance to go back until mid week.

Cycle day 14 today, 3d day back on progesterone, fingers crossed that TOTM doesn't show up again. DHEA still reduced to 25mg. Still having 'testy' time of day between about 4-6pm but seems less intense.

Took a melatonin last night because of time change and staying up later than usual. Sleep was pretty good, 7 hours with one pee break.

### endless — 2013-03-10T23:20:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-79995>

Car shielding info. magnetic shielding foil minimum 0.010 thickness

> Shielding
>
> Shielding is like air cleaners - it’s a help, but it cannot make a bad situation good, just a good one better. Shielding is not a magic cure; it can produce a reduction of from 30 to 70 percent, if done well. In exceptional cases, a 90 percent reduction is possible. Very highly sensitive people may need reduction of more than 99.9% from all sources.
>
> EMF is complicated to shield, especially at the low frequencies present in a car. Expect to experiment.
>
> Shielding is most effective if placed close to the source, rather than further away from it. The exception is when a source is so strong it overwhelms (saturates) the shielding material.
>
> If the shielding is further away from the source, more shielding material is needed to cover the area and there is a higher possibility of holes and slits through which the EMF can get through.
>
> To get an idea what shielding can do, try to measure the radiation from a household breaker box, with both the steel lid opened and closed.
>
> To shield a cable, or other point source, it is best to completely wrap it with shielding material.
>
> If an area cannot be wrapped, try to cover the source on three sides. The middle of that shield will be the most shielded area.
>
> It is important that the shield is much wider than the source. Otherwise, the EMF will simply loop around the edges.
>
> Multiple layers of shielding material work, but a double layer is not twice as effective as one layer. No matter how many layers are added, it’ll never reach 100 percent.
>
> Sometimes shielding must be made from multiple pieces, to fit the location. This is fine, as long as the pieces overlap with no holes or slits. An overlap of an inch is good, and aluminum tape can be used to keep the pieces tightly together.
>
> Typical places to consider shielding are the cables that may run along the side of the car, and in the foot well. It may work to cover parts of the footwell with shielding and wrap it around the center console. Be careful not to interfere with the use of the pedals, especially if any shielding comes loose. That could be very dangerous.
>
> The closer the shielding is to the source, the better. It is probably of no use to try to shield anything if the shield is more than 12 inches (30cm) from the source, as a rule of thumb. The exception may be if the person is instead wrapped with a shield, like if the entire footwell is covered with shielding material. This can sometimes be done nicely by taking out the padding on the sides of the footwell, put shielding foil there, and then putting the padding back on again. That way the shielding is held firmly in place, and is also not visible. Expect to have to experiment a lot, using a gaussmeter to check with.
>
> **Aluminum foil, and meshes of copper and silver are usually not of any help in a car, as they are most effective at higher frequencies. Only if computerized components are being shielded should these materials be considered.**
>
> The strongest EMF radiation in a car happens a low frequencies, which are best handled with steel and mumetal. Ordinary plate steel works well, and is cheap. The very best steel alloy is 5% silicone steel, grain oriented. Mumetal is a special alloy developed for this purpose; it comes in thin foils that are easy to work with, but it is pricey.
>
> At high levels of EMF, the shielding can become saturated and ineffective. It can then be better to move the shielding material back a few inches, or use another type of material better suited. Perhaps then use a sandwich, with a special high-radiation alloy on the inside and a more regular type material on the outside. Experiment.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-15T12:04:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80650>

Today find a beautiful place in your mind and get lost. Habits are a constant evaluation of what we think and do. Many habits we have, good or bad, come from old beliefs we hold and haven't challenged in awhile. If you are looking to make a huge transformation in your body, relationships, business, etc. you must first peel back the layers of the onion on your beliefs and see where they are coming from. Once you either reconfirm the belief or establish a new one, then you can see if your habits are serving you or not. Aristotle said, "We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, therefore, is not an act, but a habit." First we have to think to practice a new habit and soon it become automatic and happens without us thinking of it. Eventually our habits mirror our consciousness. Our character is basically a composite of our habits. Because they are consistent, and begin unconsciously, they constantly, daily, express our mind's patterns. There are two great habits often overlooked in life and these in my experience are: listening to and observing the world around you. Listening and observing has to be practiced and cultivated and when they are, a whole new world of understanding opens up. Suddenly, we don't need anymore books, advice, or small talk to engage in. Today begin to listen and observe to gain a foundation for growth. Let us begin to practice more to listen and observe, ourselves more and then everything else and see where it elevates you. Will you listen to your world today and every day henceforth?

Had 6 chicken wings and a handful of pork rinds for lunch, while sitting in the sun on my car. Dinner was wild boar and venison sausages with onion, fennel and cabbage cooked in bacon fat. I've never been that way with nuts, so I thought they would be a good choice. I guess not when I'm not indulging in the things I USED to indulge in. I can keep the macadamias in the car though....it's almost impossible to overeat them, they are about 78% fat!

Caroline, I am going to see this [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9sMO7npf9lU](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9sMO7npf9lU) DJ and dance my ass off for about 4 hours Not really a great representaion of the music variety, but you get the idea. It's not exactly optimal, doesn't start till 10pm, but I enjoy it and it's only once in awhile. There's always someone who needs a designated driver too. I can't imagine drinking and doing that!

managed to get 3.5 hours between dinner and sleep last night, and only up once to pee but I think it was very early morning so a fairly good stretch of time. I have to do inventory today so will be busy regardless of whether or not I end up out on the road. I guess I have to attend re-certification next week for my medical first responder which is expiring this year...it's 7-9:30 pm 3 nights next two weeks! I'd rather do the daytime weekend ones but I didn't arrange it. That means lots of bright light next week.

Speaking of bright light, the sun is coming out so better get the pup out for her walk before she leaves without me.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-16T18:18:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80780>

Krista sounds like fun! Even if it IS way past my bedtime these days.

I have the same problem with cashews which is why I HAVE to stay away from them. Raw almonds ... I only over indulge if I go to long past mealtime. Any more a small handful (like 8 or 9 of 'em) will do me fine as a quick snack.

Did you try Jack's remedy for cravings? Somebody posted it somewhere around here ... drink an ounce of olive oil.

### janagram — 2013-03-16T20:41:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80797>

mm, I ate about an oz of pasture butter yesterday before dinner! so good.

### endless — 2013-03-16T21:43:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80804>

> Did you try Jack's remedy for cravings? Somebody posted it somewhere around here ... drink an ounce of olive oil.

Nope. I think that would cure me of cravings for sure though, since I'd be barfing. I drank olive oil as part of a gallbladder flush once and it was bad enough mixed with grapefruit juice!

Eating butter, however, I am totally on board with.

I expect the craving thing to drop off quickly anyway...I've been out of most of my supps for over a week and finally back on them all, plus cutting out the kombucha and zevia. The other day as a 'treat' I had 4oz of full fat coconut milk with 4 drops of stevia and a few drops of vanilla, and that was perfect. I have organic cherry jello I can make with stevia if I feel the need. I sometimes crave sweet and fruity, like gummy berries or dried fruit, and that's when the dark chocolate or manna doesn't help.

Inventory is done, and the day has turned drizzly and cold. I'm going to eat my lunch of leftovers and then decide whether to get outside and dig a new flowerbed or stay in and ct....

### endless — 2013-03-17T19:30:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80875>

Beautiful sunny day today! It's cool and windy but I was able to sit out with my feet on the ground anyway. I'd stay there but I have an appointment. Hopefully still sunny when I get back. Spring cleaning has begun...I washed all the 'cat blankets' that protect my chairs and did some hair removal with a sticky roller. Took my swiffer vac apart and fixed it. Love that thing, it's so great for sucking up hair fluffs off the hard floors.

Eggs, avocado and bp chai for breakfast. I made a grass fed roast in the crock pot last night. It was a blade roast. Extremely fatty...I took off a lot of the fat that didn't render out, but the rest I just blended with the juice and the onion and it made really good gravy. had it last night with just sauteed mushrooms, but tonight I'll make some cauliflower mash.

### janagram — 2013-03-17T19:37:07+0200

mmm, nice dinner. tonite I'll be having...what else? corned beef. (st. Pat)
and good on ya for spring cleaning....still haven't recovered from a massive one I did 2 yrs ago. Traumatized! (I obsessed ...went around on hands and knees with a dust rag...)

speakin of CTing....which I haven't done in many moons....I was wondering if living in a warm climate would help. Like you're always warm, so CT sounds appealing. Since just being in cold air doesn't seem to count as Ct. Forgive me using your journal to ponder.....

Nope.....not for me anyway! I am sitting in a tub right now with ice on top of me - bot not happy!

### endless — 2013-03-18T01:01:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80911>

I have a hard time CTing if I am already cold, so for me I think summer would be easier. Today I would have seriously considered getting in the river but it was really windy. The water is probably 38 but would feel colder because of the current I would think.

Speaking of CT, I think I have noticed some strength gains from it. I have taken the 'no exercise' LR pretty seriously and other than walking have done almost nothing for the last year (April I think was when I started...) Today I had a fitness assessment with a friend who is a personal trainer, because like an idiot I have signed up for the Tough Mudder in Whistler in June and I need to start training. She was actually very amazed at how strong I was for how little I have done. I had to keep going up in weight so she could get me to failure on bicep curls. I managed to plank for 1.5 minutes. Now if only my body would begin to LOOK like it's strong and fit. I am really hoping this bloated middle of mine starts to deflate soon, it's so embarrassing to not be able to suck in my gut.

### diane — 2013-03-18T03:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80929>

Way to go Krista! Those strength gains are amazing!

### endless — 2013-03-18T05:01:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80934>

Thanks Diane! I'm pretty impressed, and I figure it must be the CT that's kept me from becoming a total weakling....good motivation to keep it up.

I started digging a flower bed beside the porch today. I can tell I will be outside lifting heavy things very soon, whenever it's not raining!

### caroline — 2013-03-18T06:48:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80936>

That is awesome krista.....before you know it you will be back to your swelte self! That is what JK has been saying all along .....you will get much fitter and stronger without the exercise

I see so many people jogging at the beach on a hot day and I just see a heart attack waiting to happen.. Lots of older and really out of shape people too. There was a young girl yesterday who was very obese and her trainer kept hollering at her to pick up the pace. I was hoping she had her cell on her to call an Ambo.

### endless — 2013-03-18T17:00:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-80977>

I'm glad I don't fall for that anymore. Sometimes I watch The Biggest Loser on tv and their workouts are alarming! I can't even imagine what their cortisol levels must be at. Yet, how do they lose so much weight? It's a mystery to me.

Things seem to be ok on my HAB regime....I'm making sure to have a BP matcha every morning for the fat, and today it was 3 eggs and an avocado again. Lunch yesterday was a can of tuna with celery and green onion and curry powder and mayo, eaten outside in the sun with bare feet!! Love that spring is here

Friday the oyster merchant is in town, I will buy my usual 2 dozen and have a feast. Maybe some mussels this time, too. I\`m not buying them from the grocery store lately, I\`m trying to cut down on my spending as the supps were pretty expensive this last order. I\`ll eat the GF beef and elk in the freezer and the canned fish, and save my splurge for the oysters.

### caroline — 2013-03-18T22:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81017>

sounds like a good plan .....I am spending so much money too.....I am gonna need to get a job!

### endless — 2013-03-19T03:13:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81048>

Well, had a can of sardines for lunch while sitting in a parking lot in my car....not ideal but what the hell. I need to have something more, though, I was really hungry by 4pm. I forgot to put the mac nuts in the car, a few of those would have been enough I think.

Strange weather...snow one minute, sun the next. Bought myself a wheelbarrow today for gardening purposes, but when I went to assemble it I discovered they'd given me the wrong handles Sooo, I could either drill new holes, or wait till tomorrow and exchange them. I'm thinking I won't be building the flower bed tonight so I might just wait.

I started my 'couch to 5k style' running training today....1 minute running, 2 minutes walking, repeat 10 times. After this week it's increasing the running by 1 minute a day till you are running for 30 minutes. Running is not really my thing but I will have to train in it a bit for the event in June. It's a 20km course so if I don't run some of it we'll be out there for hours! The CT is good training too...here's a pic of one of the course obstacles from last year.... { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/tmvan-jpg.57/)

### endless — 2013-03-19T17:07:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81094>

My sleep seems to be getting better. I don't wake up thinking 'wow, what an awesome sleep', but I often don't get up to pee until about 5am, and that's usually after about 7 hours. Ideally I'd like to sleep 8, but I've never been a long sleeper so 7 might be my limit. I try to stay up but 10 is my usual bedtime.

Pan fried sole fillets for breakfast, with my BP matcha. No ripe avocados this morning so I grabbed a couple slices of bacon to top off the fat. No carbs other than the coconut flour breading I used. I peed on a stick this morning out of curiosity and I am definitely in ketosis now, despite having a single glass of wine with dinner and a few squares of 80%. I think cutting the dairy out (except butter) and laying off the stevia soda and kombucha has made a huge difference in how I feel.

I've made it to day 23 so far this cycle with the progesterone started on day 12. Was feeling a bit twingy last night, so I'm not sure if I will make it the whole 28 days this time or not, but at least I'm heading back in the right direction! Hopefully this bloating will start to decrease as well. I was thinking of stopping the D-Ribose for a week to see if that might be contributing, after reading that thread with Mamagrok's experience. Maybe next cycle. Right now I'm just visualizing getting my period at the right time, and peeing out all the extra fluid my body seems to be storing!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-19T17:09:48+0200

### endless — 2013-03-19T19:52:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81113>

Wow, I'm getting off lucky today....no work intruding on my life yet today  Did another 30 minutes of intervals followed by 15 minutes of lower body CT. I was tempted to try the river but didn't really plan for it when I left the house....maybe tomorrow if it's appropriate.

Sun shining again today too...now that my legs are freezing I think I'll head outside for some sun and see if I can warm them back up.

### endless — 2013-03-20T03:21:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81152>

Well, the sun may not be high enough to give me vitamin D, but it is high enough to burn....I have a sunburn on my face and chest after being out for about 2 hours this morning. Makes me wonder how high my D has to be to protect me. Higher than it is now, obviously! I've put aloe on and vitamin C serum, but it's still very red and inflamed.

Anyone have any good remedies?

### vkiernan — 2013-03-20T13:31:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81168>

I use to put vinegar on a cloth and keep lightly pressing it on the skin for a bit. Then the heat will come out (you will feel it) and you will feel much better. It can be difficult to put vinegar by the nose though, LOL! Good luck.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-20T17:07:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81182>

cold wet tea bags also should help ... the tannin in the tea is supposed to soothe sunburn. And this is why I started the tanning booth in February. I wanted to be adjusted (i.e.tanned) before I got out in the real thing. Some of the worst sunburns I had as a kid I got in early Spring in mid-morning. Only for me it would only take about 20 minutes, not two hours. So maybe you've made progress?

And just fyi, when I was in college I went skiing 2 days in a row and blistered my face (this was in the dark ages before sunscreen). I peeled 3 layers of skin and looked like a raccoon for months. There was some nasty concoction called Foille ??? the infirmary gave me to put on the burn. THAT one didn't hurt because it was so bad. It was just ugly.

### endless — 2013-03-20T17:47:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81190>

So I am confused about the vitamin D thing. How can the sun be strong enough to burn, but not strong enough to stimulate Vit. D? That doesn't make sense to me.

thanks for the suggestions, I will do both I think. I'm a little bummed about the burn (although it felt SO GOOD sitting out there yesterday!) because today it is snowing and gray again and it would have been a perfect day for the tanning salon. I think I'll be laying off for several days to heal my skin instead. I used an ice pack on it last night, and will do some face dunks this morning (maybe cold tea instead? Hmmm...)

Kind of ok with the snow other than that....I am SORE today, and not upset about missing my running/walking. I did that in the morning yesterday, followed by finally putting together my wheelbarrow and finishing my new flowerbed, which involved lifting some very heavy things (rocks). Then I raked the yard and various other tasks, followed by fire practice. I did stretch last night before bed but my muscles are sore today. I think the CT helped, I did it right after the run, and only the lower body.

I took an extra dose of mag last night, and I am feeling it this morning in the nether regions. Funny, the other three types (malate, orotate, threonate) combined are a fairly substantial amount, but I've never gotten loose stools from them. The citrate, I get it often. This time could have been just pushing me over the edge of tolerance, but I've noticed it with smaller doses as well. I wonder if that means I SHOULD take citrate, or shouldn't?

I went nuts in Crappy Tire yesterday and bought a whole bunch of bulbs! Freesias, lilies, tulips, hostas, crazy Peruvian daffodils, astilbe and begonias. I haven't even made the shade bed yet, so getting a bit ahead of myself here. I also bought deer netting, and I am going to need it. Tulips are like deer crack.

### vkiernan — 2013-03-20T19:10:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81197>

Nothing like planting all those great things and reaping the loveliness from it! Keep writing, you might motivate me to do more. I have an open bed that has been sitting for years and really needs some attention.

### caroline — 2013-03-20T23:20:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81226>

I have no affinity for plants at all - wish I did......sounds like you are quite an all rounder! Seems like you are a very handy gal!

### endless — 2013-03-21T18:05:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81322>

I try, Caroline! I have been single for a good while too, so usually if I want something done I have to learn to do it myself!

I used to have a huge vegetable garden, and I grew most of our food. I started getting burnt out because I was the only one doing most of the work (I was living with a hedonist who always found something more enjoyable to do than weed) so I quit. I moved around a lot for the last several years, and last year was my first garden again in awhile. I am back to loving it...even though I still have beautiful winter squash under my bed that I have not been eating! Guess I should give the rest away. I can easily grow all my onions and garlic for the year, and usually I grow lots of beets and carrots as well. I suppose I will shift my focus a bit to reflect my changing diet. No need for winter squash or root veggies in such abundance, and I will grow more sturdy greens. I will still grow lots of tomatos and make fresh salsa, because I love growing tomatos.

Less than stellar dinner last night...I worked fairly steadily all day and then stopped in at a gym on my way home to try out the new program my trainer friend made for me. I did only half of it though, it was getting last and I was so hungry. I picked up a cooked rotisserie chicken and some greek salad and vinaigrette slaw at the grocery store because I couldn't wait. So....not even good quality chicken! Oh well. At least I didn't use it as an excuse to go get gluten free pizza.

Breakfast was the same, leftovers from dinner plus my BP matcha. I'm very impressed with my body for handling the caffeine so well this past week! I still get a bit of 'bumpiness' in ym heartbeat, but the palpitations seem to be much less than they used to be. I have been avoiding this matcha powder because when I tried it before it made my heart crazy. I'm taking D-Ribose in the morning too, wonder if that helps?

I am going to try to hit the gym again today. My body is really loving the activity after so long avoiding it. I am staying mindful of my cortisol and making sure I don't get too intense. Making an effort to spend my evening 'tv' time on the floor stretching instead of just sitting.

### janagram — 2013-03-22T02:04:51+0200

yeah, I am doing that latter thing too! I love it! I get into some deep stretches, downward dog (that makes it hard to watch the tv), some plank poses, just plain squatting...its fun.

### endless — 2013-03-22T04:37:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81406>

I gave up at my old place too Darleen. This time I am going to make some beds and cover them with that fine black deer mesh. You can hardly see it but they HATE it. I also clean my comb out after I wash my hair and toss the strands on the beds...they don't like that either.

So I just spent some time on [http://rhythmicliving.org/](http://rhythmicliving.org/) and I am slightly alarmed.....I am seriously wondering about high dose BHRT now. How could SO MANY women all complain of weight gain and other negatives from it without there being something to it? It's bizarre.

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-22T14:11:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81425>

I remember asking JK a question after the Q and A on hormones....he is not a big supporter of women having periods after they go through menopause from taking BHRT. It's a matter of degree I think. There are certainly a lot of opinions on both sides of the fence.

### endless — 2013-03-22T17:18:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81451>

That there is....I am still cycling naturally, just trying to boost my very low levels, but I am going to proceed with caution. I don't think throwing large doses around is necessarily right for everyone.

That being said, I am on cycle day 26, and this will be my last day using the P cream this cycle! I am so glad I made it without premature bleeding! I figured out that I have been using 50mg per day, once a day. If I increase, I think I will just start using it twice a day for 100mg total. The compounding pharmacist I spoke to said that I am probably getting very good absorption from using it vaginally, better than dermally or orally, so I will stick with it. Not going to change anything else until my next set of labs, and see where to go from there.

### diane — 2013-03-23T02:48:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81509>

I've got the oral progesterone - start it tonight. I asked the doctor which one she recommended -she said oral, so I figure I have to start somewhere. Meanwhile, I can tell that the Vitex has been doing something to progesterone, as a lot of things have started to come back. Kinda cool, actually.

### endless — 2013-03-23T03:29:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81512>

Good to know Diane! Dr. K told me that oral is better for sleep. I have some, but I haven't tried it yet. I also have some vitex tincture that I have had for a long time...wonder if it'd still be effective. I might try adding that in next cycle.

### endless — 2013-03-23T20:34:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81557>

Yesterday I went to the gym and did both my running intervals and my weight program...plus some core work and lots of stretching when I got home. My body is obviously craving the movement after so long! I'm moving slowly because I had metatarsalgia on my left foot after a night of dancing in some inappropriate footwear months ago, and I'm just starting in minimalist runners so I don't want to push my feet into a chronic injury. So far so good! I'm stretching and exercising my feet a lot too, trying to rehab them after years of using high-tech super supportive running shoes. Poor feet, they have been made so weak from not being allowed to be feet and move naturally!

Today is a rest day, exercise-wise, because tonight I will be dancing again (this time in proper shoes!) and will get a good workout that way It's a beautiful sunny day here....COLD in the breeze but it's tempting to go and bare it all anyway. I'm not, because of my recent experience with burning myself. I'll do some limited exposure.

Breakfast was salmon ceviche and avocado with BP herbal chai. I haven't had raw salmon in awhile, it was a nice treat. Didn't get oysters this weekend so that was my consolation.

### caroline — 2013-03-24T00:37:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81585>

It sounds like everything is coming together nicely for you Krista.....have a great nite of dancing - I am very jealous!
You must get to meet tons of guys! Have you got your groove on?

### diane — 2013-03-24T02:43:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81590>

Yes, the right dancing shoes are very important! What kind of music do you dance to Krista? Hope you have an awesome night!

### endless — 2013-03-24T19:38:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81635>

Caroline, I need to take some lessons from you for that! I am a real introvert in person...it's hard for me to meet people. I was thinking about that on my drive home last night. I might have been able to meet some men if I wasn't so awkward. Not to mention still gun shy from my last disasterous relationship. I have never actually 'dated' anyone I did not already know as a friend, co-worker, whatever. I can't even imagine internet dating. I am of the opinion that if it is meant to happen it will....although the more I can help by putting myself out there the better, I guess.

It was a fun night...I met up with a girl I know who was buying my extra ticket, but eventually was just on my own. It was very crowded so dancing anywhere was fine and I was able to stay away from the packed dance floor. I left shortly before 2am. He was still going, but my feet were a bit sore and I was ready to leave. The 'drunk lunks' were getting harder to avoid and I am short and apparently not easily seen through beer goggles! I got jostled around a bit too much there at the end.

Diane, I like a lot of stuff, but this was a DJ that plays here in the summer at the Shambhala festival and is very well known and popular here. I heard it originally through my brother's girlfriend who is into that kind of music and discovered that I like a lot of it, especially for dancing. Now I know why there are so many people using drugs at 'rave' type events though...it would be almost impossible to dance as long as they do without some kind of help. It's an odd experience going to a bar sober....you realize there are not many other people there in the same state of mind!

Well I was hoping for a nice relaxing day off, but I just got called to work. Hopefully a short and sweet one and I can come home and putter. The sun is shining and with any luck it will kill off the last of the snow in the yard.

I stopped the progesterone yesterday, and today I got my period. In retrospect I maybe should have taken it one more day as it is day 28 today. I'm ok with that, just happy it's more normal! Now I have to decide whether to start as early this time. I started day 12, and wonder if I should push it back to day 14. Last time I tried to do that I bled before I could start the progesterone. Have to think about it some more.

### caroline — 2013-03-24T22:32:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81646>

Krista - I am very shy too but trying to do better. For me - what has made a huge difference is feeling a lot better about myself now that I have my weight under control. It just gives you so much more self confidence ...... but also - the last few years I really gravitate toward positive, motivated, kind people. That does a world of good to be around this kind of energy .....I don't have time for anything else these days.

### endless — 2013-03-25T17:13:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81695>

Caroline, I think that is what is making it harder for me right now is my weight gain/bloating from this BHRT....I feel very self conscious about my midsection and can't (won't) wear anything that shows it. I think that once that goes away I will be less reticent. I am still a few pounds heavier than my Ideal, but it's the puffy tummy that is making me reclusive.

So....I had great intentions for a 'me' day yesterday, but ended up going to work for a bit. While I was out I hit up the fish bus for some sockeye lox, so the outing was worth it. I came home and hit the trail for my run....but it was not without it's interruptions. I was stopped by an older gentleman who politely asked me if I could come into the hall where the AGM of our local co-op store was going on and sign something because they were short one member for a quorum or something. Too funny...I love rural living. So my dog and I went into the hall and signed so they could get on with the business at hand.

THEN my dog decided to head off by herself across the field to the river for a drink. I kept on going, confidant that she would catch up like she usually does. This time she was looking for me in the wrong direction, then got distracted by another person and dog and panicked because she couldn't find me. Since she's deaf, I couldn't call her, so all I could do was watch her scurrying around and then start heading home. So, I aborted my run and went to rescue her.

We stopped at the bridge for a drink (dog, not me) and I decided to test out the water. I can't believe I was thinking of getting in there, it is REALLY cold! I went in up to my knees, and staying in for 30 seconds took everything I had. My feet started aching immediately. I waited a bit and did it again, but that was all I could manage. Maybe I will try it later in my cycle...I'm thinking of what Gretchen said about CT being harder when progesterone is low.

I had a 'planned' cheat dinner last night. I've been off cheese for the most part for awhile now, so I picked up a gluten free pizza crust and made a pizza for dinner with the tomato sauce I made from my garden, some organic mozza, and veggies. Also had 2 glasses of red wine. I enjoyed it immensely and now I think I am good to go without anything cheesy-tomatoey for a good while. Was probably partly because of my period, I tend to crave that 'comfort food' then.

Today I will hit the gym if schedule allows, get my tax stuff to the accountant, and hopefully get some sun/grounding. Another sunny day!

### endless — 2013-03-26T03:15:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81800>

Well I discovered a new 'type' at the gym today, but kind of an odd one.....there was a lovely young woman there who I noticed because she was casually gliding around the weight room, stopping occasionally to pose...er..I mean stretch....in front of various mirrors. She was so obviously admiring herself that it was funny to watch. Didn't pick up a weight the entire time. It was pretty funny. Also funny was that while her shape was nice, it was obviously mostly due to genetics and diet, not fitness. She was very slim waisted (no excess cortisol) but had a booty on her like J-Lo (that she had proudly clad in tight sweats with 'vegan' blazoned across the top of her ass). I'm thinking lots of estrogen from all the soy in her diet. Top half was thin and pale, like most vegans I know. I swear, people watching at the gym is becoming the most entertaining part of my day!

Of course, I probably provide a lot of entertainment value myself, hauling up my yoga capris that have slid below my belly flab every time I do a running interval....

### endless — 2013-03-26T19:49:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81875>

Another sunny day I'm appreciating each one, because I know that the rainy spring days are on the way. If it's warm enough I will think about doing some more yard work.

Had a slightly disturbing thing happen....I have been living in this rental for 1 1/2 years now, and for the first time since 2008 I have been feeling secure with my living space. I moved 7 times between April 2008 and July 2011. This place has been a dream come true, and I've made no bones about it to my landlords that as long as they are happy with me I am considering this a long term situation. Now I've been informed that my neighbors next door want to buy the piece of land that my house is on, and I feel pretty confidant that if they do, my time here will be over. I don't know what is going to happen for sure, but I am feeling a little less secure than I was. It is SO HARD to find an affordable rental for one person in my area, especially with my critters. I'm really hoping this sale does not happen, for my sake. It's frustrating to be in this situation, and being totally dependant on others for my living situation. Sucks to not be able to afford to buy a place to live.

I'm trying not to worry about it, but it's tough.

### diane — 2013-03-26T20:35:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81881>

Krista I hope it works out for you! Maybe the neighbours will buy it and still let you live there?

### caroline — 2013-03-26T23:05:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81893>

I understand your situation completely. It is so much harder when you are on your own. I am sending you positive thoughts and some quantum energy....

### endless — 2013-03-27T01:39:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-81910>

Thanks, Caroline....quantum energy gladly accepted.

Diane, that would be great, but I just instinctively feel that they would rather have me out. We live very rurally, and if they buy this piece and boot me out, they have complete privacy, whereas now they are as close to me as if we lived in a suburb or something. I don't really blame them for wanting that, it just sucks for me. BUT, on the other hand, maybe they would appreciate the extra rental income...I have to stay positive.

Finally got my tax stuff to the accountant today....after getting everything ready it became obvious to me that I have to ask for a raise in my mileage. I need about $2000 more per year to cover all my fuel, insurance and repairs. That is only a 5 cent per km increase, but I am still nervous about asking.

NO exercise today....I took the usual doggie walk and then spent a lot of time outside grounding in the sun. Might have burnt my face again, doh! I will do my stretchnig routine tonight but will save the strenuous stuff for another day.
