### notsoperfick — 2015-04-14T15:28:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-161175>

Endless that's so thoughtful of you to respect someone's last place on earth and to mark it joyfully. I despair at the scenes of fatal accidents when people put up flowers and mementoes year after year, their loved one never lived there, they should celebrate and remember in a place they lived, seems a much more relevant thing to do to me.

I hope your cat is just momentarily entangled somewhere. I have a cat who took to leaving home every time there was something she didn't like, such as my daughter's dog coming to stay. Longest I didn't see her was nearly 3 days and then she came strolling up, hungry but otherwise fine. My Bengal on the other hand is a real homebody, he doesn't like the dog much either but gets as far as the garden across the road and then calls when he hears either of us outside, which immediately alerts the dog as to his whereabouts. I'm hoping he is back with you soon, Elvis didn't die he just went home

### endless — 2015-04-17T06:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-161486>

Thank you both. I find myself getting anxious each time I drive in to the driveway, hoping he will be back but so far he is still gone.  Realistically I doubt he will be back, he was not a young adventurous guy, and never missed breakfast if he could help it. I miss him so much...

Got some results for my last saliva hormone test today but I can't get the damn photo to upload because I still don't know how to use this stupid Mac! I don't know how to change the size of the file. Grrr.

### caroline — 2015-04-18T03:27:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-161581>

So sorry Ktista that your friend is missing .......maybe Elvis just went walk about? xoxo

### endless — 2015-04-22T03:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162006>

No, I don't think so Audrey, although if he shows up I will of course be overjoyed....he and I were pretty tight and not only is is out of character for him, I don't 'feel' him anymore, if you know what I mean.
In spite of that his presence is everywhere here and I take comfort in that at least.

I need some help....I suspect that after a lifetime (so far) of great digestion I have developed GERD or silent reflux (maybe they are the same...). I've had several episodes of strange symptoms that feel like I am having an asthma or even a heart attack, very disconcerting. Oddly, they have ALL happened when driving over mountain passes over 5,000ft, not that I think that could have anything to do with it really....but on longer drives. I'm wondering if my body position could have anything to do with it. I start to feel like I have pressure on my throat and chest and breathing is difficult, and then my heart starts to pound and I feel shaky. That part could be anxiety triggered by the first part though., since it's always happened in the middle of nowhere where there is no cell service or help if I should need it.

Also coupled with that is a more or less constant uncomfortable feeling in my upper abdomen/sternum area. It's frustrating! I'm going to start with the assumption that for some reason I have low stomach acid and try taking HCL with meals to see if it helps at all. I do know that grain and sugar give me heartburn, but it's happening with everything lately.

### caroline — 2015-04-22T04:25:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162013>

gall stones?

Please take care....xo

### endless — 2015-04-23T03:36:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162151>

So obviously I need to fashion a better cover for my new CT tank....went to get in and there was a dead mouse in there... I had to empty and refill instead. I'll have to get a piece of 3/4 inch plywood that I can cut to shape. It'll keep the mosquitos from breeding in there too.

Spent all day in the garden yesterday hoeing up raised beds...I've got blisters on my hands and a sunburn....I am not at the place where I don't burn yet. I crave the sun so much that I couldn't bear to get out of it, even as I felt myself burning. It's from the winter of deprivation...next winter I will go back to tanning and won't be so desperate for it in the spring!

### caroline — 2015-04-23T06:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162167>

Hi Krista ....How are you feeling?

You were hoeing naked - right??? xo

You are such a capable young woman ....when are the yurts going up?????

### endless — 2015-04-25T03:37:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-162360>

No, not naked this time because my mother and stepfather were stopping by and because my skin needs a gradual intro to the sun....

I am still wanting to build a little guest room outside, or maybe find an older travel trailer to use. I have a good spot to put it and it would be much easier to have guests if they didn't have to sleep on the living room floor! Mind you, soon I'll be setting up my sleeping porch outside again and guests can have my bed.

Still having the strange symptoms...but I'm trying some things to see if anything helps, including sipping a/c vinegar in water with meals.

### endless — 2015-05-16T06:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164408>

Man, I just can't seem to get over here very often....

Starting where I left off though, I got my CT in this morning outside in the sun in my lovely new CT corner (which I STILL can't figure out how to post a pic of here from this Mac, since I don't know how to resize photos ) but I posted it on FB in the quantum group if anyone is interested. Followed up with sitting naked in the sun eating my breakfast of salmon row and avocado and followed by an hour of naked gardening! My neighbours are in Hawaii so I took advantage and let it all hang out. Usually I at least have a sarong on but today I went for the full monty. Covered up after that to avoid too much sun, I still burn if I overdo it.

Productive day otherwise, too. Changed the oil in my snowblower (finally) and my rototiller, filled my gas cans, fixed my lawnmower, planted some of the greenhouse and wired on the raspberry supports. Various other chores filling in the gaps. I still have at least half of the garden to plant, too, but got flowers planted last week so the porch looks nice at least. Bought myself a beautiful Mandarin Orange Azalea bush, i memory of my big orange kitty Elvis who is no longer physically with me. It's over by my CT spot so I can see it while I'm in the tub.

I went for a stress test at the hospital yesterday to check out my heart. No problems, at least as far as that test would show. The doc thinks that I am just one of those people who is hyper aware and can feel the extra beats that my heart throws off more than some other people do. I never thought about it, but apparently there's quite a variation of how aware of their body different people are. Anyway, I still think my problem is digestive in nature because it seems a lot better since I've been taking HCL with meals. I'm still not sure WHY it's happening but working on that.

I'm going to be in bed early tonight....I've had super early mornings all week despite wanting to 'sleep in' till 7 or so. Was up at 3am yesterday for a chimney fire, and by the time I got home it was 5:30 so no point in going back to sleep. My first thought was 'who the hell is burning their wood stove in the middle of May'? It's not even cold at night anymore.....

### caroline — 2015-05-16T11:20:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164415>

Hi Krista .... You really had a big day! Have to check out your C/T pic.

Elvis has left the building?
xoxo

### endless — 2015-05-17T04:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164494>

Hi Caroline,
Yes, sadly Elvis has left the building. I really miss him and yet I distinctly feel his presence around here still so it's bittersweet.

Got cabbages, kale, brussels sprouts and kohlrabi planted today, plus the peppers are in the greenhouse ready to go in. Tomorrow, schedule permitting, I will plant some bush beans and greens, and prep my tomato bed. I have a section I was going to grow a cover crop of buckwheat on, but the prediction is for a hot, dry summer and so I am thinking I might just cover it instead to save water. I bought some planting bags to plant my squash plants in and I can just set them on top of the covered area. And then of course I will still have all the mulching to do......good thing it's a healthy hobby. Grounded and in the sun. My back suffers a bit though.

I FINALLY saw a magnetico on Craigslist, but it's on the coast. If they reply I will see if they are willing to package it for shipping because it's a really good deal. Can't really afford it but it might be my only chance to get one. I'll leave it up to the universe...if I'm meant to have it I will.

My 11 year old nephew is spending the night tonight. Currently hanging out with my snake and playing on his computer. Severe technology addiction! I'm going to try and get him to play a board game or something after dinner, but I don't think I'll be awake long. I've been out on emergency medical calls every morning this week and my sleep has suffered.

You are quite the farmer! I am very ,very impressed. Have you always enjoyed planting? Do you trade your stuff for stuff you don't have? Are you quite self sufficient? pickles etc.?

Hi Inger!

I sure hope I reach the stage where I don't burn at some point! I got quite red on my chest and arms yesterday because I was in the sun for 30 minutes in the cold tub, then another 3 hours or so in the garden. Today I used an umbrella for shade to let my skin recover.

### WereBear — 2015-05-20T03:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-164754>

endless, do you take supplemental Vitamin D3? It has helped me tremendously, and is supposed to increase one's sun exposure without burning.

Then again, I'm over forty.. when our natural ability to produce it with sun starts dropping off, I understand?

### endless — 2015-05-24T18:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165209>

Hi WereBear,
I don't anymore....I did for while and my labs didn't budge upwards at all, even at 16,000 IU a day at one point! I tried a tanning bed instead and up went my blood levels....so I decided to stop the supplemental D3. I am 46, so I guess if that's true I should be seeing a natural decline. Then again, the most nut-brown, leathery people I see are usually over 60.

I also think it's partly about honouring my northern european heritage (all VERY white people!) and taking it slow. I don't use sunscreen anymore so I'm having to become more conscious of when my skin has had enough sun. This is the first spring that I'm really trying to stick to a routine (work permitting) of getting outside at sunrise and getting my CT and breakfast in in the early morning sun, and hopefully some naked gardening before the sun gets too intense. I need one of those hats with an umbrella sticking out of the top.

I am so happy for you that you can do naked gardening! I have a similar very Northern European genetics; British Isles, Scandinavia, and some rumored Dutch for seasoning.

### WereBear — 2015-05-25T01:55:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165249>

### caroline — 2015-05-25T02:20:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165250>

I also used to always burn and not tan \[European heritage too\].... now haven't in four years.
People always tell me now that I am so lucky to have olive skin!

I think the biggest part of it is C/T .....and epi paleo of course!

### endless — 2015-05-25T04:56:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165258>

I am definitely darker than I have been in years past by this time.....albeit with a slight reddish tone. I do pink up, especially on my chest and face, and then it fades into tan. I rarely to never peel. The funny thing is, year round, people are always asking me if I have been on vacation because they think I have a tan! I must glow....

I'm on the fence about skin damage too Nicole, my mom lives in Mexico 9 months out of the year and her skin is getting that thick, leathery, 'elephant skin' look. Mind you, she is not doing CT or eating epipaleo, but she lives at sea level and has other things going for her.

Naked gardening has been awesome, but my neighbours will be back from their holiday any day now and I will have to put something on. I will still CT and sit out afterwards in the buff because I can hide behind my house, but I'll have to throw on a wrap in the garden.

### nicld — 2015-05-25T14:44:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165284>

My skin does the same thing, pinks up, especially on the chest. When I am warm it gets really pink. I even had to buy a darker shade of foundation and that has NEVER happened. Even when I went to Costa rica and got a little too much sun I really did not peel from the light burn but the skin did slough off. Now the hubby peeled from his burn and it was so gross.

I have one area that I can lay out nude too but when doing anything else I need to be covered up. Next place, walking around nude is going to be one of the biggest factors in picking the house, besides no towers near by and meter not on the house.

### endless — 2015-05-29T20:13:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-165852>

Neighbors are home....bummer. It's coinciding with some cooler rainy weather though so I'm not having the desire to get out there naked in the garden anyway.

Having a really hard time getting my CT tank cold now....I bought some blocks of ice but I would need to add several to get the temp below 60. Yesterday it was 70, and I put in one block and also 6 two litre pop bottles filled with ice, and that kept it at 60 for the 30 minutes I was in. I'd probably need a couple more blocks to get to 50. Too much money....my CT will have to be at 60 for the hot season!

I'm having trouble keeping all the balls in the air at once lately. Between work and life in general being busy, I never seem to get a full 'optimal' day in. I can have a great morning with CT, sun, seafood, etc and my day will go sideways and I end up coming home late from work and being too hungry to fast till morning so I am eating too late and getting light at the wrong time.I haven't managed to eat earlier than 8pm for at least a week. It's annoying! I need to order another pair of UVEX (or two) for starters...mine are old and trashed. Also really need to get something similar for when I am not at home. And order some more blue blocking film for my car instrument display. No wonder I keep putting off renewing my silver membership....every month I have too many other related expenses! Can't even buy a lottery ticket because I work in the industry....

Speaking of expenses, I have COMMITTED to buying the 20 gauss magnetic I found on Craigslist! Luckily the person selling is willing to wait for me to be able to arrange a couple days off to go get it, it's a days drive away. I was on the fence because it is a size smaller than my mattress, but after calling the company and speaking to Dr. Bonlie's wife about it I am going ahead. If and when I end up sharing the bed with someone else I will need to get the proper size, but for just me it'll be fine and it's 1/3 the price so I can't pass it up. That's an unexpected expense I don't mind!

### endless — 2015-06-02T20:51:32+0300

If there was a lottery of some kind on the site with silver or gold memberships given away I would be playing every day!

### endless — 2015-06-04T07:09:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-166492>

My neck is really buggered.  Sunday I rototilled a 1000sq/ft section of my garden (after digging up a bunch of raspberry shoots that were in it), shovelled and hauled 10 loads of horse shit from my neighbour's place to my garden, spread it and covered the section with 6ml black plastic. I woke up the next day in excruciating pain. Not sure what happened. I went to the chiro yesterday afternoon but it's still really inflamed. It even hurts when I swallow. Thankfully I had a slow day today and have been resting and icing it. Wish I had the magnetic already!

It's still raining.....going on a week straight, basically. It's warm out, still, but I'm ready for the sun to come back.

### endless — 2015-06-05T06:21:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-166649>

I did that last night before bed, then iced it again....had a horrible night because moving once I lay down was almost impossible! I was wishing I had a rope hanging from the ceiling to haul myself up with...lol
Today it's migrated over to the left side and now feels like my first rib is out. I can't yawn or sneeze....it's bizarre. My body stops the process before the part where I'd have to fill my lungs completely. Of course I had to deinstall a 70 pound lottery ticket dispenser at the casino today too. So it's back to the chiropractor again in the morning and hopefully he can fix me up for the weekend. I have to mow my lawn, it's gone crazy after all this rain.

I've been really enjoying some of the conversations in the quantum group over on FB, but I think it's starting to deteriorate....maybe getting too big or something. It's too bad because it's really interesting how many tangents one conversation can go off on, and I learn a lot. I wish it was that lively over here!

### endless — 2015-06-06T09:38:55+0300

Neck is getting better, after another chiro visit this morning and CT today. I'm buying blocks of ice and one block and my 6 frozen 2L pop bottles keep the tank at 56-58 for the 30-40 minutes I need it to. I need to figure out how to make my own blocks....

### prAna303 — 2015-06-06T11:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-166779>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=166649)
>
> I've been really enjoying some of the conversations in the quantum group over on FB, but I think it's starting to deteriorate....maybe getting too big or something. It's too bad because it's really interesting how many tangents one conversation can go off on, and I learn a lot. I wish it was that lively over here!

Since i do not use Facebook i do not know whats going on there but i have an idea.

Perhaps the ideas spread fast but what about thought and application? Also like many times before and it is speeding up everywhere, it goes heywire. Today we are so into understanding that everyone gets into it like crazy but forget to use it in real life. Instead we discuss things on levels that sometimes is to nerdy, some can talk math but others see it in pictures, others just live it out loud. We are so much searchers today, we want to live forever and be king of the throne but forget to use the sience we learn.

On Facebook you get a flamethrower kind of discussions, it is fast, instant and smack up your face. On a forum it is slower, more time to think, reflect. In real life it is thinking and doing at the same time. I tend to like forums better, like writing a real letter but with modern tools. In digital world it is easy to drown in the buzz, one can think a lot but forget to act.

> Also, some folks were critical of the lighter stuff here .... which, personally, I learn a lot of life lessons from.

Let them be critical, we need those who sometimes kick back, barefeet in the sun with a straw hanging from your lip. The deep science need deep mystics and weirdos who do not forget the big thing, to live.

What i done from november of last year is to use application more, sure i read some things in ones and zeros land but i sort it big time. I tend to not write as much, i can not use words for what i feel now, to narrow bandwith. Digging in books, one each day and finding it hard to like stuff there also. The only thing that gives me kicks now is humans in natural states, grass in morning sun and sea at afternoon.

There will always be a risk that Quantum things become the new Paleo but the science will still be there when the wind of trend has blown past it... Sometimes we just need some time to digest the information just like the light.

### endless — 2015-06-08T06:33:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-167007>

Interesting day. For one thing, I actually turned my phone OFF all day, for the first time in over a year. I had the day off, but because my relief worker is still fairly new I generally keep tabs on what's going on. Today I did not an it was awesome!
I just finished a 4 week experience of micro-dosing with iboga root. A family member became involved with a couple who have been working with plant medicines for several years (mostly treating addiction, which is what my family member was seeking help with). In the course of the (successful) treatment my family member decided that this is something he feels called to do as well and is now in training to help facilitate plant medicine treatments and ceremonies. I don't have addiction problems, but I do believe strongly in the ability of plant medicines to help us access areas of our subconscious and our spirits that are generally 'off-limits', and so participated in this trial for my own self exploration and growth and to try and work on some phobias I would like to get rid of. I was helpful to him as well for 'practice' before working on clients. I really enjoyed the connection with the plant medicine....setting intentions every time I ingested it, noticing what was coming up in dreams and during the day....it was a gentle corrective process, compared to the 'flood dosing' used with substance addiction that can be quite intense. Using something like that with intention is so wildly different than doing it recreationally, which I never got into.
Today, three of us who had been involved in this 4 weeks of exploration closed by sitting in ceremony and taking another plant medicine (a cactus called Peruvian Torch). I am a total coward when it comes to mind altering substances...I didn't even do acid in high school (I realize that neither did a lot of people, but I was in the group that did.) so wasn't sure what to expect. I was pleasantly surprised and we had a very pleasant day with waves of ...ultra-awareness, I guess. It was a nice way to close the experience and make me more at ease with the process....something I am working towards with the goal of participating in an ayahuasca ceremony, possibly even this year. Still nervous about that but know it would be immensely healing.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-06-08T07:11:49+0300

Ugh, it has been SO HOT here lately, I feel like my core is overheated. Too busy to CT for the last 3 days too so that hasn't helped. I have been sleeping outside and still too warm. I'm going to be up early, since it is getting light at 4:30am now, and jump in the CT tub early tomorrow before it gets hot.

Reading Wim Hof's book right now. It's interesting but he is not really a writer. I keep feeling like I've missed something, he will be discussing an event or time and the next chapter is on to something else, leaving me with a lot of questions about the previous one. I want to know HOW and WHY he got into doing the cold stuff in the beginning, and so far there is no satisfactory explanation of that.

### endless — 2015-06-13T23:33:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-167993>

Had a good turnout last night to our 'smart meter resistance' meeting..... we are one of the last areas in the province to get them and there have been bullying tactics used by the company. Lots of people unsure what to do. After showing some clips from Resonance and Take Back Your Power, we heard from a retired electrician/activist about how to go about not giving consent, and from a guy in the okanagan who is involved in resisting the swaps there. Overall pretty good. I am still torn between registering for 'RF Off', and trying to just go for full on refusal of the swap. I'm risking disconnection if I refuse, and I don't have a backup to protect my two freezers, so I have to choose carefully. The guy from Kelowna said that in that area, people who have posted notices and locks on their meters are not being bothered, but the readers here are telling people that if the meters are locked the power will be disconnected. How much are they bluffing?

### endless — 2015-06-21T06:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-169117>

Thank you WereBear! I'm still not sure what I'm going to do.

Well, it's finally happened....I've gone off chicken. Back in 2012 I remember Jack saying he never ate poultry anymore except for wild duck, and while a bunch of people immediately followed suit, I didn't because I had good quality local chicken in my freezer. I don't eat a LOT of it, maybe once every 2-3 weeks. But the last 2 times I have found it distasteful. I have changed SOME stuff just because Jack did, but overall I like to see how things apply to my own situation first. I guess I'll be giving the remainder of my chicken to my brother's household. Good thing I still have salmon and elk in the freezer!

The pre-cruise bash must be in full swing by now....I'm sure everyone will have a great time on the trip. I would like to do something like that with this group of people, but honestly cruise ships have never been my idea of a good time. I'd rather camp on a beach.

### endless — 2015-06-21T17:51:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-169169>

Well, day one of trying my UFO light. I broke down and bought one online because they seem to be getting scarce and I wanted to have it around. I decided to use it on my abdomen today, so I just sat in a kitchen chair with it balanced on it's edge on my legs, about 8 inches away from my abdomen. That way the fan in the back is unobstructed. Did 15 minutes (fits in to the routine well, I can do my oil pulling at the same time..) and we will see if I notice anything! I might alternate, do my lower back tomorrow.

### WereBear — 2015-06-21T17:53:40+0300

### endless — 2015-06-24T03:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-169414>

Well, nothing earth shattering to report after two UFO sessions....I skipped a day in between so did my lower back this morning. Had to skip CT this morning too...sacrificed it to taking the dogs for a sunrise walk instead. At least I got my morning sunlight in. Having trouble with breakfast lately...I'm inclined to skip it but unless I get more organized I dare not because then I make shitty choices later in the day when I'm unprepared. This morning I went for the high DHA option and made a smoothie of raw whole herring with parsley from the garden, dulse powder and lemon juice. Lunch of raw cherries newly in season and dinner was raw elk, avocado and steamed broccoli with lots of butter. I HAVE to find another salmon roe source, really been missing it.

### endless — 2015-07-03T08:17:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-170490>

So, interestingly enough, I got a taste of what getting cut off from power would entail...we had a kick ass storm while I was away for 3 days and my freezers were fine. Go figure.

I just drove 1400km to buy and pick up my used 20 gauss magnetico! It was a whirlwind trip and I am bagged from all the driving, but I did get an ocean swim and a sushi meal out of it so all good. I am slightly concerned about the ability of my futon bed frame to hold up under the weight, but already working on solutions. Last night was my first night on it, and I honestly had a hard time getting to sleep and felt like I spent a lot of the night awake, but that might have been a combination of anxiety about my bed frame and the full moon. Reserving judgement for at least 6 weeks. It's too small for my bed but the area not covered is really easy for me to avoid.

### Psyche — 2015-07-03T14:54:03+0300

I wanted to send you a note of encouragement and tell you to hang in there. You are making a ton of progress. The harder you work for something the more value it has.

### endless — 2015-07-04T23:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-170835>

Thanks Psyche.

My sleep last night was great, so I think I am adapting to the magnetico fairly well. Still have to consider upgrading the futon bed frame with additional 1x4 slats, and a few extra centre supports. It's fine for me but if I give up my bed to a heavier guest there could be a disaster......
It is very hot here right now and I am recovering from heatstroke, suffered during work 2 days ago. I had to wear jeans and my steel toe boots that day and it did me in, but 3pm I had to quickly bail on what I was doing and rush home and into a cold shower. I'd stopped sweating and my heart was pounding. Scary experience and left me with nausea and a headache for the following day. This morning I used all the ice and frozen jugs I had to get the damn CT tub cold, and still only managed 60F....felt good though. Even the river is warmer than that now, so I've been craving colder water.
Another 100F day and more coming up....I'm not going into the sun till at least 7pm!

Garden is in full production now...I ate spinach, arugula, cukes, onions and tomatoes out of it last night for dinner, with salmon.

### endless — 2015-07-04T23:39:13+0300

### endless — 2015-07-07T00:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-171188>

Awesome day for a Monday! I didn't have to leave for work right away, so had time to do my morning routine: outside at sunrise, 15 minutes oil pulling and UFO light while prepping CT tank, 40 minutes CT (well, kinda.....still struggling to get the water temp down. 60F was all I could get.), breakfast outside while naked sunbathing. My neighbours were both gone so I just stayed naked for a couple more hours and did some work in my garden. Other than a slight mishap involving a nipple and some stinging nettle it was great to be able to work nude.
Got all my garlic harvested, hauled a couple loads of manure to spread on the soil, fed and watered my compost bins and refilled the dog's wading pool after using the yucky old water on the raspberries.

I'm definitely noticing better sleep with the magnetico. I've been wanting to sleep outside but if I do that I will be missing sleeping on it I think. Was thinking about storing water on it, but I think I'll stick with using the ceramic block magnet I've been using, because I can keep it in the fridge and on the magnet at the same time.

### fitness@home — 2015-07-07T02:27:37+0300

Chiller is set to turn on at 2 pm and turn off at 6 pm. I get in after work. I should add 30 minutes in the morning too.

We've had the Magnetico for a good month now. Sleep is better but still could improve. In bed at 8:30 pm and up at 4:00 am. We didnt have any detox symptoms at all.

I got my bed frame reinforced today.....I was a little concerned about the extra weight because it is just a futon frame with 1x4 slats and only two small supports in the middle. I doubled the number of slats and added 4 2x4 supports to the centre rail, and now I feel ok about sleeping on it. Not to mention having bigger, heavier guests sleep on it. I usually give up my bed to guests since I don't have a guest room. I'd hate to have someone visiting and have the bed collapse on the floor.....

It was extremely frustrating work because I needed a metric hex key which I didn't have, and I stubbed my toe on a section of Magnetico and broke the nail halfway down which really f\*%king hurt! But it's done. Even gave the room a good smudge with sage afterwards to clear all the negative\]ity I was generating...lol

Not sire if anyone remembers but I was debating whether to buy this 20g Magnetico because it was a double and my bed is a queen. Heres my frame with the upgrades, and how much is covered by the magnetico. I have had no problem staying away from the edges.

### endless — 2015-07-15T20:41:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-172029>

I'd like to see that, Audrey.
I actually have a bamboo fibre mattress topper now that works well for most of the year, but this extra hot summer requires more.

My garden is exploding! I'm picking long english cucumbers daily, and flogging lettuce and kale off on all my family. We haven't even gotten to the carrots and beets yet, but some are ready in 'baby' form. Bush beans are up next...I am enjoying eating some of each thing as it comes. and my family is enjoying the rest.

### caroline — 2015-07-16T00:32:24+0300

### endless — 2015-07-24T08:08:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-172871>

Not sure Audrey...what product are you looking at exactly? The futon? All looks like pretty good stuff!

Nights are cooler lately and I am enjoying that because as much as I enjoy sleeping outside normally, I want to sleep inside on my magnetico! Now it cools down nicely in the house once it gets dark.

Started a jar of fermented salsa today, all from the garden(well, except the lemon juice). I finally have tomatoes ripening along with the jalapeños and onions. Garlic is all harvested and curing. I need to clean a few spots and replant cilantro, spinach and arugula, and plant some fennel seeds for fall bulbs. I've got HUGE zucchini plants and NO zucchinis..... Blossom end rot. Caused by calcium deficiency and uneven watering. Now I know. Tomorrow I have to attack the aphids on my brussels sprouts, and spray all the cucurbits with milk again to keep the powder mildew to a dull roar. My early cabbage is ready so next chance i get will put on a batch of kraut, or maybe half kraut, half cortido.
The raspberries are pretty much done already. Amazing how early everything is this year. I have a beautiful ripe cantaloupe I picked today, going to wait till my dad arrives on Saturday to share it with him. I have a few more coming that will probably go to my brother's family so I will enjoy this one for sure!

I need to order some more UVEX, and possibly something to use out of the house. I can't keep getting up in the night for emergency calls without using something, the lights in the fire hall are really bright.

### caroline — 2015-07-25T04:58:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-172920>

I need to learn how to grow stuff ....... I love it when you talk about your garden!

We have a huge old glass aquarium - it seems to me that it could be put outside and used to start/grow things?

Krista - do you grow mushrooms?

### endless — 2015-07-26T08:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-173029>

Well, I've only grown a certain variety...  But I could grow edible ones too I'm sure.

If I had a glass aquarium I'd make a terrarium out of it and grow some cool tropical plants.

### endless — 2015-07-31T22:15:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-173638>

Wow, lots going on around here this week. Summer is always busy. The 'shift' has happened though, nights are cooler, and my CT tank is colder out of the tap, finally! 54F this morning, without ice.
I had the opportunity to participate in some healing ceremonies too, which are still ongoing. Will report back later....

### endless — 2015-08-02T22:07:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-173807>

I think I finally managed it.....consistant am full body sun seems to be working finally. No more sunburn! My skin is darker than it has ever been, including belly, breasts and upper legs. I will miss it come winter, that's for sure. Might need to continue short tanning sessions.

I've been doing the UFO on my belly about 3x week for 15 minutes in the morning too. Feels great, I love the way the warmth penetrates. Still haven't tried nicotine. Not sure I will.

The garden is in full on production, I have to get started making kraut with some of this cabbage. Ate my very own home grown honeydew melon last week, amazing! I have 3 more in there that will be shared out to the family. I'm finding myself quite satisfied with a single meal of each summer fruit as they pass through. I have a small stash of raspberries and blueberries in the freezer that I will use over the winter for making contributions to family dinners etc.

Disconnecting a bit from the FB group. Lots of amazing information flying around there, fast and furious, but it's too hard to keep up without spending WAY more time on there than I want to. Better to spend it reading all these books I have accumulated, now that the tv is dormant. I think by the end of this week I will feel even more inclined to step back. As exciting as all this is, I have other things going on in my life that I want to spend time on too. Easy to get caught up in the panic about 'keeping up' with the blogs and the discussions. It causes stress. I admire Jack's brilliance and will never totally understand all of what he writes, so I will plod along here at my own pace and be content with that for now.

### nicld — 2015-08-04T16:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-173912>

I have 8 cabbage plants that will be used to make sauerkraut too!!! Nothing better then knowing that you grew it.

Oh and I will be trying to make some refrigerator pickles this week. My pickle cucumbers are getting ripe.

### endless — 2015-08-11T05:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-174399>

I just spent a weekend away, and came back to a huge list of garden chores that have to be done.....onio0ns are curing and need to be cleaned up, got to finish cleaning the garlic, pick and clean jalapeños to make lacto-fermented hot sauce, pick and roast eggplants, and of course get started on the kraut. The red cabbage is for sure ready to use now, so will start with those. I add beets and small amount of onion and apple to that. I made a jar of green cabbage/juniper berry/peppercorn and one of green cabbage/dillweed last week. With the late cabbage I might make some cortido and some more variations of basic kraut.

Also need to recuperate from some very un-optimal activity.....late nights full of LOUD electronic music and lots of flashing lights. The music went all night till about 7am, though I went to bed by 2:30 all three nights it was tough to sleep since the bass was rattling my tent. Fun, though.

### endless — 2015-08-15T08:17:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-174716>

This is nerve wracking....we are surrounded by unconfined wildfires (at a safe distance, so far...), it's hot, dry, and windy, and the smoke is so think in the air that my eyes are burning and throat is sore. We are in no danger from the existing fires, but the biggest one in the area grew from a small spot fire to 25km sq. in 24 hours. The conditions are the same here now. Everyone is praying for rain, no more lightning, and the wind to die down. I'm gathering a few things together in a safe place just in case.....cat carriers, passport and photos. Better safe than sorry I figure. Of course I'll probably be trying to haul the Magneticos off the bed if I have to leave in a hurry. And then of course there's my CFB/CGB and my UFO light.....hmmm. Might need to buy a trailer.

I finished harvesting all my garlic and onions today....think I'm set for sulphur for the season!

### endless — 2015-08-17T03:37:15+0300

I was just concerned that we might have one start here. 26 homes were lost and 11 more properties lost other structures and equipment. The campground burned with vehicles and trailers. Lots of damage. Just hoping now that all the animals that were set free are found unharmed!

Just got off the Q&A.....I need to take a couple days off with my phone unplugged just to get though Life on the Edge! I try to read in bed for a bit but since I got the magnetico I fall asleep to fast.

### cinnamon — 2015-08-18T01:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-174974>

### endless — 2015-08-18T22:15:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175064>

Thanks! Other than that one sketchy night all was pretty safe here.

My ex is staying with me right now. It's kind of challenging and makes me realize that sharing my space is something I will have to weigh carefully if I get into a relationship that goes in that direction. I'm finding myself annoyed at how he does things in my kitchen, for example. Trying to find a balance between voicing what I want things to be like and not being a nagging bitch. He's gone all day working (although often so am I) and here in the evening so I am feeling the lack of solitude. Working on going with the flow. It's not terrible...we know each other well and I am comfortable around him, plus he helps me out with expenses while he is here. Every day I see things that remind me why we are no longer together, though.

### endless — 2015-08-20T06:41:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175190>

So Alyson gave me some homework.....after being single for 5 years (and celibate for 4 ) it's really time to get back in the saddle, so to speak. I am going to write out exactly what I want in a partner. I don't think I'll do it here, at least not yet, but maybe I will add it here at some point. I already had someone on the FB group say that I was setting myself up for failure because I've chosen to not have casual sex....maybe that's true, maybe it isn't. I have had enough casual sex in my life though, and now I want to save that connection for someone who deserves it. Just need to get my nerve up and start looking a bit.

### caroline — 2015-08-20T10:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175196>

I made a list in my journal - just accidentally ...or maybe it was incidental to what someone was asking me about. I really just wrote it off the top of my head without any prior thought at all. I had never even thought of making a list....

when I went back and read it a few months later - I was blown away ....Tony ticked every single box. How's that for magic and quantum entanglement?!

We really have to think about the things that are very important to us ...and the things that are deal breakers.
I remember at first being so agitated because I thought I shouldn't be choosing some one on looks .....but you can't be with someone you think is gross!

### endless — 2015-08-22T05:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175360>

I was thinking of you Audrey.....hopefully I have similar results!

Ugh, turning off the computer and reading tonight....tired of all the chest puffing and posturing I'm seeing in my newsfeed today. People are so weird..... Back to Life on the Edge.....

### caroline — 2015-08-22T08:21:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175363>

It seems Gretchen had a melt down today ...LOL.

The funny thing about my list - Tony did tick all the boxes ...but there is so, so much more that I hadn't given any thought to. It seems, all of a sudden , everything is in harmony ...we are in harmony and we bring out the very best in each other. Going dancing is so sensual, and he sings to me and looks at me like he has waited forever for me to show up .... and I am the same.

It is magnetism to the nth degree - I have never experienced anything like this - not even close...... and we give off something that attracts others.

I wasn't even looking for someone really ....just trying to make myself move forward. It is funny to admit this - I think I was expecting a big, tall, wonderful man like my DH or like Jack who I think the world of. But guess who shows up .... a little pommy guy - not on my radar at all! Little guys have huge benefits I want you to know!

When we are dialled in, the universe knows what we want and need it seems ....

### endless — 2015-08-23T04:30:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175416>

I'm really happy for you Audrey! I'm going to make sure my expectations don't blind me to the right person when he shows up. I've never been all about the appearance, even as a teen, so I don't think that would make me miss out but you never know!

All this talk about roe on the forum makes me realize how much I do (will) miss things that are no longer around....I've always taken it for granted that someone will show up with a couple huge freezers of whole fresh salmon on the back of a truck for me to buy each year. It might not happen this year and if it doesn't I will really miss having abundant salmon in my freezer all year!

### endless — 2015-08-24T05:24:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-175513>

I bought a used greenhouse frame today for $50. I'm trying to save money for the Quantlet and various other things, but this came up and was a good deal. It's 11 x 17ft, so big enough to grow a lot in. Who knows if next year is the year I will have to grow all my own food (and hunt/fish). This extends the growing season considerably so I figure it's a good buy. I will likely be here for several years before I can consider a move.

Garden is in full production mode. I have tomatoes coming out my ears now. Made a batch of lactofermented jalapeño hot sauce and starting a batch of fermented salsa. Setting aside the seed garlic and divvying up the rest among the family....I'm going to have a lot of winter squash and potatoes for them as well. My winter veggies will be mostly sauerkraut and kale, I think. Well, and garlic and onions of course. I actually grew some damn nice potatos this year....I will have to eat a couple here soon at least.

Used my last jar of salmon head stock yesterday....and still no word on salmon for this year. I might need to start stocking up on other options....

### endless — 2015-09-08T06:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-176715>

I really enjoyed 'summer CT' in my barely cold tub, basking in the morning sun and full body exposure in the sun afterwards.....but the cooler temps now are what make me want to CT more. The tank is dropping to better CT temps! I miss warming up in the sun (and will miss it even more when it gets colder outside) but enjoy the need to actively warm myself now. Still haven't shifted my diet much, as I am still enjoying seasonal stuff. Tonight had some local chantrelles with onions and kale from my garden and bacon, and cooked one of the red kuri squash I grew.

It occurred to me today as I was doing some cleaning in the garden that even if by some bad stroke of luck I had no seed saved and next season I had to be self sufficient, my garden could still feed me. Between the large variety of edible and medicinal 'weeds' that exist there and the volunteer plants that come up I would just have to let things grow until they are recognizable and then transplant. This year I had a lot of the usual volunteers, dill and chamomile, tons of sunflowers, but also some unusual ones, like a bunch of tomato plants! I\[m letting the cilantro and basil go to seed in there, and the peas and beans dropped some seed too. My more relaxed gardening style over the last year has some hidden benefits.

I've got enough naturally growing greens that I don't really need to plant any cultivated ones, actually. Purslane, chickweed, lamb's quarters, sorrel and sunflower sprouts make a great salad.

### Inger — 2015-09-08T16:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-176737>

Krista I love that my rain-barrel water has gotten cooler too now... feels so refreshing so dive in there every morning

I love wild gardening too...... I like that kind of gardening way more..... easier, takes way less time.. it is full of surprises.. and I have the feeling stuff grows better when things are mixed and growing wild... so cool.. and the "weeds" are often foods too
so I like to leave them there to snack on

### endless — 2015-09-10T08:28:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-176900>

Hi Inger  Yes, I have some basil that is ready and I think I will make some pesto with chickweed in it. Might as well try some weed recipes!

Dinner tonight was %100 local.....local lamb sausage, cabbage and onions from my garden fried in duck fat, and grilled carrots, zucchini and wild chantrelles. Gotta love mushroom season.

I drove a LOT today for work, and was acutely aware of the sun shining through the windows of the car into my eyes and on my skin. Whenever the angle was right I rolled the window down beside me to get the real thing. I'm going to move the daytime Blutech glasses up on the priority list, I spend SO m uch time in the car it's basically the same as being at a computer all day. I figure I can wear the glasses while driving, push them up onto my head when I am walking outside to and from my service calls, and put them on again when I have to go into jobs. I have no problem getting the am light unadulterated, although it's sometimes just that, light, not actually sun. Going to order a new pair of Uvex and maybe one of the Dewalts too, for home. Eventually will get some stylie night time blue blockers like Jack had on in his last Quantum video, but I think the day ones are more urgent.

### fitness@home — 2015-09-10T15:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-176906>

Krista, it's getting harder for me to get my morning sunlight before work (shorter days).

I am actually going to switch from stopping at the park on my way in to just parking on the top floor of the parking garage, because i will actually be able to see the sun easier from there. At the park, I have to wait for the sun to rise above the buildings and trees...

You should see me on the way home every day between 40-50 pm. Sunroof open, opening windows to collect more sun when the angle is right, and my glasses OFF (which could be a bad thing as I should be wearing them to drive). Good thing I've driven that same route a gazillion times and know it like the back of my hand...

That afternoon drive home recharges me.

P.s does anyone know why those smileys get inserted into my text? Are those keystrokes a shortcut or something?

Dawn, if you have tiny glasses they can just sit down on your nose, below your focal point, and you can just slightly tilt your head back to look at signs or whatever.

I finally bought a new pair of glasses, with plastic frames (but with a wire core). So, so much more comfortable than wire-rims. None of those pesky zapping feelings on my temples or near the nose. I only wear them to drive or when looking for something particular, like locating sardines in a new grocery store. However I don't like they way they block my peripheral vision.

So a 0 and a : make that smiley? 0: nope.
4:00
Maybe it's just you, Dawn. ;-)

### endless — 2015-10-02T06:00:21+0300

Once sun is up, outside for as long as is manageable (usually 10-15min). If I have to work right away, that's it. If not, I try to jump in the CT tank for 30 min(outside) and warm up in the sun (if any) naked before I go anywhere. i-2 mornings a week I also do the UFO on my belly for 15 min.
Just bought more black light bulbs so will be increasing the intensity in the AM.

### endless — 2015-10-05T06:38:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-178630>

Thanks Jack, good to know. I will look into that.

Audrey, no, that's not the deal. I will have to make it work there....he doesn't want his place empty for months or his daughter staying alone. It'll be a challenge!

I hope so rlee, but you know how teenage girls are....she mostly just thinks I'm odd.  I will do my best to influence her.

It occurred to me today that if I was actually following what NATURALLY grows around here instead of what I am able to cultivate, I would be keto a lot sooner.....I'm fooling myself because I have carbs in my garden but I couldn't go out in the forest right now and pick a winter squash! So....plentiful now are wild apples, rose hips, elderberries and mushrooms. I'm giving myself until the end of October to fully switch to this new revelatory plan, mostly because I put a lot of work into this garden, dammit, and I want to eat some of it! Everything is out now except the kale and broccoli and fennel anyway, but I have carrots, beets, squash and potatoes harvested. Onions and garlic I will eat year round.

QUESTION for anyone: the only 'mismatches' I am reluctant to give up are coconut products and avocado. Neither of these things grow in Canada so I suppose that theoretically I should NEVER eat them. Problem is, I love both of them and rely on them both for fat sources (palatable fat sources!) in my diet year round. How much do you think this is likely to fuck me up? I love me some coconut milk in my chaga....

### endless — 2015-10-05T08:11:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-178636>

There is an amazing list of wild plant foods in my area....I just spent hours on one website that listed a ton of thewm in a highly unpleasant format. So....if I DID decide to really go with the seasonal/local thing i9n a seriopus way, I probably could. Mind you, it would take a lot of my time to collect these foods, so it's not completely practical. But here is a list of the things I know I can readily obtain:

hazelnuts
wild mint
wild carrots
lily bulbs
wild onion
wild garlic
wild chives
fiddlehead ferns
pine nuts
walnuts
nettles
sorrel
chickweed
lamb's quarters
plantain
portulaca
clover
cattail
wild asparagus
pine nuts
pine needles
sunflowers
oregon grape
huckleberry
wild strawberry
saskatoon berry
elderberry
wild apple
mushrooms

There is more, but I am unfamiliar with them and could not identify. I think I will take a class. Ironically, several of these are 'weeds' in my garden that I pull out to make room for my seeded plants.
I'm going to start to think how I can incorporate as many of these as possible into my diet without having to quit my job to forage....I already use some of them, but if I was actually relying on them for food I would need more. I have LOTS of saskatoon berry and elderberry around. Lots of wild apples. Lots of greens, in spring. The challenge would be in finding starchier carbs. I know they exist here but don't know what/where they are. Will enlist some help to find them. There are LOTS of amazing wild mushrooms here, right now in fact. Lobsaters, chantrelles, honeys, shaggy manes, morels (in spring), pine. It's huge industry here.

### endless — 2015-10-10T05:32:40+0300

For now I have one beside my bed and one by my computer.
Frustrating day with no opportunity to CT or bask....I drove all over hell and back. Opened the window when I could to get some sun on skin action at least. When I got home I sat outside grounding feet in the grass for a half hour, helped a bit.
My chiro appt. today was much needed.....my neck was badly out from all the overhead work I've been doing lately and I've been feeling dizzy and nauseated often, especially when I look up. Which isn't ideal when I am on a ladder holding a tv.  He said he got a good adjustment so hopefully it'll be better soon, although today I felt like my equilibrium was off.

### endless — 2015-10-10T19:55:35+0300

I am becoming such a sun junkie....so precious this time of year! This morning I was grumbling in my CT tank because it was windy, and the clouds were dark, and I was thinking about how much more I enjoy CT when it involves warming up in the sun afterwards. I went in after my 30 minutes and resigned myself to warming up under my own power. Then the sun appeared! I literally leaped up and ran outside naked to suck up as much as possible....
I am now a very happy little sun worshipper, tank is recharged for now.

### endless — 2015-10-13T04:47:26+0300

Thanksgiving dinner was a success....had my mother and stepfather, brother and nephew here, plus my ex who has been staying with me. He deboned the turkey, which was interesting. Great because the bone broth was started the day before the meal and in the freezer before we even ate.  I also cooked a small local ham in the crockpot, and we had mashed potatoes and roasted veggies from the garden. I made a salad of black kale and chard with apple, walnuts and blue cheese that was really good too. Wallowing in the last seasonal carbs before I go keto for the winter.

Seriously, how many times do I have to type 'keto' before my computer stops changing it to leto?? My spellcheck must be a secret Dune aficionado......

Finally starting to crave ketosis....I have a houseguest for one more week and then I am going to get into it. ### endless — 2015-10-17T19:13:05+0300

Another brilliant blue day starting...every one is precious as the clouds and rain are on the way. Time to go jump in the tank and soak in the sun....down to 48F. I'm re-adapting as it seems to be dropping a couple degrees daily.

### endless — 2015-10-18T01:07:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-179758>

The sun is visible here now from 8:15am till about 2:30pm. The downside of living in the mountains. Today, I was lucky to spend all of that time outside with minimal clothing and barefoot. Other days I will find that entire time eaten up by work and not get any at all. Hopefully my supplementing of light will help offset those days.
Today was productive...after CT and nude basking at breakfast I got my garlic bed planted and mulched, cleaned some more spent stuff out of the garden (fed it to the horses) and brought all 25 of my winter squash inside to store under my bed. I'll be doling them out to my brother's household as needed but it's cooler here so they will store better.
Now 3pm and I feel like the day is over because the sun is gone! I could jump in the car and drive to the sunny area of the river, or get to work on my GST remittance for this quarter.... Sadly, the paperwork is going to win today.

### endless — 2015-10-21T04:23:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180044>

My long term houseguest left today. I almost feel guilty about how relieved I am! My dogs are despondent....he spent a ton of time with them. He was a big help around here too, helped me with the rent, did some cooking, etc. Hauled a bunch of horse shit for the garden. But he's my ex, and all the things that USED to bug me about him still do. I know he'd like us to be a couple again, but it's just not going to happen. I will always love him but that part of our relationship is over so I feel uncomfortable about that even though it's unspoken on both our parts. All in all not so bad to have around but today I finally have my house back!!!! Hunting down and disposing of the stuff left behind that is not ok for me to have in the house right now...rice noodles, gluten free crackers, etc. I've still got the carb mania cravings happening so minimizing exposure is important.

Happy the election is over, and I can quit annoying everyone on FB with my political posts. Happy the fucking psychopath is gone. Hopeful about the future.
My back and neck are really fucked up right now. Still getting dizzy spells and arm numbness at night, and my thoracic spine is seized up tight. I will need to seek treatment other than the chiro....the adjustments don't last. Acupuncture is my best bet to get a handle on it. I also realized today that one of the self care things that went by the wayside when my ex was here is my supplement routine. I've been off magnesium for at least 3 weeks and that's not good with muscle spasming. Tonight after fire practice I will get in a warm bath \[fuck I forgot to buy epsom salts today\] with some mag chloride and see if it helps, and take a large dose of mag bisglycinate before bed.

Haha, thanks all!
Maybe I'm a bit OCD, but when I have someone staying, I have to clean when they leave before I feel totally relaxed again. Today after work I cleaned the bathroom, and with a few more small chores it'll be all mine again. Good grief, how am I going to manage if I ever get into another relationship?? Separate homes, methinks.
I am so over this dizzy, nauseous feeling. Back to the chiro in the morning and hopefully some more relief. I think I need acupuncture, and if that doesn't help I might go see the doc.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-22T15:23:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180144>

My bet is the lack of powerful sun has a lot more to do with how you feel than anything else based upon latitude and time of the yr. Go to a tanning salon and see if it helps more than any doc. Cheaper.

### endless — 2015-10-22T23:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180186>

Will do Jack....got my tanning package all ready to go.

I DID go back to the Chiro this morning after doing some more extensive foam rolling/tennis ball stuff on my thoracic spinal area last night and he got some good adjustment of my upper neck. He also did a procedure called 'canalith repositioning' that had him moving me into different positions so my head was at different angles. He said if my eyes did some kind of movement (nystagmus?) it was likely inner ear related but I had no sign of that.Funny though, I do have all the same symptoms as the description of Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo, which is what he was checking for. CRAZY dizzy when I sat up, and felt nauseated for several hours. I'll wait it out and see what happens.

Came straight home and got naked in the garden in the full sun. Feeling better now.

### endless — 2015-10-25T07:41:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180396>

Awesome, I will have to try it...I love going out in winter for a full moon xc ski, but I'm pretty covered up of course. Summer might be better for a full body moon bath.

Looks like my mom's house survived the hurricane, with no structural damage but it made a hell of a mess and destroyed most of the roof tiles. Her town was the site of some of the worst damage . Lots of trees and power lines down and the roofing materials of a lot of places didn't survive. Saw a video today of the town and it's a mess, but cleanup has already begun. Nobody died, that's all that matters.

I'm having another wonky cycle....day 15 and I started to bleed again, heavily. Looking back over the year, I've had all relatively normal cycles, between 25-29 days long (average 27 days) except I had one 21 day cycle in September, followed by a normal one, and now only two weeks! Not sure what to think. If anything I am much more conscious of getting sun this fall than in previous years, and it's been uncommonly dry and warm. I guess I should go in for blood work again.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-25T07:53:32+0200

He does........and is right now. Naked as a jay bird in Bourbon street sitting on the porch. No one even notices as I listen to Clocks ticking.

### caroline — 2015-10-25T09:57:39+0200
