so ..... tell us more.......you haven't mentioned naked moon bathing before - have you????

I asked Tony to hook up an outdoor shower today- so we can take a cold shower before rolling around on the grass ...... he doesn't even blink an eye any more!

of course ..... we do need pics

### Inger — 2015-10-25T10:35:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180404>

I get moonlight on my face at night in the horse barn... through the open window.... I love it.... I never ever use curtains...........
Yesterday I stood nude in the terrace door sucking up the sunbeams as I talked to the ex and I did not realize that my landlords wife was in the garden with her grandchild... right in front of me... oh my god..... pretty embarrassing...lol

### endless — 2015-10-25T17:07:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180421>

Haha, Inger...we are all a bunch of exhibitionists, really..... I don't care if someone sees me naked, I am usually only modest to avoid their uncomfortable feelings about it. I was raised in a hippie commune with a lot of nudity. Even afterwards, my 'family dinners' usually involved lighting the wood sauna and everyone doing saunas and cold dunks before dinner, all together. I miss having that sauna.

Audrey, look who's talking! I want to see pics of you and your man in the outdoor shower!

Praying for an easy work day today....I have not had a morning at home all week to do 'me stuff'. This morning I will be here for the sunrise. I'm doing my oil pulling and UFO on my belly while I wait, under my black light. CT tank is likely 40F this morning, if I am still at home when the sun comes up fully in I go.

### endless — 2015-10-27T18:43:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180571>

Feeling better about my winter food supply now with my whitetail in the freezer! I need at least one alternative to seafood and this is it. Last year it was elk, this year venison. I didn't buy chickens this year, so I guess I'll be getting creative with what else I have. Managed to save the heart but the liver was damaged. Looking forward to making amazing bone broth from it too.
Never made it into the tank....had second thoughts due to this heavy bleeding. I was headed there this morning too when work interfered. I'm going to have to adjust my schedule somehow......I can't get up and do it outside in the dark. Well, I could, but I'd rather do it outside in natural light and sun if any.
Still toying with the idea of making a doc appt. The BPPV (if that's what it was) seems to be abating but this bloating, cramping, irregular cycle thing is starting to piss me off. I can't think of anything that's changed, really.....if anything I've gotten way MORE UV light heading into winter this year than I usually do. At least more with intention. Less CT, maybe.

### nicld — 2015-10-27T18:57:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180574>

That's awesome you got a deer. We are hoping to get a deer again this year and hopefully NOT with the car. There are new dead ones on the road everyday and I see that as such a shame and a waste. I hit 2 so far in the past couple of years and did not get to harvest either one of them (they ran off).

It maybe worth just getting checked out. Do you get your hormone levels checked regularly? I am NOT looking forward to the day when mine start getting jacked up due to age but to not cycle is somewhat appealing.

### endless — 2015-10-27T21:53:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180584>

Nicole, I used to be obsessive about labs, and I kind of burned myself out. Now I'm not as diligent as I probably should be. Last time I went (March) I had low E, low T, PG/E2 ratio of 218. Low DHEA. I should probably go back and see where I'm at.

Well, I got an unexpected break in my day so forced myself outside into the tub. Wasn't all that excited about it even though the outside temp is pretty good (10C/50F). Water was 45F. I only lasted 10 MINUTES! Totally wimped out. And the stupid part is, I could easily stay in longer, but I'm anticipating how much it sucks when I get out! I get SO cold, and now that there's no warm sun to warm up in I just walk around my somewhat cold house naked and uncomfortable. Putting a robe on just makes me colder. I did get out and use the UFO light on my belly and thighs while they were still extremely cold. I think I'll be doing shorter stints now that the water is colder....I have to train for next weekend so I don't wimp out at the lake with Alyson and Jena!

### endless — 2015-10-28T03:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180603>

Hit the tanning bed for the first time today as it seems that my sunny days outside are (finally) done. I've been incredibly fortunate this year to have the weather be so nice for so long! BUT...damn I love tanning. I have never been a tanner for the sake of tanning, just for vit. D or before a trip. I went for 10 minutes today after my 10 minutes of CT (which left me chilled for a long time) and it felt really good.

### endless — 2015-10-29T18:01:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180722>

Jack was in my dream last night.....it was great, some kind of member gathering and he was helping me work of my fear of flying. Obviously my subconscious knows it needs to speak to me in the form of someone who's knowledge I respect and that I will listen to....haha!

### endless — 2015-10-31T20:09:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180845>

What a dark and gloomy day......I failed miserably the 'CT outside in any weather no matter what' test.  To be fair, I didn't have time this morning, and while I was in town for work I snuck into the tanning salon for 10 minutes. No water CT today though. Took there pups for an underdressed walk in the rain this afternoon but that's it.

Was thinking about relationships.....I have a relatively enlightened and totally supportive person who I think would be happy to renew our relationship (my ex, the one who was staying with me this fall) but who I am completely not attracted to anymore. I love him and we are great friends but the nature of the relationship has changed. Too bad, because he loves Jack and listens to webinars with me when he is here. When he is not here (spends winters in India/southeast Asia, lucky guy) and he calls he will say 'is that Jack'? if he hears anything in the background.

On the other hand, the idea of dating is daunting.....there are so few prospects in my area. I'm actually quite content alone for now but I keep getting outside pressure that I shouldn't be. Not sure what to do. Am I avoiding and need to 'get back on the horse', or is it ok to enjoy my life solo until I meet someone who makes me want to change that?

### caroline — 2015-11-01T11:02:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180882>

I think your worlds will collide at the right time ..... You are content with you - that is awesome - in my opinion. What business is it of anyone else's????

Enjoy every single second of your life ........ and when lightening strikes - you will know .... make no mistake about that! It will be crystal clear when someone comes along that really rings your bell and you want to spend the rest of your life with.

no matter what ....don't settle - ever - in anything. You want it all.........the whole enchilada!

No one could have been more reluctant than me - especially at my age...... but we have inspired so many people - so many people repeat our story.

Strangers just beam at us ....for reasons I don't exactly get ...... it seems to be quantum entanglement that draws people to us.

We were at a Jazz festival over the weekend, in the wine country ....... Tony won the tickets for the festival and a really nice hotel....amazing luck once again!

It was drizzling rain but everyone was having a ball ...and a lot of bonding going on - it could have been the wine as well I suppose. An English woman, who had had more than her share of wine kept stroking my face and wanting to hear our story ..... as did others - they seem to be completely fascinated.

A friend of ours, a teacher, spent an hour telling her kindergarten kids our story ......they were so interested for some reason...

### endless — 2015-11-02T03:13:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180945>

You are my inspiration Audrey! You're right, no settling here.

I'm doing the three day fast thing. I always have trouble this time of year transitioning into winter without sliding into carb induced torpor.......I start to dread the cold and want to curl up under a blanket and eat winter squash soup and mashed potatoes. 3 days to reset and start out heavier on the seafood again.

### caroline — 2015-11-02T03:35:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-180947>

fasts are so hard when it is cold.....good luck!

### endless — 2015-11-02T20:37:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181001>

So far so good Audrey! Day two and feeling good. I did a short CT this morning despite REALLY not wanting to (I tend to get cold when I'm hungry...) and survived. I AM daydreaming about what I'm going to eat for breakfast on Wednesday......Alyson's pic of bacon and salmon on the QH group this morning was tempting but I'd probably do better with a soup made with my homemade halibut stock with some scallops, shrimp and clams. And possibly some kelp noodles. Yum. Can't wait. This is the magic of short fasts for me...not the physical benefits so much as the mental refocusing. When I am daydreaming about fish soup instead of pizza I know I'm back on the right track.

### jenaf — 2015-11-02T20:59:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181008>

so the fast is just to refocus on the seafood? short term 3 days only?

and note from the last page: my last two periods have been screwed up and that has been since i chucked out the contacts and started using UV light bulbs. my second period (about 2 wks ago) had no cramps, no signals that i was going to get it, and was super light. then day 3 (which is usually my last day) i got SUPER depressed, unmotivated, fatigued. I actually stayed home from work and laid in bed all day. So i KNOW the UV is doing something to our hormones.

i guess i should start a journal. Alyson has inspired me.

### endless — 2015-11-03T02:36:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181031>

> so the fast is just to refocus on the seafood? short term 3 days only?

Mostly....just to focus on good habits in general, too. I end up with a much greater appreciation for the things I 'should' be eating/doing after being deprived for a few days. I've always loved that about fasting, but I don't do long (10-14 day) fasts like I used to because I do not think they are healthy for me now. Back when I used to do them I felt fantastic and rarely wanted to start eating again though. I love that feeling. The shitty part about doing 3 days is that the first 3 days are the hardest.

Good to know about the cycles....mine are super weird. Interested to see what will happen next!

### endless — 2015-11-03T08:23:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181046>

Got my light order from Amazon today....two red LEDs for my overhead lights in kitchen and living room and two 365nm LED black lights. The incandescent black bulbs I bought were very dim and put off a lot of heat. I'm liking these better so far.
Also found and couldn't resist buying the box set of Madeline L'Engle's 'Wrinkle In Time' quintet! I'm excited to read them again, it's been a long time.

### Inger — 2015-11-03T16:16:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181062>

I feel you on the dating, Krista. I seem to fall so much in love.... but it is not always good...... and I get so hurt.. like crushed, really bad. No idea why I love so much.. idk why I hurt so much
I maybe forget to think about it forehand if this is really a good thing... like.. for everybody! I get so fascinated... from the beauty of man
I get fascinated from people anyways.... they fascinate me... every kind of
But I take the pain..... and I know I will come out again on the other side of the dark tunnel...... I can always see the light.. not one second in my life that I lose that, no mater how dark
maybe it is something I have to learn. For sure it is!
Truth always win no matter how much it hurt..... because when you do the right thing, it might hurt but it is a different kind of pain, and you know in the end everything will be better because of it

Good thing is I like to be alone too. I have beautiful moments.. a lot .. just with me..... makes it easier
I think I am gonna marry the nature and the wild...lol
what to do

I am not going on any dating site... because i have important stuff to do...... before I have it done no dating for me....
I like to be alone too
maybe we are getting old?

can you give me a link to what red led lights you have been buying, Krista?

### endless — 2015-11-03T17:52:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181079>

I have not tried that Nicole.....been tempted a few times but in my early CT (indoors) days I found that CT in the evening makes it really hard for me to sleep. I really prefer it earlier in the day. I should try though, it has been 3 years or so since making that observation. Of course, having another person to cuddle up to afterwards would be helpful too!

Inger, I bought these bulbs, [http://www.amazon.com/Sunlite-LED-A...1446565679&sr=1-16&keywords=sunlite+led+bulbs](http://www.amazon.com/Sunlite-LED-A19-Medium-Listed/dp/B00R9MXXBU/ref=sr_1_16_m?s=hi&ie=UTF8&qid=1446565679&sr=1-16&keywords=sunlite+led+bulbs)
I like them, but definitely like candle light better! Just trying to replace the overhead lights for times I need more light.

I think I'm still not ready to try dating sites. I found it kind of depressing sifting through the many profiles that just didn't seem suitable. It's kind of like trying to pick a movie to watch on Netflix....if you just came across the one movie, you'd likely give it a try, but with so many choices all at once, it's hard to choose anything!

### endless — 2015-11-04T08:05:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181117>

I really have to get something to wear for practice nights....all the lights in the damn fire hall are super bright motion activated ones. I'm constantly getting flashed. Uvex are too big. Maybe I'll look at the lowbluelights ones.

### endless — 2015-11-05T07:52:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181193>

Holy fuck, I got bitchslapped badly by my day today....had all these great plans to come home at a decent hour and prepare for my quantum blondes weekend, but I ended up with a problematic install today that took a lot longer than planned. My poor doggies sat waiting for 11 hours and then got no walk. I sometimes wonder if working this job so I can live here is worth it. I work damn hard. I don't even work in my 'chosen career' of nutritional consulting because Jack ruined it for me..... Plus it's impossible to make a living here like that. If I moved to the city I could likely find a much less physically demanding job but I'd be living in a worse environment. So yep, I guess it is worth it.
It's tough being blue collar! Especially as a woman. It always amazes me how many times a day I get comments from men because I am wearing a toolbelt, or up on a ladder, or laying on the floor with my entire upper body hidden inside a cupboard, pulling cables or something. Why is this so fucking amazing to them?? As luck would have it, after a long tedious install I had a service call to a pub at 6pm. I swear it's like the men around here are having their realities challenged by seeing a female tech. I am SO TIRED of being chatted up by the half cut guy at the bar while I replace a computer lottery terminal and answer his inane questions. YES I have a brain AND a tool belt and know how to use both. Sheesh.....

### endless — 2015-11-10T03:19:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-181551>

What an amazing weekend with Jena and Alyson! It was refreshing to hang out with people who understand me

We spent the weekend CT-ing in the lake, gathering UV in the hot tub and eating seafood. Bit of wine and sleep as well. Jack joined us VIA FaceTime for a wee bit on Friday night and that was fun.
There is an exceptional seafood market in Vernon, if anyone can use that information....we got oysters, mussels and clams live plus huge wild shrimp. Had a fantastic seafood bake brunch with Clay on Saturday.

I don't get many weekends off work, so this was a treat! Not only because of that, but also because the weekend was devoted to the pursuit of optimal....everything we did was to that end, and it was a lot of fun doing it with friends.

>

Krista

I keep toying with doing a fast. I've never done fasts well, in the past. I'm a cranky bitch and DH hates it when I get that way. but over the past 3-4 weeks, I just feel so bloated after eating (but I'm not bloated). It feels like I just stuffed my face at thanksgiving dinner.  There are mornings where food is un appealing - so it doesnt occur until btwn 11-2. This morning I woke up feeling full.... but i knew i wasn't ready for a fast.

I think I need to just draw a line in the sand and do one.... and see how I feel after...

As for CT. its been non-existent in my life this past month... I've been really struggling with Cold due to my thyroid being all off. I've been doing coherence testing on myself, and discovered that I wasn't taking the correct thyroid meds, or dosage. So I've adjusted - and I'm beginning to start to crave the cold. But the lack of interest in cold may also be the result of days where we're hitting the mid 70's!

I felt better after the 3 day fast, but I'm still having digestion issues.....basically just bloating. Its driving me insane. My belly swells up like crazy and I look and feel like a fat ass. I've started liver support again , and HCL with every meal to see if it makes a difference.
Gretchen, I AM craving cold but only in the morning and my god damn job is preventing me from getting it. I start feeling chilled in the evening and CT is completely unappealing then.
I'm tired of everything today.....must be low dopamine. I just want to go back to bed and snuggle with my dogs, but instead I have to drive over a mountain pass in a snowstorm for work. Fucking awesome. I'd better stick my head in a snowbank while I'm out there and try to snap out of it.

### endless — 2015-11-20T03:29:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-182397>

Nothing to report withy the dating site yet. I'm being passive, because I am not excited about it right now. It's definitely brought up issues I need to address. Such as: The guys I find physically attractive, I dismiss automatically because I am convinced they will not be attracted to me. Yes, I know that's fucked up but it's what's going on. The ones I think are 'in my league' are not attractive to me. This is why I've always avoided online dating I think. I need to really MEET people and see past their exterior to know if I'm interested. Problem with that is I live in a rural area...to meet most of these guys requires travelling, and I'm not sure it's worth it. I still think that when I am ready the right guy will appear. Maybe next week when I'm not crampy and irritable I'll feel more positive about it.

### Linz — 2015-11-20T14:10:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-182416>

Krista, I think you might be surprised who finds you attractive. I live in a rural place too but went to a big light exhibition in the city to try and find some inspiration for house lighting. A horrifying experience as every exhibitor was promoting LED as this is what the low energy regs permit and the technical experts are mostly engineers with no knowledge of living cells at all. Some were using wireless to make the lights change colour and a very few trying to promote this as healthy 'circadian' lighting despite wireless control and using light with a very poor spectrum. I asked a few superficial questions about lighting options and what was available with improved spectrums and the responses were eye-opening. Many immediately said no-one has ever asked that before and I was introduced to several bosses very keen to give me their business cards. Professional masks slipped off everywhere along with confessions about the technical problems. Only one (involved with industry lighting) walked rather too briskly towards the exit when I asked him if he had thought about putting up a human equivalent of the plant light that was on display to illustrate the effects of light.
What really surprised me was how many real people surfaced from the crowd. If I was single I don't think I would have found any of them on a dating site but I might consider travelling to another light exhibition!!

CT tank is frozen over this morning and I'm just not up to it. Instead I'm sitting outside naked in the sun, bare feet in the crunchy frozen grass. It's -6C or so but the sun feels amazing. I'd stay out here for hours if I could, but unfortunately I have some work to do today that won't wait.
I'm getting better at catching bits of sun during the day.... Now on my drives if it is sunny I roll down the window enough to get the sun in my eyes and on my face.

### endless — 2015-11-26T04:35:20+0200

Well, I sold it anyway.....I've made $200 selling stuff I don't need this week. That's 2 months of gold membership!

I'm starting to get less hungry in the evening. ALMOST skipped dinner tonight but I am trying to be very strict about not eating after 6pm and I didn't trust that I would not get hungry before bed. I can't sleep if I'm hungry. Plus I had some oysters to use. Fried them breaded in pork rind crumbs and butter. Also had a few scallops and sone homemade sauerkraut. 2 squares of 85% Lindt. Actually I ate quite a lot for not being very hungry!

I'm going to spend an hour on the floor stretching while I watch Survivor, then treat myself to an Alitura face mask in the tub!

### endless — 2015-11-26T19:36:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-182714>

So much for the tub.....fell asleep before the end of the show. That's why I rarely watch tv or movies, puts me to sleep. Now I will have to go online and watch the end to see who got voted off the island.

Collecting my little whitetail from my brother's place this morning and taking it over to the meat shop for processing. Good timing, as I am down to my last package or two of elk from last year. Getting all the bones for bone broth (and maybe a few for the dogs) and again, good timing as my halibut broth is getting low already. I will be waiting for frames to go on sale again at the fish market. Unfortunately they don't sell many anymore, or heads, because they've discovered that it's more profitable to make the stock themselves and sell that. I have become a head-hunter......

I'm back to face dunks every morning. I had stopped for awhile (a long while, actually) in favour of full immersion CT but I am enjoying this a lot. It's a manageable part of my morning, while trying to fit CT in doesn't often happen and leaves me feeling stressed. Now if I don't get any other CT in during the day, at least I've done my face. This morning I followed it up with 5 minters of UFO light on my face afterwards while my skin was cold. I'll do that a couple times a week and see what, if anything, I notice. My skin felt dry, so used argan oil on it today.

### endless — 2015-12-05T04:07:16+0200

Supposed to be heading out to a club for a burlesque show tonight but I'm going to stay home. I've been feeling really antisocial lately. Not to mention it's a 30 minute drive each way and I don't have any suitable eye protection for nights out yet. I don't feel comfortable driving in UVEX. Another thing I'm saving for.... Thanks Audrey!
It's gloomy outside again today, so I ended up spending a few hours doing a good house clean. I was only going to do a few things but discovered one of my cats has been peeing in a corner which necessitated a lot more work and evolved into full blown cleaning the house. Animal hair control is always an issue, in my house. I use a swifter vac daily but to really get the hair up I need to damp mop everything too. Offending cat got face planed into a snowbank and told to go think about it in the woodshed for awhile. I've doused the area with peppermint oil and will be keeping my sprtay bottle of vinegar water handy as a behaviour modification tool.

### Inger — 2015-12-06T14:55:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183504>

Krista, if you feel depressed maybe try more raw seafood and meat? High meat? Raw seafood is so "happy-making"........ for me at least
and light... I try to go out every day when I can. I cant always. Because I work so much. But I have my reptile light then.......... today it is so stormy here I cant go out in the woods it is a little dangerous
But being outside even if rainy and cloudy is a life savior... I always look up into the sky too.. suck in the light

For CT you could just dip in a barrel or a outside tub? I dip in my barrel every single day. Always. I just stay in a minute or two..... and then I dry off and stay there nude on my terrace and let my whole body feel the light. Just for another minute... it is so beautiful....... The best moment of the day. And it takes almost no time, but it charges me big!
Maybe you could do something like that? Then the stress will not be there about having to CT for hours... when you have no time...... I think important is that you do it every day. I know for me it is.
It is a habit you do not want to miss.... and it feels like no stress... the opposite

Something has to work because even if it is very gray and rainy and stormy here I do not feel one bit depressed.. quite the opposite. Lots of challenges..... but I feel like unstoppable...lol
I need to run run forward..... I can manage any challenge..... it is so sexy to feel powerful like that. IDK if it is the reptile light... the raw seafood..... the CT... the horse barn.... or something else but I do feel very happy inside despite my life is chaos as always...lol Sometimes.. I think maybe I have gone mad   Something inside me creates this happiness, it is so weird!

### Jack Kruse — 2015-12-06T15:14:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183505>

Sunny as a mofo here...........I'm telling you the subtropics will wake you up Krista. When you get to Mexico it maybe your big wake up call.

### Inger — 2015-12-06T16:02:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183509>

maybe I am able to survive here only because I was a sun addict as long as I remember
I plan my day around the sun..........I do not care for anything else when sun is out.. than tanning... if possible nude.. it always was like that
it is like I cant live without it.. a part of me dies without suns light and I am not willing to let it

### endless — 2015-12-06T19:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183538>

Yes, a quick dip would be good outside, but my tub is frozen! I am resigned to indoor CT for the time being. I am doing face dunks in the mornings at least. And Jack suggests I should do naked snow angels, so might try that later today. No pictures, though....

### endless — 2015-12-06T19:13:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183539>

### endless — 2015-12-09T04:18:06+0200

Interesting possible inadvertent light biohack today.......I used my UFO for 15 minutes this morning. About 5 min on belly, 5 on breast/throat area and 5 on underarms. Two things happened, my FBG that is usually around 5.9 to 6.5 (that's 106-117 for you 'mericans...) was 3.8 (70). Blood pressure 118/80. I will have to experiment further.....

### nicld — 2015-12-09T16:02:40+0200

### endless — 2015-12-10T04:20:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183825>

Yes, the 660 nm red one.
I'm doing my underarms because I want to see if it has any affect on my two skin tags that have been there forever. They rarely get any light being right in my armpit.
I went to the doc for a rare visit yesterday. Mainly to get a prescription for Ativan for my upcoming air travel in case I need it....being terrified for hours is too exhausting. I've also been using a subliminal recording every day so I'm hoping it will be enough and I won't need the medication.
I also got req's for some bloodwork since it's been 9 months since the last set. I asked him about ways to check into vagal tone and he had a HeartMath device right in the drawer that he brought out and hooked me up to. He suggested I look into getting one. Just went on their website and it's 25 off right now....hmmmm.
Also going to get an overnight pulse/ox test to evaluate me for possible apnea. Saw a new dentist recently and she said she suspects I might have it. That'll likely happen in the new year some time.

### caroline — 2015-12-10T10:48:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183834>

Krista..... lets hope you have no apnea... it is not a great thing to have  and you k now what.. almost everyone here seems to have it
looks like it is crazy common! It it is sad... because they will all suffer from oxygen depletion.. which is really not good
I cant believe how common it is............it did not use to be that?

### endless — 2015-12-12T03:05:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-183948>

I don't really think I have it, or if I do it is mild....I'm going to see what the test says though. I used to have a really bad teeth clenching/grinding habit that I believe was because of major magnesium deficiency....i sleep alone so I can't say for sure but I don't think I do it anymore. I know I don't do it at all when I'm awake, at least, and I used to.

I am slowly getting ready for my trip to Mexico....made an appointment to wax the old beav and get a pedicure. My splurge. I could really use a massage but my Jeep decided it needed a spa day so I'm spending $400 on that instead. Bloody vehicles!

### nicld — 2015-12-12T19:21:20+0200

### endless — 2015-12-14T04:19:14+0200

I REALLY wish people would be courteous and not treat it like a personal consult when they get on....ask your question and GTFO so others can play too. I know Jack's answers benefit more than just the person asking, but sometimes it gets a bit ridiculous.....

Or maybe I'm just grumpy.....

I did have a crappy sleep last night because of drinking wine and being exposed to light last night at my FD Xmas party. I really have to get some BPI tint glasses or something for nights out. First priority when I get back from Mexico. I wish I had time to do it before. I was kinds thinking about checking out frame prices in PV though.....if they're reasonable I might buy a couple pairs and get the lenses added when I get back.

### caroline — 2015-12-20T08:18:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184556>

### endless — 2015-12-20T17:27:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184594>

Well shit. I am disappointed in the quality of HeartMath.... I took advantage of a sale to get an Inner Balance device for my phone, to take to Mexico primarily for help with my fear of flying, and the damn thing stopped working after 5 sessions. Now that it's too late to get a replacement before I leave. I was really looking forward to using it on the plane. I wonder if I made a big enough stink about it if they would overnight a replacement for no extra charge.....doubtful at this time of year.

### Inger — 2015-12-20T18:40:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184607>

When I fly, I like the relaxing feeling about te fact that I am stuck and cannot do anything.... just relax...lol so I enjoy it!
If you are scared that the plane will drop down you must just remind yourself that flying in fact is way way less risky than driving a car!
and... if your plane crash you will move into the beautiful universe pretty sure.... in a car crash it is possible to get injured and disabled for life. Are you scared of driving?
Use the flight to explore the feeling of letting go  it is cool
and maybe watch the people around... I find that fascinating too.... people are fascinating
If I sit by a man... I use to think if something scary happens I will grab his hand and hold it tight....... so I have no fear

### endless — 2015-12-22T05:52:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184741>

Thanks Inger.....I will keep these suggestions in mind for the flight.

Also, my HeartMath device is NOT broken, after all....it just needed a reboot. Yay! I'm planning on spending a lot of time on the flight working with it so really glad it's working again.
What a day.....started with discovering my old kidney impaired cat had pissed all over the dog bed AGAIN , and had to try and wash it in the bathtub since I need it clean and dry in 5 days when they go to the sitters. Then discovered the washing machine was falling apart. Literally....two of the control knobs seem to have spontaneously disintegrated. Took the top apart and discovered the plastic brackets that hold the control modules to the housing were completely gone. Strange that two of them failed at the same time. Cheap ass plastic. I'm going to try to use some epoxy to reattach them but probably not till I get back.

I've been woefully low energy/mood/compliance lately.....my dopamine must be suffering. I'm keeping up with the tanning 2x week and I'm getting morning light (not sun) but CT and DHA have fallen by the wayside in the last couple of weeks. Other than general cold temps in the house and working outside lightly dressed. No deliberate water CT lately. I'm so looking forward to the ocean....I think the place we're staying for one of the weeks has a pool, too, so I will do morning CT there as well. Starting to load up on DHA for the flight, more so because I've sucked at it lately. I've been craving red meat so have indulged that with elk, beef and venison in place of seafood. Today I got back on the boat with mussels for breakfast and cod for dinner. Tomorrow I will eat one of my precious jars of salmon roe provided by Jena, and I have another 2 jars set aside for the days leading up to my trip. Maybe some salmon patties for breakfast......

### endless — 2015-12-24T16:37:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184866>

Thank you, I will! I have never needed it like I do now. Funny, after a much more 'aware' spring, summer and fall in which I was much more focused on light and Ct, I crashed HARD in mid November and am still struggling.  Weight gain (but I suspect bloating/fluid retention mostly), carb cravings, low energy/mood......yuck. I am going to watch the sunrise every day I am gone, hopefully from the beach or in the pool, get my morning 2 hours of sun and eat fish every day if I have to go stalk the fishermen to buy it!
Got two tanning bed sessions in this week at least. Morning UV has been spotty, generally I am catching a glimpse here and there through the open car window.

### endless — 2015-12-25T08:39:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-184916>

I've got stress related abdominal pain......here I thought I was doing ok leading up0 to this flight but my body thinks differently I guess. I recognize it because it happened once before, the night before a flight. I actually went to the ER that time because I was in so much pain and I didn't know why. It hasn't gotten as bad this time, probably because I know what it is and am working to manage it. I'm using the Inner Balance app to try and ease it. I doubt anyone is on today but I'm open to any other suggestions if anyone sees this.
Hope everyone is enjoying the holiday!

### caroline — 2015-12-25T08:47:38+0200

Today is going to be about finishing up stuff before leaving tomorrow so I'm going to make a point of stopping for a HeartMath session every couple of hours and keep the anxiety at bay!

### endless — 2015-12-30T19:43:52+0200

Thanks everyone. The flight was manageable in between takeoff and landing, both of which caused me to lose my shit.  Didn't take the Ativan though....what helped the most was listening to Icaros (music used in Ayauasca ceremony) for some reason. Not looking forward to the return flight but I'm putting it out of mind for 2 weeks.

Actually hasn't been sunny much yet! Overcast and humid, which is fine for acclimatizing. The first night after getting out of the airport and renting a car we went to eat, checked in and went to bed......and everyone was hit by some kind of intestinal issue. We suspect the shrimp.

### endless — 2016-01-01T04:37:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-185203>

Good idea!
It's too bloody hot here, to be honest. I'm sure I would get used to it but I like a cooler climate. It's 8:30pm right now and I am sweating my ass off. My moms house is basically open air, just screened at night. No a/c, just ceiling fans.
Heading out soon to set off fireworks on the beach....
Happy New Year everyone!

### endless — 2016-01-06T04:51:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-185536>

It's going to be hard to leave here and go back to the snow and dark! I'm still finding it too hot for a lot of the day but getting my morning/evening sun in and staying under an umbrella between 11-3. No sunscreen, no sunburn! No bug bites, either....some of the family are getting eaten alive by Mosquitos, no-see-ums and sand fleas but I'm not bothered. They bite me the odd time but no reaction or itching.
Right now it's 9pm and I'm sitting outside in my bikini listening to music wafting up from the square below....drinking a piña cada on the rocks. Tough life! I'm going home to a lot of snow so going to enjoy this while I can.

### endless — 2016-01-07T06:46:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-185626>

I've been going to a yoga class many mornings at 9. It's held on the tile roof of the instructors house, under a palapa roof. So nice to be able to be outside for it! Tomorrow will be the last one.
Spent the day at the beach today. LOTS of sun, was feeling slightly burned afterwards, but a 15 minute soak in the pool followed by cold shower and aloe gel did the trick....red is almost gone even only 4 hours later.

Btw - are you home?

### endless — 2016-01-12T17:43:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-186085>

I am home as of last night. I'll do a longer trip report later, have to get ready for work. I'm just doing my oil pulling/UFO session now. My gut is definitely a little off from the travel so seems like a good time to get some red light in there.

I hate how stunned I look in that pic....I was really tired after a bad sleep and had been waiting around for my mom at several mechanic shops for an hour before we stopped in at the base to see if the commandant was in. Back story....I took one of the ball caps from my fire dept with me in hopes of trading it for one from the local FD there....a common practice in fire depts. I went there the first full day we were in town, and was told I needed to come back later in the day. I couldn't, because the single vehicle we all had use of was elsewhere. Stopped in again the following week, same story, come back tomorrow. Again, I couldn't, but my mother was using the car to go to market in the nearby town (she sells coconut oil products at the local markets) so I gave the hat to her and asked her to stop in for me. She did, and did the trade, but said the commandant really wanted me to come in so he could meet me. So the next day after driving someone into town to meet the bus to PV (and getting several mechanic estimates for my mom's car), we stopped in and he was there. Really nice guy, wanted to come over to La Manz and take me out but was on duty until after we left. When we left he took his necklace off and gave it to me, and said he hoped I would come back soon. Maybe I will.....

### endless — 2016-01-13T16:57:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-186177>

Ahhh, I don't know what's going on right now..... I'm really struggling. I thought coming home after a good hefty amount of UV exposure and all I would be feeling good and instead I am just plummeting downhill emotionally. Waking at 4am and experiencing really profound depression and despair.
Realized this morning that I am feeling exactly as I did exactly 6 years ago during the absolute darkest days of my life. I had just struggled through a difficult 6 months with my fiancee who was a narcissistic, sex and cocaine addicted compulsive liar who I loved but who really made my life hell. I had finally convinced him to go to recovery and was looking forward to moving forward with him in a new way when he came back and dumped me unceremoniously. I had nowhere to go and ended up living in my brother's basement for 8 months in severe depression. It was awful. It took me a long time to recover. I spent a LOT of time on myself afterwards and came to a good place eventually about my experience and even forgave my ex. So why do I feel like this current state is somehow connected? Other than the time of year being the same I can't figure it out.
I'm actually pretty scared of this....I don't think I could stand being in that state again. I don't have time for it. I just want to stay in bed and not engage with the world but I have work and responsibilities and nobody to do any of it but myself.
Kind of wondering if meeting someone I might be interested in is triggering this in some way...I've been single since 2010 when that happened. Gun shy about putting myself out there after a rough ride and having my self esteem completely destroyed. I'm still working on that. I guess I try not to think about it but I really am very lonely. I miss human contact.
Fuck, if this is the aftermath of vacation I think I'm better off staying home.

### endless — 2016-01-18T02:02:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-186604>

Working on it....thanks Nicole.
Ok, I think I'm getting a handle on the situation....had a hellish work week coupled with a super heavy period and it kind of threw me for a loop. Getting back on track now. I needed to get a few things accomplished to get back in the groove. Today was my first 'day off' (sorta) so I started out with some morning light exposure by snow blowing/shovelling outside in tank top and shorts for an hour or so. I briefly considered hopping in the CT tub (inside, the tank is frozen outside) but was feeling motivated to other things so I got my 4th quarter GST remittance done instead. Cooked up some squash from the garden and made some 'veggie pucks' to add to the dog food with garlic, kale and turmeric. Payed a few bills and did a few chores, and I'm starting to feel a little better. Tomorrow I'm hitting the tanning salon and seeing the doc for some test results.
Finally listened to the January webinar and Q&A today, too. It's particularly apt for me right now with my dopamine issue. I didn't realize how well I had been doing before until lately. I tried to read Time 7 too but I'm not comprehending it as well as I'd like. Time to pull some of my precious salmon roe out of the freezer and see if I can give my brain a boost! Got the UV lights back up and running today too and used the UFO this morning on my gut again. Finally starting to return to normal bowel habits, more or less. Whatever it was that hit me (all of us, really) the first night in Mexico seemed to have lingering effects and I've felt off ever since.

Yesterday there was a bit of clear sky so I was driving with the window down as long as I had it in sight. Amazing how warm it is on the skin even at this time of year. I can't wait for the day I can be at home in the morning on a sunny day and strip down in my snowy garden!

I'm starting to have hope for an early spring. The last several days have been brighter, no precip, with actual blue sky at times, instead of the low cloud that has been hanging here for months. A very welcome change. I can see the outlines of the garlic bed in the garden under the snow and I know that they are beginning to sprout even now. One of my favourite parts of early gardening is watching the pale little sprouts green up almost instantly once they reach the light.

Gave myself a pretty good knock on the head yesterday...dropped me to my knees for a few minutes. I was working so couldn't do much for a few hours, but iced when I got home. It really hurts to the tough so couldn't tolerate the cryohelmet unfortunately, just a gel pack that I wore to bed. I'll take the cryohelmet out to soften and use that today instead.

### endless — 2016-02-15T23:32:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-189126>

7 days till UV! I've been trying to make it to the tanning salon twice a week as well, but I'm looking forward to the real thing! Unfortunately my brother's place has less sun than mine, I may have to create a little time each morning when I go over to feed the cats to strip down for a quick UV session. Mornings are so busy...

### endless — 2016-04-01T08:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-191810>

Yikes, time to pull my poor journal out of the weeds......

11 days till I'm back in my own bed in my own house!! I've been astonishingly busy since moving in here at my brother's place so the time has flown by. Winter is well and truly over, the temperature is climbing (22C today) and I'm ready to be home so I can hop in the tank first thing and hang out naked in the garden again.
Had a bit of a health crisis since I've been here, some excess overall inflammation as well as some acute inflammation in my index finger that seemed for all the world like gout! Josh F. gave me great advice and it resolved really quickly, thankfully. Working with ones hands is tough when you're down one dominant finger....
Still working on the overall inflammation but getting better. I am on the last day of a 5 day 'fast mimicking diet' like Ruben was talking about. I ate only 3 avocados a day with salt, and 4TBSP of greens powder. Plus water, of course. Abdominal bloating is WAY down and experiencing the usual lack of hunger from ketosis. I can see doing this once a month, for sure. I love the re-set I experience from a short fast, and the renewed appreciation for simple food. I have fresh greens for tomorrow morning (dandelion, cilantro, parsley, spinach) to blend with lemon juice in the morning, and a chunk of sockeye to go with it.

Have been communicating with my Mexican friend, el commandante.....many thanks to Lahelada for proofreading my Spanish before I sent my email! I don't know if anything will develop there since I will likely not be back to Mexico anytime soon. He is travelling to Utah in May, but that's not going to work either as my employee is already scheduled to be away. For the same reason I am going to miss the Quantum Canuck gathering in Vancouver! Bummer.....
I did meet another interesting (and attractive) guy last week who just bought one of the lots across the river from me, where I walk my dogs. I don't know if there's anything there, but I will see him frequently as he is building his house. Although, I suggested he jump in the river the other day and he informed me that he hates cold water. Hmmmm, that might be a problem!

### caroline — 2016-04-02T07:34:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-191862>

Hi krista ..... sounds like all is good!

### endless — 2016-04-02T08:18:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-191870>

Yes, all is pretty good Audrey, how about you?

Went out after dark for the first time in months....and forgot my UVEX. Oh well, just reminds me that I need to get a couple more pair for now until I can get the BPI tints for night done. Still want some BluTechs too, both are on the priority list.

First day back in the CT tank and it was awesome! 48F, full sun, I stayed in 25 min followed by an hour of naked sunbathing. I've missed that so much. Makes a world of difference. Wanted to listen to the April webinar but time got away from me and had to leave it. Probably Sunday....

Upside to being out tonight, I got to enjoy grilled ahi salad and mussels in Thai coconut broth, and a large glass of Malbec.

I just wanted to give it a try and see how I felt. I liked it. Like a little vacation for my digestive system without feeling like I wanted to eat my own arm...lol

Good idea, jack....I'll just start swimming past his property like a mermaid.  Speaking of which, today was absolutely amazing and I met some friends up at the nature park for some dog playtime. There was about a dozen people there, basking in the sun, but I was the only one in the river. Water felt like about 40. I wouldn't be in long at that temp but my tank is 50 and I could definitely wallow in there like a hippo.

### endless — 2016-04-04T19:02:13+0300

Got LOTS of sun yesterday, not sure if that was part of the reason. I woke up with a flat stomach, which didn't even happen when I was sorta 'fasting'. This inflammation and bloating I've been dealing with for the last several months has been persistent, but this morning it was totally gone. Think I will experiment with that a bit...

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-14T20:43:13+0300

Don't survive, thrive. Do life soaring. Our cells read and react to our environment; it shapes us and we are shaped by it.
The six best healers in the world that I know:
1\. Sunlight
2\. Un-fluoridated water
3\. magnetism/grounding to Earth/CT protocol
4\. seafood
5\. Self Confidence
6\. Friends
That is all...........Carry On.
[http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029](http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029)

Here is the key that most of the critics miss..........the order of the recipe is critical to the success. Your perception will be thrown off if you do it other ways. I can think of 3 recent members who went of the rails this way. The last 4 series pointed this point this out but few understand the implications. Neil BB and I have been offline talking about it. Your BUN/creat ratio and ketosis must be started before DHA loading. the blogs have been clear that the exclusion zone inside of cells manifests due to proteins within becoming hydrophilic first. How do you do you make things hydrophilic? You slowly add in electrons. The way to add them is step 4 above. The closer you are to the equator means you have more push from your current (DC) and then you may not need as much........unless there is something peculiar about your environment, job, eyes, skin.

Even though I have no science background, I was able to perceive how phenomenal the April Webinar is intuitively.

I think for a lot of us, we may never be able to understand what you are trying to teach at the academic levels others are able to. But this does not damper my enthusiasm one bit. I feel so privileged to be able to share in the excitement of your vast realm of knowledge, expertise, infinite wisdom, and awesome ground breaking theories. I am so proud to be accepted as a member of this tribe.

I have a very simple way to look at complicated knowledge that is beyond my 'paygrade'. If it is true, then it must manifest itself in some form that we can relate to. And that always mean there should be a way we can experience and/or manipulate (biohack) it. It doesn't matter if you don't understand how A gets to Z. As long as Z is true, then it can become one's reality if one is brave enough to embrace it.

(Going one step further, from a spiritual perspective, I think even if Z may or may not be true, it can become manifested as one's reality...and that really can get me into trouble sometimes...)

Thank you again for everything you have done for us. We are truly blessed.

### endless — 2016-04-19T20:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-193036>

So it occurred to me this morning, while I was standing on a ladder in my white 'Barefoot Dreams' bathrobe, barefoot, hammering a nail into the side of my garden shed, that there might be a reason the neighbours think I'm a bit strange....
Often when I go outside in the morning to walk around and ground, I get distracted by things that need to be done, and so find myself doing them...usually in my bathrobe or naked with a sarong wrapped around me and bare feet. This morning it was hanging out my duvet in the sun, inspecting the raspberry canes, replacing a nail that I needed to hold my rolled up tarp up on the shed, hauling out the lounge chair into the yard and picking nettles for breakfast. Free-form multitasking.....

And it looks like I have a blind date, set up by my sister in law...... That ought to be interesting!

Doris you are new here but I must say you are breath of fresh air........you really have made this website fresh for me. It was getting stale and then you came in and pushed some of the dead air out.........I rather like that and I wanted to say it here. Embracing the beginner's mind is how I tripped over these things.......it came from nothing I knew or was taught. That is why I am sure any of you can do what I did because we are all starting at the same point.

I've come full circle on education and experience, especially when it comes to knowledge. People see thing with the lenses they learned. It takes character to divorce you mind from beliefs. It took me 40 years to get that courage. Medicine applies technical solutions to adaptive challenges today and this is why it is failing. Instead we should all follow our internal compass again and again, and soon our life seems to gain a revolutionary momentum for our soul. Embrace its pull and its gravity. Sometimes, when you ride nature's waves you begin to trust the revolution brewing within. You begin to innovate your life with your real intent and your "WHY" begins to manifest our of the ashes of what was.

Yesterday, I went for a haircut in Austin, and my stylist’s jaw dropped. She could not believe how much hair has grown since she last saw me at the end of January. She said it was like I got a whole new head of hair. So I started to talk to her about the sun, and other important teachings of yours. She was so excited that she wanted to learn more so she can help some of her young clients who have thinning hair and beards.

I would also like to report that since February, I have started at least 16 people on sungazing and eating seafood. Some of them live as far away as in Hong Kong, Toronto and Vancouver!

I realized today when I was cutting the grass that the amount of time I spend grounding in spring/summer/fall is huge, and in winter it's pathetic. ### endless — 2016-04-24T03:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-193335>

So I had my first osteopath appointment today! Not an osteopathic physician, but a practitioner of manual osteopathy. It was fantastic. I almost fell asleep. He worked on my extremely tight hips and also did some work around some of my organs. I randomly had mentioned my CT tank when we were discussing magnesium supplementation (in the context that I add a lot of mag sulphate to it...) and he asked me if I was a follower of Jack Kruse. First time I've randomly met someone who is familiar with the protocols! I will definitely be going back.

I haven't had too much interaction with the former parasites yet so not sure what I will notice there but I'm hopeful that it will positively impact those relationships!

### endless — 2016-05-08T19:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-194381>

Finally got into the garden yesterday. It feels good to spend the entire day barefoot in the dirt. No sunburn. Well, a little pink on face and upper chest but it's faded to tan today. I'm sitting naked in the garden now after my CT and just shading my head and chest under an umbrella while the rest of me soaks it up.

### endless — 2016-05-11T02:08:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-194530>

Heading to Vancouver tomorrow for a visit with my 91 year old Oma. I'll be staying in the guest suite of the extended care facility she's in and trying to mitigate as best I can. Taking 4 gallon glass jugs of my own water which I will supplement with Gerolsteiner, my UVEX, some seaweed and my bathing suit in case I can find a quiet corner on the grounds for morning UV. Then 2 days with Jena before coming home! It's nice to get away for a few days but I really love it here this time of year so I will also be happy to get home again.

### endless — 2016-05-13T18:47:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-194705>

I've discovered a possible reason for the longevity of my Oma, who I've never considered to be in particularly good health. She's now 92 and has various long standing health issues but she was born and grew up in Husum, on the North Sea. They ate a LOT of shrimp and fish, as that was what was cheapest. She was born in 1925 and lived on the coast of Germany until emigrating to Canada in the early 50s (to Edmonton).

### caroline — 2016-05-14T07:19:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-194762>

The things you discover! Did you have a nice visit? How is her cognition?

### endless — 2016-05-22T07:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-195173>

The visit was challenging...her cognition is really bad. She has no short term memory and gets very confused about the past now as well, not able to identify people from photos. She doesn't understand distances anymore, and gets pissed off that people who live far away don't visit her more often because she thinks everyone lives nearby. I'm glad to have had some time with her, even if she likely won't remember it. Each time now could be the last time.

Did have a great 2 day visit with Jena and got some good nature time, CT in the ocean, and tanning in! Also a quick visit with the VIGR boys who were in town for the CHFA trade show. We met up for oysters downtown.

I started methylene blue today....using 100mcg once in the morning. I noticed that I was NOTICING more, if that makes sense....while driving a route I drive almost daily I was seeing things I had never noticed before. Could be a coincidence, but I thought it was interesting. No obvious physical effects yet.

### endless — 2016-05-22T20:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-195188>

It absolutely amazes me how childish people can be on the internet....it's like stepping back into high school sometimes.

Finally got my hanging baskets planted and I'm so happy! I love this time of year, beautifying the space where I spend most of my time is a great way to relax and enjoy my home and my yard. No naked gardening today, it's cool and rainy, but the plants are digging it. Off to walk the doggies and then plant some more stuff in my veggie garden.

in response to your osteopath post: if it wasnt lasting then two things may have occured = the primary lesion was not addressed and therefore everything returned to the way it was, or stability/movement at the most basic level was needed to integrate the changes, but wasnt so then the brain reverted to what it has patterned to do. from my perspective

### caroline — 2016-06-20T07:16:52+0300

### endless — 2016-07-08T10:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198049>

I'm 47 years old, and over my lifetime I have been blessed (?) to have three men at var0us times in the role of father. Honestly not sure 'blessed ' is the right word

My biological father was not around long for my childhood....early divorce. Visitation didn't really develop a strong bond when I was a kid, because his next wife treated me like an unwelcome guest. It wasn't until I was an adult that I developed a closer relationship with him, working around his lifelong severe clinical depression.

My current stepfather is slowing down....when he first came onto the scene he was someone I HATED with a passion.....but also loved his 4 kids that came to visit from Denver CO every summer . I was conflicted....life was good Sometimes.  We have a much better adult relationship.

The third 'father' in my life was Randy, who came into my life when I was too young to remember.My mom met him after leaving my dad and moving to Calgary. I was too young to remember the progression of that meeting, but as far as I was concerned, Randy was my daddy. He was my daddy for the happiest time of my life. I look back on this and am amazed that he just took me on with no question, even though he didn't have kids of his own.
Some of my best memories are related to Randy. I remember trips to visit his mother in rural Alberta. I was only 3 years younger than his twin sisters, so we were inseparable!
Anyway, he passed away last week...so I'm reflecting on my time with him and on fathers in general. I feel lucky to have made it this far without losing any parents. Knowing what I know now makes it harder because I see some of them doing things that I know will affect their health and longevity but there isn't much I can do about it except try and be a good example.

### [email redacted] — 2016-07-08T10:40:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198050>

I am in London has to do with 'chemtrails' anyway I have a question and hopefully Dr Kruse or someone else can help. I have a good friend here and I am shocked to see him blinking his eyes all the time sometimes really kind of scary. Is that likely to be some kind of 'light issue'. I have been here a week and have NOT seen a sunrise or a sunset a kind of grey murk most of the time. Even some guesses on this 'blinking' issue would be appreciated very much

### Jack Kruse — 2016-07-08T14:29:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198055>

You blink a lot to cool the cornea so that you can absorb more light that is present. This happens when you live in a place like London or have a low dopamine state.

### [email redacted] — 2016-07-08T21:10:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198073>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=198055)
>
> You blink a lot to cool the cornea so that you can absorb more light that is present. This happens when you live in a place like London or have a low dopamine state.

Thanks very much for that Jack, it makes thing in relation to my friend a bit clearer. One thing I notice in London and I have been here only 4 days is forget about sunrise and sunset. You do NOT see either one and the people I checked with said that is 'normal' there. I put a fair amount of that down to 'chemtrails' there was one sunny day and the spraying was INTENSE. Worse than anything I have seen in LA.

Most of the time you have this dome of cloud I won't say 'clouds' as it is like one big continuous dome of cloud. I was also in Yorkshire for a week and it was much better there real clouds, sunrises and sunsets a bit at least yes it was cloudy but 'real' nice white puffy clouds. I think London is a dreadful place to live and my friend though a super 'smart' guy and the author of several excellent books is suffering I would say and all the blinking sort of shows it. Thanks again for the quick responce Jack

### endless — 2016-07-09T18:29:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-198134>

Make yourselves at home, guys.....

I discovered SUP recently and LOVE it! I was lucky to find a used board for a really good price and couldn't not buy it. It's amazing for core and stability. My SIL convinced me to try a stretch of the river with her the other day (I was a little nervous, and had only planned to do flat water for awhile) and it was great! I need to get an ankle strap but I managed to not fall off, even in the 'rapids' we have (class II). And I can feel it in my ass, legs and torso today. Moving water while standing is a whole different animal than I am used to. Can't wait to do it again!

### endless — 2016-08-24T03:36:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-200109>

Ok, the FasciaBlaster has arrived (thanks Dawn!!).....and I'm scared of it!  Interesting insight though. I've been reading the damn FaceBook group and watching YouTube videos about it so I've seen and heard a ton about people's experiences already and thought I would be ready to jump right in there but.......I'm scared of pain. Having realized that, I started reflecting on all the things I have avoided because I am scared of getting hurt. There's a lot. I have a big ass tattoo and can sit completely calmly for blood draws and stuff, but pain from other sources is very scary to me.

This is the same thing I experienced when I started to CT. I used to be scared to be cold. I would never consider leaving the house without enough clothing that I would never be cold, no matter what time of year. If I ever did get cold, I would panic. I had to overcome that fear in order to CT in cold water and take walks without a jacket, etc. Now I am often the one who goes to an evening event in shorts and a t shirt with no sweater or whatever while everyone else is bundling up. So, I got over it. Now I have to get over this!

### endless — 2016-08-24T19:29:07+0300

So, oddly enough, the first thing I did after admitting to being scared of being hurt was fall on my steps and hurt myself. Now I have a big swollen bruise on my left quad...probably very similar to what I will get from the blaster! It's like the universe is saying 'See? It's not so bad'.....

### fitness@home — 2016-08-24T20:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-200147>

> Does she use the nugget on you? I'm having a hard time understanding how people use that....

Yes, she does. For me to apply the pressure I want on my upper thigh with it, I place it between me and the wall (while standing) and lean into it.

When I did this on that area of fascia in my upper left thigh and hit it just right, I could feel a sensation of it in my thigh wrapping around to my hip area. I use inoculation method. Press, hold for 30-60 seconds, move on to the next point about 1/2 inch farther.

### endless — 2016-09-02T06:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-200693>

Well, well.....my appetite for seafood has finally returned. Wonder if it's the days getting shorter. I'm still eating a fair amount of seasonal carbs but just this past week I've been back to eating fish after months of not being attracted to it at all. Doesn't hurt that I had this amazing Thai seafood salad for lunch the other day....mussels and prawns and fish and squid in a typically flavourful dressing with a few veggies (celery, onion, tomato, lettuce). I already want that again...might have to start making it myself.
The fruit consumption this season has been great! I've indulged in each thing as it has been at peak season, enjoyed it over that week or so, and moved on. Most recently I've been eating prune plums, next up is apples and pears. I've also eaten a LOT of watermelon this summer. I'm not going to miss it when the colder weather hits.
Theres a forest fire burning on the other side of the ridge from me....earlier today it was a bit sketchy with high winds and I was starting to get ready for possible evac. notice, but now the wind has died off and light rain has started so hoping by daylight it'll not be any closer!
I made myself a mini fascia blaster, by removing two of the claws on the big one. I don't want to cut it until I can order another and it's expensive and a pain to ship it here.

### caroline — 2017-02-08T09:22:22+0200

### endless — 2017-03-04T18:57:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209618>

Haha, unfortunately not much Audrey! I've just been busy and my computer time has consisted of jumping on FB. I've been staying part time at my brother's place and I find it frustrating trying to type more than a few words on my phone.

What a winter! We've had everything from deep cold to huge dumps of snow to huge dumps of rain. Lot of sun for part of it, and then gloomy socked in days for weeks. I'm really ready for the end but apparently mother nature is not as we are still hovering around freezing and getting more snow. Melt, dammit!
I've been far too sedentary the last month as well. Before that I was getting into a groove on the xc ski trails, getting both me and the dogs exercised and uv'd most days. Then the conditions went to shit and I couldn't get out for awhile and now I'm finding it harder to get back into it. Just bought a TRX-style suspension trainer that was immediately borrowed by a friend, so when I get it back Im looking forward to trying it out!
Ct has been sporadic, not much in water, some in snow and some just in cold air. Once the river is accessible I will start back doing dips there, right now that isn't possible because of snow and ice.

I'm caring for my brother's dog for a couple of months while he is overseas, and learning the ins and outs of cannabis cancer treatment. He was diagnosed right before they left. They refused the biopsy, not wanting to put him through anesthesia, but the vet was pretty sure. SO every day we have a regimen of proteolytic enzymes, cannabis oil, turmeric and medicinal mushrooms going on. I've taken on a lot in addition to my already busy life but hey, it's my pattern....

So I finally had success with the glasses I ordered from Zenni....the 550 turned out pretty good and so did the 500. The lack of anti-reflective coating is a bit annoying but I can live with it. Now have to find the time and $$ to go get REAL glasses (prescription) to tint for night driving.

I watched that movie, 'Captain Fantastic' last night. It was interesting because I actually DID grow up like that, for awhile. Or, at least, what COULD have been like that if the adults in my life had made my upbringing a priority as the father did in the movie. In my situation the kids were definitely not the focus of the whole experience. I'd MUCH rather have been taught the way they were than left to my own devices to work my way through the correspondence school curriculum on my own. Nevertheless, I learned a lot of things that most of my peers did not. It was a definite advantage in ninth grade (my first year back in the 'real world') when our class did a 'survival' camping trip in November, north of Edmonton and I was the only one who could start a fire.  Damn city kids.....

Off to walk the menagerie.

### OptimalSkr — 2017-03-05T22:33:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209711>

Just wondering. Which lenses did you order from zenni? And how much did the bpi tints costs? I was thinking of doing the same thing. Have ordered from zenni before now just have to contact bpi. I have a pair of blutech that I wear all the time but I want a true blue block for night with a prescription pair.

### endless — 2017-03-09T21:08:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209970>

I ordered plain, uncoated lenses from Zenni (which are free with the frames). You have to make sure to NOT get anti reflective coating, that will screw it up. The dyes were $30US each (I got both the 450 and the 500/550)and I bought a lens holder for $25. It was fairly easy to do and I'm pretty happy with the results.

### OptimalSkr — 2017-03-09T22:22:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209988>

Thanks for the info. I'm gonna order another pair from zenni and contact bpi to get this done. So do you wear the 450 in the day and 550 at night? I see the 600 is complete blue block. Wonder if that would be too dark at night might be interesting.

### endless — 2017-03-09T23:24:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-209994>

I will use the 450 if I'm indoors during the day with no natural light, or doing computer work. 500 or 550 at night. I think the 600 would be hard to function with, but maybe ok for bumbling around at home. Don't forget the light hitting your skin has impact too, so reducing ambient light is also important. If I'm hanging out by candlelight at night I might not even wear my glasses, but if using amber bulbs or anything higher lux I put them on.

### endless — 2017-08-01T08:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-journal-or-why-is-the-neighbor-wearing-sunglasses-at-night.3226/post-220590>

Hi Audrey,
No, I did not get a Quantlet....perhaps in future though. I'm place marking yet again because I really do intend to post in here again. I think the thought of 'catching up' has been to daunting, so I will not try, and just start from now.

Fully into the peak of summer now here, with garden burgeoning with food and the river beckoning every spare moment. I resent every moment that work ties me away from my little oasis. The heat has been brutal...many fires north of us but so far so good for my area. Thankfully my skin is now used to the intensity from my morning full body exposure. Still have to seek shade midday since my heat stroke experience last year but no burning!

Finally having some success with reducing inflammation by following the low lectin diet plan espoused by Steven Gundry. I've come to the conclusion that food DOES matter, at least for me, at this time. Light hygiene and EMF mitigation is of course important but my persistent 'puffiness' has finally subsided by eliminating a few things from my diet.

I bought a good quality Far IF sauna and am looking forward to using it but with these temps it's been the last thing on my mind. CT, on the contrary, has been welcome and amazing. We (the pups and I ) spend time in the river every day, and occasionally take the paddle board out.

T minus 2 days and counting till the first family arrives for the family reunion/memorial for my stepfather who passed last July. Roasting a pig and spending a weekend on the river. I'm hoping my garden is up to the task of salad for 200 people.....

### drezy — 2017-08-01T17:26:05+0300
