> why would 500-640 be ideal when the top range is 1636?
>
> am I missing something???

You are mixing DHEA with DHEAs

also it is good idea to describe what was tested and where and give units.

Preferably serum at LabCorp.

The

DHEAs(500-640) ng/dL

looks to me like "old" recommendation (just few months old).

Those recommendation on lef.org usually come from dr Dzugan.

Recently that changed to

Men DHEAs(350-490) μg/dL

Women 275-400 μg/dL

Dr Dzugan (in his book) also recommends (100-200)mg/day of 7ketoDHEA

and then plain DHEA as much as it takes to get to that range.

When I (initially) following 640) ng/dL got there, I have found having very high

5α-Androstane-3α

while

my DHT remained high but within range.

That have made me change the goal to 490.

\--------------------

TotalTestosterone= 761 ng/dL

is respectable

if it is good highly depends on SHBG level

also E1, E2 and progesterone are highly important.

..

Speaking of DHEAs

I am taking 7ketoDHEA and DHEA from lef.org

100mg/wakeup 7ketoDHEA

100mg/wakeup DHEA

100mg/4hrs latter 7ketoDHEA

100mg/4hrs latter DHEA

I came to that dose by frequent testing.

200mg/day 7ketoDHEA is my "permanent" dose, I do not know how to test it or evaluate required dose size.

If someone have idea about it, please speak up.

Dr Dzugan, in his book, describes use of either with no explanation why.

If I was (like you are now) in a process of establishing your DHEA dose,

I would use

DHEA Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology/Allergy Research

...

# Thread: Help me figure out what's going on!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-09-08T14:27:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-50228>

I'm desperate and need some help here! Hoping some "outside eyes" will be able to point me to a resolution. For the last week or a little longer I've been having major stomach pains - almost feels like gas pain, but I'm not gassy at all. It's so bad that I'll be walking or even sitting and find myself totally bent over, unable to take a deep breath it hurts so bad.

I'm pretty sure I've had this happen in the past and it just eventually resolved, which is what I was hoping for this time. The pain did seem to diminish a few days after it started, but now it's back to being pretty intense. It happened as soon as I woke this morning even while lying in bed. I can't figure out if it's related to something I'm eating or not since I've been pretty much eating the same meals for the last several weeks and since it happened this morning 12+ hours after I'd last eaten it makes me think it's not food related.

The only changes I've made in the last 10 days were:

\- lower my DHEA (because my blood tests were very elevated)

\- I started using a progesterone cream about 2 weeks ago - don't know if that could somehow be related, but I did stop it for the last 2 nights and the pain hasn't stopped

\- I also started using 500 mg curcumin extract with my breakfast. I do have oxalate issues and turmeric is very high oxalate, but testing has shown the extract to be ok, so I thought I'd give it a go. I didn't use it yesterday morning though and like I said, the pain is still very present today.

Thoughts on any of the above 3 contributing to this?

I pretty much thrive on routine, so my diet really has stayed pretty consistent over the last 3-4 weeks:

BAB - generally red meat + offal, CO, tallow, bone broth, sea veg, mushrooms

Lunch - seafood (usually shrimp, scallops, fish), avocado, bacon

Dinner - about the same as lunch, but some days I'll add a few raw oysters or some roe

I drink about 8 oz of kombucha a day and have sauerkraut with every meal, but I am super strict with my diet and don't "cheat" or add any extras at all.

The other thing is that the pain generally starts after I've had my very first bite of food - which is usually sauerkraut since I eat that before every meal.

\*Gold star\* for the person who figures this mess out! I suppose I could go to my PCP, but I really can't see them being very helpful and I keep thinking it's something that will pass. I just need to figure out the cause.

Oh - should also mention that cold baths definitely help it, of course  Just with I could stay in the tub all day!

### Stavity — 2012-09-08T15:08:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-50232>

I would look at the fermented food and the tea you are drinking. What you are describing to me happened when I was overdoing Bubbies pickles. I decided fermented foods were not for me. I think seafood is for everyone, but the rest we eat should be tailored to each individual.

Wanted to say it sounds like you keep offending your gallbadder by what you are eating. Let it rest.

### Shijin13 — 2012-09-08T15:23:45+0300

My guess is you're referring to changing the curcumin and I should keep using that? Thanks!

### PrimalPam — 2012-09-08T17:25:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-50255>

You say you eat sauerkraut before each meal. And you are drinking 8 oz. Kombucha. All great stuff! But for me, the timing of these is important. I eat my sauerkraut & drink kombucha WITH my meals, not before. So, I'll have a plate: a large piece of fish, some spinach, some sauerkraut (Bubbies, about 1/2 c), a Bubbie's pickle. I eat it all together, like on the same forkfull. And drink the Kombucha while I'm eating it.

I'm wondering if the probiotics are going down on an empty stomach, which is causing you pain?

### kathylu — 2012-09-08T20:34:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-50286>

Gallstones or kidney stones? SIBO? Sounds like you need to see your doctor.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-08T20:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-50289>

> since it happened this morning 12+ hours after I'd last eaten it makes me think it's not food related.
>
>
> The other thing is that the pain generally starts after I've had my very first bite of food - which is usually sauerkraut since I eat that before every meal.

irritated stomach lining, gastritis..... acid (sauerkraut) on abrasions in your stomach = pretty bad pain

turmeric can irritate your stomach lining

I'd stop the acidic foods, add DGL, and think about what might have caused it (stress alone can do this, but also infection, too much acid in your food, gerd....). And empty stomach often will bring the pain on.

### BJK77 — 2012-09-08T23:04:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-50311>

> **Souldanzer;50843 wrote:** irritated stomach lining, gastritis..... acid (sauerkraut) on abrasions in your stomach = pretty bad pain
>
>
>
> turmeric can irritate your stomach lining
>
>
>
> I'd stop the acidic foods, add DGL, and think about what might have caused it (stress alone can do this, but also infection, too much acid in your food, gerd....). And empty stomach often will bring the pain on.

Y'know what Soul? I think you may deserve the gold star  That makes total sense. It did all start after I began the turmeric and it's definitely much, much worse on an empty stomach like first thing in the morning and about 3-4 hours after eating. It also seems to calm down after I do eat a meal even though that first bite of kraut causes some initial pain.

I do have some DGL here, but never took it before. I guess I'll give it a try and hope that it works to calm things down.

Now to figure out what caused it. I'm wondering if it could just be the turmeric. It's possible I had a bit of extra stress this week at work, but nothing major. My bet is on the turmeric. Darn! How do we get around that for those of us with sensitive guts?!?!

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-08T23:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-50313>

> How do we get around that for those of us with sensitive guts?!?!

Sandwiching it often works for me. I eat the offending items including supplements in the middle of my meal. Food below, food above, jump up and down to mix it up a bit (ok, don't do that ).... lessens the amount of concentrated acid or irritating whatever that will touch your stomach lining. Things will mix and be less offending. I have to eat my cysteine that way or it wouldn't stay down ever.

The occasional bottle of very acidic kombucha still sends me cringing sometimes though.

### Stavity — 2012-09-09T16:02:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-50470>

I keep forgetting to buy DGL but it is on my list. I use to take it.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-14T01:57:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-me-figure-out-whats-going-on.3523/post-51537>

BJ, I tried to pm you but your box is full.... you need to be off the turmeric if that's what's causing the pain. And you need to be off it for a while...... if you have gastritis you need to heal the gastritis before you eat anything that will upset your stomach lining more. Healing gastritis can take weeks, months, years for some. It won't go away in a couple days off, unfortunately.

# Thread: Low T3, Low Carb, Low Life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-09-08T19:52:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-50282>

I feel like I am in between a rock and a hard place with my lack of a working thyroid gland.

I tried moving my T3 up this summer, and it was a disaster. After 3 months of trying to make the dose work, I gave up, and went back to T4 only meds. I think my symptoms were adrenal related (high heart rate, low blood pressure). Even though I was really struggling, my mood was still optimistic.

Now I am taking 150mcg T4 with 12mg T3. I am 3 weeks into this new regimen, and hoping my T3 levels will come up (they were below the bottom of the range before a added the T3). I've gained weight (8 pounds), and I feel a different kind of terrible. Almost depressed mentally, and no endurance. As in, I can barely make it up the stairs. Doing anything feels difficult. Last night it occurred to me that everyday is a struggle, and another 40 years of this sounds unbearable. I've started taking cycloset and getting am sunshine, and I'm hoping those things will boost my adrenals so I can eventually tolerate more T3.

Well, this morning, I was cutting up cantaloupe for everyone else, and decided to eat a small serving of it. And I feel a little better. Still not great, but not so deathly. I am able to do a little more...

I have been really strict about staying on the reset protocol, but I'm not really living. I am surviving the days waiting for it all to "work." It's always a struggle to not eat. I think I am in such a low energy state and my brain really, really wants food (even my regular epi-paleo stuff) as much as possible. Food gives a little pick-me-up, but I am never satiated.

So, I am wondering if there is any merit to increasing my carbs, just a little, at least until I can get a better hold of my thyroid status? Would this be counter-productive? I want to move towards better living as quickly as possible, and so I have made my choices that way. I still need to have a little bit of energy to do my basic living requirements, and I'm really wondering if a little more carbs would be safe, at least short term? Thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-08T20:11:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-50284>

wait for BG 12.......

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-09T21:51:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-50557>

Ready and waiting...

Up another 1.5 pounds on the scale this morning (probably from the cantaloupe, or possibly the vinegar that I used to marinate the steak..? I'm betting on the fruit though), and neuropathy starting in 6 of my FINGERS. I've felt twinges of it over the past few months, but nothing like today... Sigh...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-09T22:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-50563>

BG 11 just got up.......as a precursor to BG 12......READ BG 1-6 about ten times. It is going to matter when you get BG 12. And when you get it.......brace yourself. Some major circadian knots will be tied nicely. Paleo beware.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-10T03:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-50624>

Have you tried the Quelsen shake?

Quelsen has a great coconut milk shake - 1 can whole fat coconut milk, a little drop of stevia, 100% dark chocolate powder, 1/4c of HWC, cinnamon, and nutmeg - blend up and drink 3x/day w/meals - you'll be a fat burner in no time. I took the more gradual approach and did that 1x/day

It helped me with the muscles that feels like rocks and low energy, but I make no promises on the weight side. Omit heavy whipping cream if AI.

### AKMan — 2012-09-10T19:43:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-50749>

[http://www.tpa-uk.org.uk/mgt\_of\_thyroiddef3.php](http://http://www.tpa-uk.org.uk/mgt_of_thyroiddef3.php)

Maybe something in here to find helpful. It sounds like you need to get some really good labs and find a really good doctor who can interpret them. Adrenal support is very important and conversion issues can make thyroid unmanageable with meds. Don't look to Jack to cure you and don't rely on BG-12 giving you the answers you need--use all that info, and what you have learned from Jack to find a good doc and know when to call 'bull\*\*\*\*' and fire the ones that aren't helping you. If they aren't talking adrenal support and getting lots of labs it's time to find a new doc. All the carbs in the world won't help if you have AI and don't know it.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-10T20:15:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-50756>

Love your advice AKMan! It took me years to find a doc who recognized that I had both adrenal issues and Hashis. Keep looking and find the one doc who can help you. They are out there. But keep researching to learn more and in turn a doc will be easier to find. I got to the point that I started interviewing the receptionists over the phone before I made an appt to see if they were aware of the issues I was facing. They know a lot more than you think they do. The right doc for you is out there.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-12T03:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-51112>

> **bigknitwit;51112 wrote:** Ready and waiting...
>
>
>
> Up another 1.5 pounds on the scale this morning (probably from the cantaloupe, or possibly the vinegar that I used to marinate the steak..? I'm betting on the fruit though), and neuropathy starting in 6 of my FINGERS. I've felt twinges of it over the past few months, but nothing like today... Sigh...

My thyroid levels are ALL below where they should be. I had a consult with Dr. Kruse on Saturday and he told me I need to cut the occasional fruit. It makes me sad  Also said minding light and sleep was very important. Not sure if that helps at all?

### popcornsally — 2012-09-13T17:49:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-51443>

Bigknitwit-- I don't have a solution for you. Just wanted to express my sincere sympathy. What you describe sounds very similar to what I've experienced. It's all very confusing and finding a _good, pro-active_ doctor is much easier said than done. I resorted to ordering my own blood panel (and paying for it myself). I still don't know all my numbers, but the info I got from my most recent panel actually helped me move in the right direction. In my case, low pregnenolone and DHEA were present and replacing those has made the biggest difference of any single thing I've tried. I'm also on natural desiccated thyroid supplement plus adrenal support of various kinds. Compared to one year ago I feel about 80% better.

You can't sit back and wait for your doctor to actually CARE enough to keep trying things to make YOU feel better! If you feel crappy, you are going to be much more motivated than anyone else to figure out a solution. Either confront your current doc with what you think needs to be done or find a new one. What I did was threw away the lab order my doc gave me, ordered what I wanted (which included her stuff plus more) then gave her those results. Luckily, she's pretty mellow . I believe you WILL feel better. I hope it happens soon.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-13T17:51:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-51444>

you are all assuming doctors learn or know anything about this.......they do not. You have to go and learn it on your own

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-11-14T23:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t3-low-carb-low-life.3528/post-64336>

For me as long as the dr. is willing to hear me out, then that is good enough for me. If it's my concern, then it's up to me to be armed with knowledge. As many people stated, if you go to a dr. with reason and rationale for certain tests you would like and they still give a hard time, then yes, you 'fire' that doctor and find a new one. You can't let someone else determine your health, at least not like that.

# Thread: Hello from Austin, TX
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.3530/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-09-08T21:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.3530/post-50301>

Hello Dr. Kruse and company. My name is Natalie. I am 34, 5'5", 245lbs (!), married, no kids, small business owner... and a serial yo yo dieter! My goal is to lose weight (about 100lbs) and, more specifically, snow ski with my family this winter.

I've been reading and practicing Paleo lifestyle for 2 years, sometimes 100% paleo and sometimes 70% and sometimes not at all. I love Paleo food. I feel good when I'm 100% paleo. I love to cook. It's a dream come true. I read obsessively on the subject. Plus or minus 5 lbs, **I have not lost weight while Paleo**. I may have lost/gained inches, but have had a real tough time moving that scale. I'm not totally discouraged. I just keep reading and perfecting my food because I know/I feel I'm on the right path.

This is how I found Dr. Kruse: Primal Blue print -> MDA -> a bunch of other Paleo blogs -> Bulletproof Executive -> Jack Kruse

I am currently: Bulletproof IF, lifting heavy weights 2x week, trying to maintain keto and face dunking CT. I went straight for the Lepting Rx and CT Protocols. Started dunking my face. I also just went to a cryotherapy studio to try and ease some sore muscles. I'm curious if that can help with my road to cold adaption.

From what I can tell, I am showing almost all signs that I am becoming Leptin sensitive, per Kruse's Leptin Rx post. Meaning, better mood, better sleep, better energy, and less cravings. Not sure if I need to start Leptin Rx anyway? I'm concerned about this because I lift heavy weights 2x week. Per the L Rx I might need to stop.

I'm so thankful Dr. Kruse is on a mission to save us. No sarcasm. I am seriously thankful this information is free. I am having a hard time getting started with the Kruse informtaion. I just found this website last week and have since listened to every pod cast I can find. I'm trying to read blog posts, but I just can't keep up.

So, here I am. 100 lbs over weight. Feeling good. Blood work looks good, per my palo-loving/holistic ND. No real reason why I shouldn't be losing weight. Hoping Dr. Kruse can help me find out why I am not losing weight.

Questions:

1\. Where do I start? What do I do? What do I read?

2\. Will cryotherapy help with cold adaption?

3\. Should I start the Leptin Rx even tho I'm seeing signs of L sensitivity?

Thanks again for your good work, Dr.

Sincerely,

Natalie

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-08T22:24:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.3530/post-50304>

The easy start is the place to start.

### caroline — 2012-09-09T11:05:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.3530/post-50453>

Welcome to the forum and keep reading, reading, reading and keep us posted on how you are going

### nonchalant — 2012-09-09T17:20:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.3530/post-50489>

Hi Natalie, always good to have another Texan here!

You didn't mention seafood. Are you eating seafood regularly? It's packed with nutrition, more than the usual Paleo choices.

About your lack of weight loss, I would say that a lack of hormone balance probably has to be the problem. You see the benefits of your efforts, just no scale moving. Most doctors won't see any extremely low numbers on the hormone tests, and therefore say everything is fine. But you need high numbers on some hormones to be optimal. And the ratio of one hormone to another has to be right.

Another potential issue is toxins. If your body has tucked toxins such as halides, excess extrogens, or pesticides/plastics away in your fat, it will fight tooth and nail to keep those toxins safely hid. You have to be sure to take Vitamin B complex, and add some dietary elements for detox.

How are you doing with the CT? Is the face-dunking easy? Does it feel good? Do you look forward to it? If so, you may want to progress further into the protocol. I'd be interested to see if after a few sessions of room-temp water, your body begins to adapt to the cold. That will tell you if your thyroid is stepping up and doing its job. Also CT will help change your hormones for the better. One thing that may help is a scale that measures body fat and muscle %. It helps when you CT and don't see the weight move, but see the body fat % steadily creep down and the muscle % steadily creep up, while not exercising.

About your weight lifting, does it make you feel really good? If it is a chore, you may want to stop it. It could be causing more stress to your body.

Welcome to the site! There are threads here that discuss every point I made above. Enjoy the process!

### shilohman — 2012-09-09T19:54:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-austin-tx.3530/post-50524>

Welcome, like Nonchalant said, its great to have another Texan here.

As to your questions:

1\. Dr. Kruse answered that

2\. cryotherapy - don't know but probably wouldn't hurt.

3\. LeptinRx - You need to get blood work done before you can

answer that. Go back to answer one and start from there.

> 1\. Where do I start? What do I do? What do I read?
>
>
>
> 2\. Will cryotherapy help with cold adaption?
>
>
>
> 3\. Should I start the Leptin Rx even tho I'm seeing signs of L sensitivity?

### TheKid — 2012-10-04T20:10:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-have-i-spawned.3547/post-56271>

Speaking I what I might have spawned. I am wondering how much protein your little ones eat.

I have a 21-month-old daughter who is only 50th percentile in height and weight.

We have an Elevation Burger in town (grass fed, organic beef) and we go there from time to time. It is not uncommon for my daughter to eat two patties. They are "kids burger" patties, but I was told that a kids burger is just an adult single.

My understanding is that those burgers are 1/2 pound in size. Is my little girl really eating a pound of meat? I mean, even if those burgers are 1/3 or 1/4 lb. I'd still be shocked.

Is that possible? Is that healthy? She won't touch most veggies. Loves fruit, but we're trying to cut back now that fall is upon us. And if there are no sweet potatoes around before the meat comes, she just makes massive amounts of meat disappear. Ou should see her go to work on a leg of lamb.

Help!

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-04T20:44:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-have-i-spawned.3547/post-56282>

> **TheKid;57527 wrote:** Speaking I what I might have spawned. I am wondering how much protein your little ones eat.
>
>
>
> I have a 21-month-old daughter who is only 50th percentile in height and weight.
>
>
>
> We have an Elevation Burger in town (grass fed, organic beef) and we go there from time to time. It is not uncommon for my daughter to eat two patties. They are "kids burger" patties, but I was told that a kids burger is just an adult single.
>
>
>
> My understanding is that those burgers are 1/2 pound in size. Is my little girl really eating a pound of meat? I mean, even if those burgers are 1/3 or 1/4 lb. I'd still be shocked.
>
>
>
> Is that possible? Is that healthy? She won't touch most veggies. Loves fruit, but we're trying to cut back now that fall is upon us. And if there are no sweet potatoes around before the meat comes, she just makes massive amounts of meat disappear. Ou should see her go to work on a leg of lamb.
>
>
>
> Help!

NORMAL! at least in my house... there are nights the Havoc (2yo boy) will clip off 2x 1/2lb burgers. some mornings he clips off 2-3eggs etc. Chaos doesn't eat as much but its pretty solid in the meat variety. There are nights she doesn't want veggies so she just eats meat. Its not that she doesn't like them...she just doesn't want them.

they'll each eat a whole can of sardines by themselves (no sharing) let her at it - she knows what her little body needs!

+1 my 8 year old's typical breakfast is 4 eggs and a whole bunch of meat. Sometimes with yogurt and seasonal fruit, too. Dinner is typically the same amount of food. (Very little for lunch.) # Thread: Need Tips for keeping Cortisol in check on school mornings

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-10T01:29:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-tips-for-keeping-cortisol-in-check-on-school-mornings.3552/post-50589>

Does anyone else feel the strain, as you to help everyone get ready for school? Or church, or soccer, or wherever you're taking young people on a timeline?? I do my best to make the older 3 (11, 9, 6) as independant as possible (I'm going to have no problem if they forget their lunch (no cafeteria) some day). They are actively involved in packing their lunches (in the evening), and getting ready in the morning. But still... My 6 year old left the other morning without eating breakfast. She was served what she asked for, and decided she didn't like the look of it... I figure I have to let them fail to help them succeed independantly, but our mornings are stressful!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-10T01:36:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-tips-for-keeping-cortisol-in-check-on-school-mornings.3552/post-50590>

better off not to eat......IFing is good. But yet you stressed over something that was actually good for them. Another neolthic thought thta hurts you yet again. Being a mom is not always optimal. Said that to MJ in BG 8 and it hit her between the eyes.

### indigogirl — 2012-09-10T05:43:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-tips-for-keeping-cortisol-in-check-on-school-mornings.3552/post-50652>

Bigknitwit,

I'm past those years (my kids are 30 and almost 21), but of course I remember them and of course raising children is sometimes stressfully.

Jack says being a Mom is not always optimal, yet we'll be Mom's for the rest of our lives!

A friend once said to me that Mom's are only as happy as their

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-14T19:30:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-else-dying-for-bg-12.3567/post-51655>

One thing I keep coming back to, that I think is a missing piece to the whole-life-optimal picture, is auditory things.

It's known that rhythmic sounds can alter neuron firing. It's also known that music can alter the production of certain chemicals and hormones in the brain.

Just like the light/visual thing is relevant, might auditory input be as well? If we are going by the argument that visual input is 65% of the pic, then does that leave diet the only other relevant input?

Also, since our brain can get multiple overlapping signals (visual, auditory, taste, touch) all at the same time. . can you REALLY say at any given moment JUST visual is 65%

Mebby BG-32 will explain how dancin in rhythm keeps out little bacteria happy and pulsing together.

# Thread: Why did I wait so long?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2012-09-10T23:35:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-50790>

I finally tried some of my fish head bone broth today, for lunch. Lunches have been a problem, lately, because I find I am not hungry enough to make much effort to have something nutritious. I poured a small bowl of the broth, added salt and a bit more pepper, and wow, so good! I went back for seconds, thirds, and ended up consuming the entire quart of broth (minus the solid parts I strained out.) Dr. K had recommended bone broths for my gut health; itâ€™s about time I finally took that advice.

A couple days ago at lunchtime I bought a fish head from the asian market. Not the expensive salmon head, but the cheap one that had no species identification. I picked out one that had the nicest eyes. The man at the counter was a bit surprised when I didnâ€™t want it cut into pieces. He assured me it still had the brain. I took it home and popped it into the crock pot with water to cover, peppercorns, powdered spirulina, turmeric, bay leaf, and the rest of a package of fresh seaweed that was packed in salt. I rinsed the seaweed and artfully arranged it on top of the fish head, so if DH were to open the lid, he wouldnâ€™t be â€¦ startled.

After I got home from work I pushed on the fish head with a spoon, and to my surprise it easily crushed down to nothing. There were just thin triangular bones left that were all etched clean in the 5-6 hours it cooked. I put everything into canning jars & put them in the fridge, because we had other plans for the night.

I didnâ€™t follow a recipe, just tossed in stuff I had on hand. With the good results Iâ€™ve had, I may actually find some recipes for other ingredients to try next time. And I want to buy a whole fish, so I'll be sure to get the liver and thyroid, and filets to grill. Oh, and next time Iâ€™ll probably put the crock pot out in the garage. Whew, it was a bit pungent when I came home. And yes, DH did open the lid out of curiosity when he got home, but nothing stared back at him, thank goodness!

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-10T23:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-50800>

Sounds delish! I have made fish broth before, but recently have been doing bone broth. I stopped because I wondered about cooking the fish for so long... how do you gauge the temp? And the length of time to cook? I was under the impression (wrongly?) that cooking will destroy the DHA. I'm sure you've figured all this out. Can you say more?

### nonchalant — 2012-09-10T23:59:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-50809>

I just cooked it on the low setting for the crockpot, whatever that was. A simmer. This was just an experiment. And the time was from my lunch break to when I could get back home. I figured I didn't have much to lose, with the cheap ingredients.

I'm sure I destroyed lots of DHA, especially compared to a MHS. But cooked seafood is good for us, and tasty, and the dissolved cartilage and bone was what I was after. It took me about 5 minutes to assemble the ingredients and start the cooking. Then I left the house. Easy peasy.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-11T00:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-50810>

Thanks, makes sense! I wonder if dissolved cartilage and bone of fish are better for us than grass fed cows. Probably!!

### Primal Dream — 2012-09-11T00:02:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-50812>

That sounds really good. Congratulations! I've added your 'recipe' to my fish broth recipe page.

I just had my own woo-hoo moment: I made my first fish head smoothie!!! And I loved it!!!

It was a scup -- not familiar with that kind. They are larger than mackerel, but the fish guy only had headless mackerel. I have good kitchen shears, but I had a really hard time cutting up those heads (I used 2). I kept saying, 'I can't believe I'm doing this'. The bony/cartilage parts were very difficult to cut through. But I did it. I added 3 lemons in my Vitamix. And some sea salt and a swig of lemon CLO.

I thought the vitamix was going to break at first. I added a couple of ice cubes because I didn't want a warm fish head smoothie. I first removed the tamper thingie -- and what I had was a really thick pudding! So I added some water, not much, and whirred it again for a while. It was still thick but not as bad, but it was definitely warm. So I added some more ice and some more water.

It still was pudding consistency. But at least it was very chilly. I had 2 glasses of this stuff, and it actually tasted like a lemon pudding. It was so very good. Ate it with a spoon.

My other part of dinner was 6 raw oysters that I shucked. I was going to add some veggie, but I'm stuffed. And I feel so incredibly energetic.

This stuff is all really great. I'm going to have to start a journal. I'm so proud of myself for doing this. You all don't really know me, but I'm a big wimp. Go me!

### caroline — 2012-09-11T08:26:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-50916>

well - I am good to go! I just went to the chinese store and bought a package of small frozen fish. I have lemons and coriander and a bottle of red wine to wash it all down! Now I just have to wait to be really hungry. Anyone else in? Cavemam?? We really have to just do this and get it over with before Inger disowns us for our wimpiness!!!! ### Primal Dream — 2012-09-12T00:01:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-51068>

> **janagram;51448 wrote:** please tell us your recipe exactly, i.e., whole lemons?
>
>
>
> (Holy \*\*\*\*! I can't believe I just asked this!!!)

If you mean my FSH (non-mackerel fish): OK, I used 2 heads, they were bigger than I thought they were. Sorry, I didn't take pictures but I will next time. The fish was scup? Never heard of it but the fishmonger said it was like grouper.

So I got out my kitchen shears and started cutting the heads. It was not easy. Lots of cartilege. Next time I will use my kevlar gloves I use for shucking oysters. So it took a while. So I got out 3 lemons and juiced them. Put the juice and pulp in the blender (vitamix).

Just to make sure it was lemony, I put in a glug of lemon flavored CLO. Then some sea salt. And a couple of ice cubes.

I thought that the vitamix was going to break down. After I thought it might be ok, it looked really, really thick. So I added a bit of water. It was still thick and felt warm. I did not want warm. So I got some more ice and a bit more water. the result was still pudding like.

I got my courage up to taste it and it was lemony and not at all fishy. there was a lot of it. I filled 2 8 oz glasses and ate it with a spoon.

I really like the idea of adding cilantro. I'll do that next time. This fishmonger was at a farmers market and they are opening up a real store tomorrow. We really need another seafood retailer.

Priscilla

### philerup — 2012-09-13T10:00:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-51395>

glad that people are starting to go to the Asian markets and get real food. The problem with most traditional American supermarkets and groceries is that they fix up the food to make it more salable and only include muscle meats etc.

I have found in all of the Asian markets that it is essentially an Epi-Paleo paradise. You can get whole fish, bones of any kinds, seaweed of all kinds, whole parts of animals including offal. That is just my input. I will never shop at a local grocery for this kind of stuff again.

### philerup — 2012-09-13T10:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-51396>

Chicken feet rock! I know Dr. K doesn't approve of fowl, but the collagen that you get from chicken feet when making broth is unlike anything else you can get. I make sure to make chicken feet broth regularly.

### Qodesh — 2012-09-14T20:41:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-51668>

The scup, Stenotomus chrysops, is a fish which occurs primarily in the Atlantic from Massachusetts to South Carolina. Along with many other fish of the family Sparidae, it is also commonly known as porgy.

They are fished for by commercial and recreational fishermen. **They are a fine fish to eat because of their light flavor** and are sometimes called panfish.

Primal Dream's fish has a light flavor. This could make it easier in our "beginner's" taste buds?

### nonchalant — 2012-09-14T21:55:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-51675>

I recommend Primal Dream's pudding idea. I just had my second MHS, using one mackerel head. This was a different type of mackerel than I had before. I made sure to include the thyroid and liver this time. I added cilantro and limes. The first spoonful was awful. I ended up adding a third lime. I remembered to hold my nose. Wish I had more cilantro! I managed to get more spoonfuls down, spacing them out between kitchen cleanup. But a funny thing happened after I had eaten about half of it. I started realizing that it didn't taste so bad after all. It was like my body (mind?) was overruling my taste buds. I didn't have to hold my nose. I ended up scraping the bowl for the last spoonful. It still didn't taste good, but I wanted it.

It was better, for me, than holding my nose and chugging it down.

### Primal Dream — 2012-09-16T01:45:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-51875>

OK, I'm sitting here with my MHS. Real mackerel this time. What a difference! The scup was more difficult to cut, but it was a 'cleaner' head -- don't know how to describe it. And those mackerel teeth are sharp. Ouch! I forgot to put on my kevlar gloves.

At any rate, it was a mess.

Scup is a lighter fish. This tastes gamier, if you know what I mean. it's still not bad, just not as tasty as the scup. And yes, Qodesh, it's from the Atlantic -- my fish guys fish off of the Hamptons, Long Island.

So: 1 mackerel head plus liver etc. 2 lemons, juiced, with pulp. cilantro -- a big handful. A glug of lemony CLO. And 4 ice cubes. Not bad, but I liked the scup better.

I'm still proud of myself.

Priscilla

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-16T01:53:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-51877>

MHS=BG12. Just saying

### caroline — 2012-09-18T03:57:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-52271>

Well ......I finally had my MHS! It really isn't bad at all. All I could really taste is lemon and corriander. I just used 1 whole frozen fish - about 6 inches long. I was really worried about getting it smooth enough because my food processor is crap so I used a hand held immersion blender and it worked great - except - I didn't notice that the lid wasn't on properly until it was too late. Lets just say - I had to change all my clothes!!! I have had a big couple of weeks - swimming in the ocean, eating fresh oysters and lots of other fish and now Inger's cocktail Inger is so right - just do it. It is also pretty nice to have a few swigs of red wine after your cocktail first thing in the morning. Every cloud does have a silver lining! I am really having a hard time eating beef now - does not appeal at all.

### Stavity — 2012-09-18T15:20:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-i-wait-so-long.3572/post-52332>

> **Jack Kruse;52491 wrote:** MHS=BG12. Just saying

Guess I will need to start looking for fresh Mackerel for smoothies. I have been in serious trouble, health wise, for years and need to do what is good for my brain.

# Thread: When inlaws give pretzels as a present....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hope2conceive — 2012-09-11T02:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50857>

Anyone have any ideas for what to do when inlaws give your husband a huge plastic jar of peanut filled PRETZELS?? I just want to scream.....because they know how important diet is to me so of all the things to give my hubby as a present for his birthday, they pick THAT!

Though he's not on the Leptin Rx, I try to have as healthy foods around as possible and incorporate as many of the principles in my cooking even for him which thankfully he is open to and is enjoying....I know I can't control others and what they do. It just makes me so frustrated. Maybe I just need to vent....

What do you guys do about stuff like this? Not stress I am sure....

### nonchalant — 2012-09-11T03:07:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50864>

Re-gift! Take it to the office/church/school. Donate to charity.

Yeah, it's bad stuff, but the recipients would just be eating other grains if not for the pretzels.

### caroline — 2012-09-11T03:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50870>

I think a lot of people think pretzels are a healthy option. Just a thought.......... and venting is good! I cannot get a very dear friend to understand that I don't do wheat at all and she thinks everyone should have some sugar - need it for energy she says. Even tho she is very supportive she often thinks that just a little of something won't hurt. Now I just say no thank you - firmly - and grab an apple altho somedays I am winding up with way too many apples!!! She is a devoted follower of weight watchers and moderation is their mantra in all things.

### nonchalant — 2012-09-11T03:18:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50873>

Yeah, moderation. So you can get moderate relief from cancer/diabetes/heart disease...

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T03:33:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50879>

Don't be annoyed - gifting "treats" is such a part of our culture - you just can't expect everyone to be on our page. The tendrils of the idea of "moderation" and "now and then won't hurt" and "ok to do on a special occasion" go so deep, it would be an exercise in futility to expect others to really get it.

I think it is ok to smile and nod and show appreciation for thoughtfulness that WAS there. I really bet it wasn't purchased with "going against your wishes" in mind. Take it in the spirit which it was given.

hehe I know some will disagree, but that's my take - Family, goodwill, enjoyable moments are valuable to our health as well - why generate negativity that takes away from your health even further?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-11T04:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50890>

> **Nonchalant;51424 wrote:** Re-gift! Take it to the office/church/school. Donate to charity.
>
> Yeah, it's bad stuff, but the recipients would just be eating other grains if not for the pretzels.

That is real christian.......LOL

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-11T04:45:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50891>

People when they buy gifts don't necessarily think what you might like - they just want to buy something quick and get it over with. Rarely are people thoughtful with these kinds of gifts.

In 20+ years of severe anorexia, I was still being gifted with chocolates at all times. My very best friend would do this. Heck, my own boyfriend did. They knew I wasn't going to touch any of it. Yet it's the "accepted" thing to do, so they did. And everybody else, too.

What I'm saying is that gifts like these are symbolic. They have a meaning different from the actual item being given.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T07:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50910>

Make a wall decoration of them:

Take the pretzels, glue them to a large piece of posterboard in a design like a kaleidoscope. Add a little acryllic paint in the holes, like stained glass. Spray with varathane, so they don't drop salt on everything, and so that eating them is OUT OF THE QUESTION!

### hope2conceive — 2012-09-11T20:09:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-50989>

Y'all this is too funny. And Ealachan you're right - that's the issue....they gave it to my DH, not me, and why? Because he visited them before, ate them there and loved them (UGH! Probably because I don't buy this stuff...his snacks are usually trail mix, sometimes dark chocolate, and sometimes popcorn) so he loves it when away.

His family isn't all that healthy - the bro and SIL were infertile, never had a baby but tried till she went into menopause. She takes a drug every day for migraine prevention...

Nothing much I can do about it except make healthy foods palatable at home. But this thing just irked me and it was good to vent. Thankfully DH has been letting me feed him lots of ghee, Kerrygold butter, and EVCO, GF meats, all kinds of seafood, nut butters and he gained a nice amount of weight (was underweight before)...and hasn't complained about the lack of pasta, rice, potatoes at home. So it's not all bad...incidentally, he totally loves grass fed meat (always been a lover of seafood but growing up never had 'red meat' because the mom thought it was bad!).....

Anyway, thanks for all the input. Totally love this group

### caroline — 2012-09-12T03:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-inlaws-give-pretzels-as-a-present.3579/post-51094>

See ...... venting does help!

### msclman — 2012-09-11T07:18:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-50908>

i went from 205 to 212 in one day of cheating. how is this even possible?? is it water weight?

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T17:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-50954>

yes. it also depends on what you ate. didn't you just start?

### msclman — 2012-09-11T18:23:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-50961>

half way thru the 40 days

very discouraging. takes such hard work to lose and if you slip on one or two meals you gain it all back

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T18:42:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-50971>

> **msclman;51528 wrote:** half way thru the 40 days
>
> very discouraging. takes such hard work to lose and if you slip on one or two meals you gain it all back

Suck it up, buttercup,

Heh, just wanted to give tough luv. Nothing to do about it now but hit the plan again immediately. Hopefully it will drop as fast as it came.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T21:45:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-51039>

a gain that large usually indicates a food intolerance

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-11T21:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-51041>

> **msclman;51471 wrote:** i went from 205 to 212 in one day of cheating. how is this even possible?? is it water weight?

its called inflammation from sub optimal cellular signaling.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T21:51:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-51042>

> **msclman;51547 wrote:** mmmm butter

Ok, if you are thinking of food like that - your dose is off. What/how much are you taking? Colleen would say skip a dose and see how you feel the next day.

This advice was a miracle worker for me. I fought the idea of skipping a dose for way to long (weeks!) but when I did it was like the sky opened up and angel's sang!

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-11T22:28:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-51053>

> **Cavemam;51613 wrote:** Ok, if you are thinking of food like that - your dose is off. What/how much are you taking? Colleen would say skip a dose and see how you feel the next day.
>
>
>
> This advice was a miracle worker for me. I fought the idea of skipping a dose for way to long (weeks!) but when I did it was like the sky opened up and angel's sang!

definitely concur with this advice if you are feeling hunger.

### msclman — 2012-09-13T02:54:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-51312>

doing 180 a day in the morning sub q in my abdomen. started at 140. skipped a day and got more hungry. thats why i went to 180. should i go to 200?

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-13T17:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-51442>

> **msclman;51897 wrote:** doing 180 a day in the morning sub q in my abdomen. started at 140. skipped a day and got more hungry. thats why i went to 180. should i go to 200?

nope, if you are hungry go down not up. it's counter intuitive but I would suggest skipping a day for dosing and if you are less hungry go down in your dose. if you manifest greater hunger consider upping then.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-13T18:36:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheated-one-day.3582/post-51451>

> **msclman;51897 wrote:** doing 180 a day in the morning sub q in my abdomen. started at 140. skipped a day and got more hungry. thats why i went to 180. should i go to 200?

the jump from 140 - 180 is too big. Make sure a week passed, then do another skip day. Going up or down should be from 10-20 at max

# Thread: I CANT eat cream anymore.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-eat-cream-anymore.3590/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Qodesh — 2012-09-11T17:58:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-eat-cream-anymore.3590/post-50958>

If I have heavy cream, I have stomach cramping about an hour after for a few hours. I'm nauseous and keep man-burping.

The 1/2&1/2 in coffee or cottage cheese has a very mild, hardly noticeable affect. I even had a milkshake about a week ago with NO problems.(I haven't had one of those in about 2 years)

I never had a problem with this until recently. Could it be the gut bacteria changing from high meat? And why some things are OK,........... but the HWC is killer!

After a few times of exploration and testing.................. I'm avoiding it.

Are those of you giving up HWC doing it _painfully_ voluntary? Or has anyone else had this happen to them and **had** to give it up to avoid pain?

### caroline — 2012-09-12T03:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-eat-cream-anymore.3590/post-51095>

I have had this happen too - I ate a bit of a really rich dish with heavy cream and a bit of a very rich dessert \[social setting\] and I was sick all nite and most of the next day.

### Michael — 2012-09-12T14:23:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-eat-cream-anymore.3590/post-51160>

> **Qodesh;51525 wrote:** If I have heavy cream, I have stomach cramping about an hour after for a few hours. ...
>
>
>
> I even had a milkshake about a week ago with NO problems.(I haven't had one of those in about 2 years).

I can't say I understand that. For most people if dairy products are problematic it seems to be either lactose or casein (or perhaps specific types of casein) that are responsible. The higher the level of fat, the lower that of these other components. Heavy cream ought to be better for most people, butter a little better than that, and ghee probably OK for most people.

So I don't know. Maybe it's the context in which you have it.

How are you with butter and ghee, since you didn't mention those?

Can you eat heavy goat's cream with no problem?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-12T14:55:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-eat-cream-anymore.3590/post-51170>

You should consider BCM-7 AB testing if that is the case.

### Michael — 2012-09-12T15:30:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-eat-cream-anymore.3590/post-51175>

> **Jack Kruse;51752 wrote:** You should consider BCM-7 AB testing if that is the case.

IOW, it's likely to be the source of his heavy cream.

While there's more casein in the milk in his milkshake, it may have come from a herd that quite by chance has more A2 cows?

[http://keithwoodford.wordpress.com/category/a1-and-a2-milk/](http://http://keithwoodford.wordpress.com/category/a1-and-a2-milk/)

### Qodesh — 2012-09-12T21:05:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-eat-cream-anymore.3590/post-51228>

wow! thanks. This makes sense to me. It explains why the different reactions from different milk sources. Butter has no problem for me.

I will stick with goat milk until I can find some A2 cows.

### Momma28 — 2012-09-18T02:51:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-eat-cream-anymore.3590/post-52254>

Hi Dr. K,

Is this a blood test? I read up on what BCM7 was and read that children with this can have cognitive impairment. My four year old deals wth this and I would like to have him tested. Is this repairable for him?

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-24T18:16:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/webinars-on-ipod.3591/post-53809>

B/c I know I'm going to forget it again I'm going to post my own instructions here  so my bad brain can find it:

putting webinars on iTunes:

\- download webinar (right click while holding down either option or control... can't remember ), wait for download to finish, click on webinar in downloads, it will open in quicktime

\- click on SHARE in the top bar in quicktime

\- choose iTunes

\- choose iPod/iPhone

\- export will start automatically

wait........ it takes a while. Once it's finished you will have exported the movie into your iTunes movie folder  You can now watch on your computer or put it on your iDevice of choice and make your dog happy with a walk while listening to it

### tellmisty — 2012-09-28T02:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/webinars-on-ipod.3591/post-54905>

We went out of our way to communicate the changes coming so that everyone could prepare for them. It is not our intention to gyp anyone out of what they were expecting; that's why we made announcements via email, during webinars, and in the forums--to give people the opportunity to buy consults and download webinars before the switch.

I had many, many members email me about downloading the webinars, and got a lot of confirmations that they were able to download all of the webinars. I was not aware of anyone who was actively trying to download webinars but was unsuccessful before the switch... I believed we helped every single person who reached out for help to ensure they were able to download successfully.

If you contacted me because you were having issues before the new site switched over and we did not successfully help you download, open a support ticket and we will consider the circumstances on a case-by-case basis.

If you did not reach out for help before the new site switched over, there is not anything I'm going to be able to do for you. I got HUNDREDS of emails leading up to this change, so I feel that we communicated well and were very available to helping anyone who struggled or reported issues.

Wow I felt like I never stopped talking. We recorded it and she will edit it and send you all an email blast when it goes live........as soon as you listen to it we will have a Q&A live........Misty will tell you when it goes live and then the Q & A is live after it.........We may release it earlier for a our foreing country members to shorten your time differences! We are trying new things out. New website should be live in the next two weeks too.......should be better!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-12T03:23:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-finished-the-epcotx-webinar-with-misty.3597/post-51097>

EPCOTx Protocol: Re-engineer Your Gut Flora

This month's webinar is going to introduce you to Jack's newest protocol, the EPCOTx Protocol. This protocol is specially-designed to help you reverse leptin resistance and heal your leaky gut, which as you all know, is the foundation for brain health. Leaky gut is basically tied to cellular oxidation and cellular aging. This protocol will help you rebuild your gut flora create the healthiest gut (which will give you the healthiest brain) possible. The EPCOT Protocol works because it helps lowers inflammation, reduce oxidation, and stay in reduced, pro-gestation pathway.

This protocol is especially effective in helping reverse:

\- Crohn's

\- SIBO

\- Candida, GERD or helicobacter infection

\- Skin problems

\- Eating Disorders

\- Hormone imbalances

\- Obesity

\- Infertility

\- Hashimotos

Dr. Jack will outline the fundamentals of this protocol and give us all very specific instructions on how to tweak our diets to achieve optimal results. We've jam-packed this webinar with a LOT of information you can use \*right now\* to fast-track you to optimal!

# Thread: Unable to sweat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-sweat.3606/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyha — 2012-09-12T16:10:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-sweat.3606/post-51181>

Hello. In the past year, I have gradually been feeling worse and worse to the point now where I feel awful everyday. I have very little energy and absolutely crash around 2:00 every day. Also, during the time, I lost my ability to sweat. I mean I cannot sweat at all. I am a 30 year old male who has always been athletic and energetic and had no problems sweating. In the past year or so, I have totally lost my ability to sweat, which coincides with me feeling horrible the past year. I was working outside on a deck this summer in 100 degree+ temps, and I didn't drop a bead of sweat. And the two guys I was working with were absolutely soaked all day and both mentioned that it can't be normal for me to not be sweating at all on a day like it was doing the work we were doing.

The two doctors I went to both said everything is fine, despite me feeling awful and having a long list of symptoms that I've never had in my life previously, such as:

\-inability to sweat

\-very low levels of energy

\-suddenly looking bloated and soft despite being very muscular and fit my entire previous life

\-low body temps (I avg. 97.5 degrees)

\-no sex drive (I mean none at all-despite being a 30 year old male)

\-very depressed/pessimistic toward all things in life

\-horrible acne (probably due to my inability to sweat and release toxins from my body)

I could go on forever. Also, I took some labs and my T3 came back low (2.5 on a 2.5 to 6.0 "normal" range) and my TSH high and out of range (3.5 on a range that says above 3.0 is high and out of range). Doesn't this along with my symptoms suggest I probably have some kind of thyroid problem? And why can't I sweat any longer?

### quelsen — 2012-09-12T16:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-sweat.3606/post-51183>

no sweating??? think lack of signal to keep water balance properly.... think a lack of aldosterone.... realize how low on the hormone chain that is ..... know your hormones are fraked

start with Vitamin D and DHEA and Pregnenolone....

if you cant afford labs eat 2 million iu of Vitamin D and start going to bed with the sun, take enough dhea to keep you asleep all night, work with pregnenolone until you get serious morning wood every freaking day....

take control and ignore dumb doctors who tell you everything is fine when you damn well know it isnt

### bennyha — 2012-09-13T04:32:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-sweat.3606/post-51370>

Thank you for your replies. I did get some lab work done and I have no idea how to interpret the results and where to start. My DHEA was in the very end of the high range according to my labs. My testosterone was low as was my T3 and my TSH was out of range high. I don't know where to start like I said. I was going to try and self medicate and take some T3 or Armour thyroid to see if I can improve. I have no insurance and lost my job so I can't waste any more money on doctors who tell me nothing is wrong with me when I know something is obviously clearly wrong. I read entire BG series but my brain is so foggy and this is so far over my head I don't quite understand everything. So confused and so tired of feeling this lousy. I can't make it to even 2:00 each day without completely crashing

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-13T05:20:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/unable-to-sweat.3606/post-51377>

> **bennyha;51763 wrote:**
>
> \-very low levels of energy
>
> \-suddenly looking bloated and soft despite being very muscular and fit my entire previous life
>
> \-low body temps (I avg. 97.5 degrees)

This definitely says thyroid problem to me but I am not a doc. Read all you can about it. [www.stopthethyroidmadness.com](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com) is a great resource website. There could be a multitude of reasons why you have a poorly functioning thyroid as well as the sweating issue. Another lab to test (I know you are low on funds but keep this in mind) is the TPO and TgAB antibodies test to see if you have the autoimmune version of thyroid disease, Hashimotos. You really need to find a doc...maybe at a local clinic where they charge based on your income???

Sounds like your testosterone is tanked too from pregnenolone steal syndrome (hence the low energy/depression)

# Thread: Epi-Paleo Impossible ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-impossible.3615/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Adria — 2012-09-12T20:57:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-impossible.3615/post-51226>

I've been allergic to multiple foods over 20yrs, Ige positive and elevated eosinophils. Tried every allergy treatment that exists and traveled to the top allergists in the US. I've been unable to sustain any allergy treatment for long without developing a severe reaction.

Since reading about epi-paleo I've tried shrimp, cod, lake trout, whitefish, salmon, tuna and had either an allergic reaction or anaphylaxis. Ironically.. I live on Lake Superior and have access to fresh fish daily. I also test positive to crab & lobster but haven't tried them for years.

Otherwise I'm at a normal weight. I sleep 9-10hrs and get natural sunlight and CT. I have no symptoms of hormone issues or any signs of menopause. I take probiotics and try to follow the suggestions on the leaky gut forum.

Any advice for me?

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-12T21:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/epi-paleo-impossible.3615/post-51242>

yes......go to an alergist and get tested and ask about desensitivity training.......but when I hear this story I think low DHEA, Hashmoto's or some other oxidizing gut condition is present like an AI.

# Thread: Is there such a thing as a healthy balance?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-a-healthy-balance.3616/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 30
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-09-12T21:18:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-a-healthy-balance.3616/post-51233>

I find myself seriously stressing out lately over not being absolutely perfect with CT and minding the light cycle. Thankfully diet is not an issue for me at all. Ever since I started on a real foods diet over two years ago when I began GAPS I can honestly say I havenâ€™t cheated once. Following the light cycle is proving to be a bit more difficult for me though and Iâ€™m afraid Iâ€™m so used to striving for perfection that every little failure is stressing me out to the point of detrimental health.

When I believe in something I go â€œhog wildâ€ with it. In my mind, there is no point in putting time and effort into anything unless youâ€™re going to give it all youâ€™ve got. I still wholeheartedly believe in that, but Iâ€™m beginning to wonder if itâ€™s possible to achieve some sort of healthy balance. I agree with Jack that moderation is not optimal and I donâ€™t want to shoot for mediocrity, but I donâ€™t know how to live life in this day and age and stick 100% by the light cycle and get in my CT. Iâ€™ve been doing a 30-minute cold bath every morning before work, but had to skip a day this week and a day last week because I had to go out of town and had to leave the house much earlier than normal. I found myself in near tears over it, feeling like a complete failure! Maybe some of itâ€™s just hormones, I donâ€™t know! Iâ€™m also stressed over the fact that I still canâ€™t manage more than 25-30 minutes in a 50-degree tub. I know itâ€™s related to my low T3 that I just canâ€™t seem to get figured out, but it still adds to the stress.

My main concern is dealing with the light cycle. Seriously, how does one actually LIVE in the modern world and not be exposed to light after 5 PM in the winter? Honestly Iâ€™d probably love nothing more than to be in a deep, dark, cold cave with just my family and not deal with the outside world, but thatâ€™s just not practical. We actually have been doing really well the last 2 weeks with implementing candles at sunset, no fake light/electronics, bedtime soon after, etc, etc. BUT with school just starting and my kids having activities they go to in the evenings and winter juts around the corner I donâ€™t know how weâ€™ll be able to continue that. Do I tell the kids they canâ€™t participate in any activities? They do things that help them grow and enjoy life in other ways so I donâ€™t think thatâ€™s an option. My son does karate and just started cub scouts. My daughter does a weekly book club, dance and participates in a youth small group that I actually lead. These are all worthwhile and enriching activities for them. For the most part theyâ€™re home by 7:30-8:00, so itâ€™s not even super late, but of course theyâ€™re going to be exposed to fluorescent lights the whole time theyâ€™re away from home.

I know my hormones are off now, so that could explain my needing to vent and my total stressing out here  I guess Iâ€™m just curious how others plan to manage it. If it were just me, I think I could easily give it all up, but I canâ€™t do that to the kids. I want them to be healthy, but I want them to enjoy life too. Make sense? Not to mention my daughter is at a really critical point socially. She just started middle school and I think itâ€™s a tremendous help to make new friends who share similar interests. There has to be a balance somewhere, right?

### bigknitwit — 2012-09-12T22:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-a-healthy-balance.3616/post-51245>

No real advice to give you, but I feel the same pressures/issues, and haven't resolved them for my household either. I'll be watching this thread...

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-12T23:45:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-a-healthy-balance.3616/post-51261>

> **BJK77;51816 wrote:** I find myself seriously stressing out lately over not being absolutely perfect with CT and minding the light cycle. Thankfully diet is not an issue for me at all. Ever since I started on a real foods diet over two years ago when I began GAPS I can honestly say I havenâ€™t cheated once. Following the light cycle is proving to be a bit more difficult for me though and Iâ€™m afraid Iâ€™m so used to striving for perfection that every little failure is stressing me out to the point of detrimental health.
>
>
>
> When I believe in something I go â€œhog wildâ€ with it. In my mind, there is no point in putting time and effort into anything unless youâ€™re going to give it all youâ€™ve got. I still wholeheartedly believe in that, but Iâ€™m beginning to wonder if itâ€™s possible to achieve some sort of healthy balance. I agree with Jack that moderation is not optimal and I donâ€™t want to shoot for mediocrity, but I donâ€™t know how to live life in this day and age and stick 100% by the light cycle and get in my CT. Iâ€™ve been doing a 30-minute cold bath every morning before work, but had to skip a day this week and a day last week because I had to go out of town and had to leave the house much earlier than normal. I found myself in near tears over it, feeling like a complete failure! Maybe some of itâ€™s just hormones, I donâ€™t know! Iâ€™m also stressed over the fact that I still canâ€™t manage more than 25-30 minutes in a 50-degree tub. I know itâ€™s related to my low T3 that I just canâ€™t seem to get figured out, but it still adds to the stress.
>
>
>
> My main concern is dealing with the light cycle. Seriously, how does one actually LIVE in the modern world and not be exposed to light after 5 PM in the winter? Honestly Iâ€™d probably love nothing more than to be in a deep, dark, cold cave with just my family and not deal with the outside world, but thatâ€™s just not practical. We actually have been doing really well the last 2 weeks with implementing candles at sunset, no fake light/electronics, bedtime soon after, etc, etc. BUT with school just starting and my kids having activities they go to in the evenings and winter juts around the corner I donâ€™t know how weâ€™ll be able to continue that. Do I tell the kids they canâ€™t participate in any activities? They do things that help them grow and enjoy life in other ways so I donâ€™t think thatâ€™s an option. My son does karate and just started cub scouts. My daughter does a weekly book club, dance and participates in a youth small group that I actually lead. These are all worthwhile and enriching activities for them. For the most part theyâ€™re home by 7:30-8:00, so itâ€™s not even super late, but of course theyâ€™re going to be exposed to fluorescent lights the whole time theyâ€™re away from home.
>
>
>
> I know my hormones are off now, so that could explain my needing to vent and my total stressing out here  I guess Iâ€™m just curious how others plan to manage it. If it were just me, I think I could easily give it all up, but I canâ€™t do that to the kids. I want them to be healthy, but I want them to enjoy life too. Make sense? Not to mention my daughter is at a really critical point socially. She just started middle school and I think itâ€™s a tremendous help to make new friends who share similar interests. There has to be a balance somewhere, right?

I think the first step is evaluating (and getting honest) with what you want to do right now. For some of us, the weakness will be diet, for others CT, for others light, etc etc. You are 100% on diet, but you are not ambivalent about this issue. There are many others who are (taste, cost, giving up things they love, etc)... and they will construct a similar argument about why they shouldn't stick 100% to the diet.

\>For me, the point is to get aligned with yourself. The stress comes (I think) when we are pushing ourselves to do something we don't want to. It sounds to me like only part of you wants to be perfect with CT and lights; another part of you wants to prioritise other things. This is NOT a judgement. I think you need to know where you are at. Then you have a starting point to work from.

So... for example... you say to yourself 'these evening activities for my children are important to them and to me and I want to support them happening'. Then you can do things like get blue-blocking glasses or just start with candles/darkness when you arrive home, etc. If you deny the truth of what is important to you (realising that this may change over time) then you'll always be fighting with yourself (I am speaking from experience here!).

I really believe that ANYTHING we want to make happen, we can. We can live in our 'modern' world however we want. My family is proof that you can make it through a winter and many other months without any modern electricity or screens after dusk. But... my weakness is CT. I still dislike cold and I had to modify how I did CT over winter because I already live in a cold, unheated house and getting into an ice bath was sending my own stress sky high. That doesn't mean I couldn't have made it happen. But I didn't want to. So I went back to face dunks and taking walks without a jacket. It that perfect? Hell no. Did it work for me? It did.

I don't think you need to compare yourself to anyone else nor justify your lack of perfection. There are a lot of variables and it may help to put them in order of priority and then see what fits.

One last comment: I prioritised making sure that I got my own needs met. I was/am the sickest and the one who has the most ground to make up. And like that blog MJ wrote, I'd put my own needs aside. So when my children had evening activities, they went. I, however, did not go pick them up - I arranged for other parents to do that. I just wasn't willing to compromise for myself, but was willing to work out a compromise for them. Does that make sense?

If it's causing you stress, it's probably a signal that you are a) being too hard on yourself (which is not the same thing as being totally committed to a goal) and b) not aligned to what you really want right now.

Hang in there. We're all in this together

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-13T00:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-a-healthy-balance.3616/post-51269>

I think you need to stop thinking in negatives and start thinking in positives.

If you fill your mind with all the things you can't, won't, don't want to do then your life with look exactly like that. Try it the other way around and watch yourself become happy and successful at what you do.

The other thing I want to say is that I think some people need to stop idealizing Dr K as the Optimal Angel that he isn't. He's got family, he's got kids, he's got a job that requires quite a few Optimal violations as far as I can judge that  Dr K uses computers at night, he works under bright artificial light a lot, he's got a less-than-optimal past. The difference is that he makes the best of it with the options at his hands - adapts - and doesn't get his cortisol up about the rest. Maybe he takes an extra dose of Resveratrol to make up for the rest

There's some things we don't have control over. Make your best choice, be happy, and hope for the best.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-13T01:44:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-a-healthy-balance.3616/post-51286>

your perspective colors your reality.......and your perspective is not a healthy one to get to optimal. The journey is the part to experience. Instead of making this a task make it a challenging game to get back what you have lost. That is really what the primal sense blog post is all about. Thoughts can change our DNA. If your thoughts suck.......well you can figure out the rest.

### [email redacted] — 2012-09-13T02:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-a-healthy-balance.3616/post-51302>

ok, and now for a lesser opinion.

I think you are on a path and your speed along that path involves too many variables for anybody but you to judge what is acceptable. I "chilled the \*\*\*\* out", and let me tell you sister - it is the best move for me. I have found it important for my perspective to back up and try to live today in a reasonable way. If you are internally flipping out when you can't do what you "think" you need to do, it MIGHT be a sign that you need to figure out a way to adjust your life.

I realized, the adjustments I had to make were not more restrictions and getting freaked out over missing a bath or workout or BAB. Back up and take a 30K foot view of your life. If you are near tears and can't enjoy the good things in life that you DO have (interaction with family? whatever it is) then that doesn't sound right either.

I have been there on the "complete freak out over something petty". You can just give this up.

My perspective is that is unhealthy and inflammation generating. . and who knows what else you make worse with this.

I know "moderation" is a dirty word around here - but your life is a complex quilt, you can't just pull out one panel and only cover up with that. You have to have the entire quilt sewn together and in balance before you can snuggle up under it. Sometimes that means you give attention to health, sometimes it means you skip a bath for something else.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-13T02:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-such-a-thing-as-a-healthy-balance.3616/post-51307>

I learned something recently that I will share as it has helped me do some things I either didn't think I could do or didn't want to do. Everyone must come up with their personal "WHY?"? Why are you going to swim the English channel or summit Mount Everest or choose a particular lifestyle component or why you dip your body in a cold ice bath or .....? What is the real reason, it may be underneath the surface, that you are choosing to do something. It may be for your health or at least that is what you think it's for but maybe deep down it's because you want to be able to play with your unborn grandbabies. Ask yourself WHY are you doing something, get an answer and then ask yourself WHY to that answer to help you go deeper. I learned the "Five WHYS" while getting my MBA but honestly I have used that technique more to get in touch with me.
