### LinD — 2012-11-17T17:45:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-64949>

i came across something on the STTM site that concerns me....

_"Why does my body produce RT3 (Reverse T3)? Your body, especially the liver, can constantly be converting T4 to RT3 as a way to get rid of any unneeded T4. In any given day, it’s stated that 40% of T4 goes to T3 and 60% of T4 goes to Reverse T3.

But in any situation where your body needs to conserve energy and focus on something else, it will change the above percentages, changing the conversion of RT3 to 50% or more, and the T3 goes down, down. Examples are emotional, physical, or biological stress, such as being chronically or acutely sick (the flu, pneumonia, etc), after surgery, after a car accident or any acute injury, chronic stress causing high cortisol, **being exposed to an extremely cold environment**, diabetes, aging, or even being on drugs like beta blockers and amiodarone. But there’s another reason for thyroid patients."_

[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/reverse-t3/](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/reverse-t3/)

Would the CT exacerbate this then?? or the cold pack I'm holding against my neck (right at the thyroid site! Eeek!)

Thoughts?

### LinD — 2012-11-17T23:03:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-65007>

I noticed this when looking for side-effects of liothyrinine:

This medicine usually takes several weeks to have a noticeable effect on your condition. Until it begins to work, you may experience no change in your symptoms. Check with your doctor if the following symptoms continue:

Clumsiness; coldness; constipation; dry, puffy skin; listlessness; muscle aches; sleepiness; tiredness; weakness; weight gain

Other effects may occur if the dose of the medicine is not exactly right. These side effects will go away when the dose is corrected. Check with your doctor if any of the following symptoms occur:

Changes in appetite; changes in menstrual periods; diarrhea; fever; hand tremors; headache; increased sensitivity to heat; irritability; leg cramps; nervousness; sweating; trouble in sleeping; vomiting; weight loss.

I had some leg cramps today, but not at the moment.... plus a headache. Oh come on! It's only my second day.... And only 2.5 mcg. Plus I'm bleeding now. About 10 days early. I am very regular so this is odd.

vkiernan.... Notice the puffy skin listing.

>
>
> I listened to three of Paul Robinson's Recovering with T3 YouTube videos... something in the information makes me think that might not fit me, but I don't know yet. I'm a newbie here. The fact that my morning cortisol result with ZRT labs was 10.3 (rante is 3.7-9.5) and was marked 'H' makes me think my issues are a little different. Maybe not. It has been stressful with our two moves (selling home of 17 years, living in an apartment for three months, finally moving into new house; also two days before closing, I found out I have a small meniscus tear. Oh, and I've had M.S. for 22+ years.)
>
>
>
> The doctor didn't think the 4X test was needed at this time, so I'll forego it for now and revisit it later, if needed.
>
>
>
> Meanwhile, just reading again with new eyes. I bet I am past due for getting some CT in, huh? I haven't done any since about May, maybe before.

Mornin', did some reading and he says to not take t3 earlier than your usual wakeup time as you don't want to increase the morning cortisol if already high. So, you can do his protocol of t3 only but just not take it before normal wakeup time. The book did help me to understand that sometimes in the morning you need a larger dose of t3 for it to be able to get into your cells since you have been without it for so long; then you slowly taper down thruout the day. I started doing that and the early am dosing and I do believe it is starting to work. He does say that everybody is different and you have to find your own n=1. I was doing more static dosing and not feeling much but since I changed methods a bit, good things are starting to happen. My doc wants me to still take the sr t3 just to tide me over but I really don't think it is doing much. I want to try without it but then again since I am starting to feel better, I don't want to change things either.

I wonder if it's related? I'll add the cal/mag back.in. Already take many forms of magnesium. Will continue.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I had a thyroid panel done recently after going on 25 mcg. T3 (generic cytomel) for 6 wks. TSH went from about 6.5 down to 4.0 but the fT3 was doubled the high range max at 8.9 (LabCorp). It use to be below 3. I did take my daily dose in the morning about 2 hrs before the blood draw as per the instructions (at least my interpretation). This seems crazy high but after reading some info elsewhere it seems not untypical with T3 only program. I don't have hyper symptoms even with one dose in the AM and don't seem to have obvious lows at other times. Just a bit of cold issues sometimes with feet and hands but other symptoms are gone. Other hormones seem OK but I have pushed up DHEA towards optimum. I've never done rT3 testing but Jack never indicated that that would be particularly useful. CRP and cortisol are normal.

I would appreciate input from anybody that has experience or thoughts that might be relevant. I am assuming my body will be adjusting for awhile after years of dysfunction.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-11-26T15:28:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-65645>

> **Jonathin said**
> I thought I would jump to this thread now that I have my name back.
>
>
>
> I had a thyroid panel done recently after going on 25 mcg. T3 (generic cytomel) for 6 wks. TSH went from about 6.5 down to 4.0 but the fT3 was doubled the high range max at 8.9 (LabCorp). It use to be below 3. I did take my daily dose in the morning about 2 hrs before the blood draw as per the instructions (at least my interpretation). This seems crazy high but after reading some info elsewhere it seems not untypical with T3 only program. I don't have hyper symptoms even with one dose in the AM and don't seem to have obvious lows at other times. Just a bit of cold issues sometimes with feet and hands but other symptoms are gone. Other hormones seem OK but I have pushed up DHEA towards optimum. I've never done rT3 testing but Jack never indicated that that would be particularly useful. CRP and cortisol are normal.
>
> I would appreciate input from anybody that has experience or thoughts that might be relevant. I am assuming my body will be adjusting for awhile after years of dysfunction.

Two hours after you take T3 is approx when it peaks so that is the highest it would be in your system.Â  When I did my last set of labs I didn't take my T3 in the morn and it was 2.1, at the very bottom of the range.Â  Next time I do it I'm going to take it two hours before and see where that lands and know that will be my peak!Â  And you are correct that when on a T3 only protocol your "ideal" can be anywhere in the range.Â  However, it's best to be aware of when you took the meds and the results so you have an idea where you are at lab-wise.Â  Symptoms are ery important: heart-rate, temp, bp.Â  If all those are normal and you feel good, then you are most likely OK.Â  My doc also checks things like my reflexes to make sure I am not showing any hyper signs I may be unaware of.

Do you take the 25mg all at once or split up the dose?

### Jonathin — 2012-11-27T13:18:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-65699>

Thanks Patty and Vkiernan for the replies and questions.

I don't have any hyper symptoms that I am aware of so I think 25 mcg is not too much. 5 mcg barely moved TSH down and did nothing for upping fT3. Perhaps I could go higher with my dose if my conservative doc would allow the prescription but I am not sure I am in a rush to push things at the moment. For good measure I have now split my dose for when I get up and then mid-afternoon. I try to dissolve it under my tongue. I know where Jack want to see thyroid numbers but I must say that Dr Lindner at hormonerestoration.com has given me food for thought regarding test numbers vs. symptoms. I must say that after 15 yrs of what I now see as slowly developing hypothyroid symptoms, I am not quite sure exactly what optimal should feel like regarding thyroid function. To make things a bit unique I have always been slender and active and at 57 have not had other obvious chronic health issues altho there are some low level issues that have improved or are improving after going full paleo (largely epi-paleo) 6 months ago. My dream is for my thyroid to recover to the level of need no supplemental hormone. For now I am grateful to feel significant improvement.

### fitness@home — 2012-11-29T13:54:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-65847>

I started the T3 protocol on October 14 and am NOT doing the adrenal method. My morning cortisol is a little on the high side. This is how I am dosing:

4 am - 25 mcg ( on weekends around 6 am)
9 am - 12.5 mcg
2 pm - 12.5 mcg
6/7 pm - 12.5 mcg

I am due to bump up again tomorrow and will increase the 2 pm to 25 mcg. Then I plan on holding at 75mcg per day until the receptors clear. Have been tracking temps and my usual daily pattern is:

97.6
98
98.4
97.9

Pulse varies. Has been as low as 64 and the high was 84. My energy has been pretty steady. Sleep is good ( I was worried it would get worse). Weight is holding steady...had hoped to lose a few lbs { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. ### megwilliams4@gmail.c — 2012-12-03T12:31:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-66223>

Shijin13.....

I have been doing n=1 experiments with armour & cytomel and I can only tell you what is working for me. I take 2.5 mg Cytomel & 1/2 a grain of armour at 6 am. at 10 am and 2 pm I take the same. At 6pm I am starting to want "something" so I am testing to see what amount of wht I want. Crazily- I find that sometimes as I lay down at night sometimes I "want some" (sound like an addict?) so I will knaw off a little cytomel. My Dr had me try T4 & T3 seperately but I did not like it.

My gut says I need more t3 so I will slowly be upping that at each dosage. It is a pain to dose every 4 hours but that is what I do best on.

What do you mean by hot? Taking your temp hot or just feel hot? Does it stay with you or go away quickly like a flash? Where is your RT3 now?

The sign for me that I need more meds is I feel my heart beating loudly in my chest. Not fast, just pronounced. When this happens I can take meds and almost instantly feel my heart calming. Don't know if any of this helps. I am learning by trying things and going with my gut and then testing.

Its weird - I've boggled Patty for several months b/c my symptoms don't fully call out hypo or hyper. Â The HEAT/Hot happens w/in 2 hrs of taking my Armour in the AM. - its like the Armour has kicked in and off my thyroid goes. Â In reality I feel like I'm idiling. Â  Â There are evenings, especially when we get a wet cold in the NOVA - where I can't warm up AT ALL. Â  But then there are other times the only way I can cool down is getting into the CT Stock tank - but here's the weird thing - I don't get cold when the water temp is below 45 deg, but I get cold when its above 45 - btwn 54-55 deg?Â 

I feel like my body is telling my thyroid - hey make more T4 for T3 conversion - but its like I'm not quite making enough... I always feel better in winter - when its colder, its like I'm warmer then...but in the past in the summer I'd be wearing cashmere sweaters andÂ woolÂ pants in DC when its 98 degrees out ...this past summer being the only exception...

I'm a bit of an oddity... All of this links back to my PCOS, and migraines. Â We discovered that this summer I'm very sensitive to light changes. Â This fall, when my thyroid started kicking back into gear - my Pg dropped, and my migraines kicked up BIG Time...Â 

Â 

Viki -when I say hot, I feel flush/hot/sweaty/sometimes w/nausea - its over whelming Â - body temp during these spurts runs btwn 97.9-86.2 - Its Â litterally like my metabolism is attempting to "flip the switch" Â  when I describe it to my post-menopausal friends - they're response is not a hot flash. Â its weird. Â I don't get the heart palpatations. Â I get the picture like there's a switch that's trying to connect - but its shorting out... the relay isn't connecting - and instead I'm burning up...

### fitness@home — 2012-12-03T13:57:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-66231>

Gretchen, are your iron and cortisol level labs within normal range? You didn't post anything for them. The human body is such a complex thing! There are so many factors to consider.

After confessing to my doc that I did the T3 (over the internet) to help clean up my adrenals and rT3 problem she wrote me a script for the Cytomel but wants me to watch my dose since non-brand from Turkey may be lower (I think it is).Â  I'm looking forward to trying the real stuff and see where my dose ends up considering it may be weaker.Â  She said that 25 mcg of T3 equate to around 1 to 1-1/2 grains of NDT.Â  Not sure if this is correct but that is what she told me.

She also said that there are reports of NP Thyroid being contaminated with gluten somehow.Â  She has had patients that have gone hypo after going on that med only to find out that their labs show gluten when they are not eating any.Â  Just thoguht I'd throw that out there in case anyone is having issues with it.Â  She said Erfa from Canada, Naturethroid, and Armour seem to be the ones that are good at this moment.

### vkiernan — 2012-12-03T21:50:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-66271>

> **SueC said
>
> > vkiernan said
>
> **
>
> > Just wanted to note that my rt3 finally cleared on Thanksgiving day of all days.Â  2 months and 4 days.Â  I went hyper and my poor heart was going crazy; first time ever.Â Â It was around 61 in the house and I was in a tank and sweating and telling dh to not start a fire.Â  Quite a strange feeling but a very welcome one.Â Â Things finally settled down around 12:30 pm and my last dose was around 2:30 am.Â Â Seeing my doc this Friday so I am hoping she will do labs to verify.
>
> What do you mean by "Rt3 finally cleared"? I've never heard the term...
>
> Â 
>
> In a normal person, some t4 converts to t3 and excess converts to rt3 and your body clears it out of your system.Â  In some people, like me, my t4 converts to a lot more rt3 than ft3 and myÂ body can't rid itself of all of it.Â  Since I make too much rt3 it inhibits the t3 from getting into my cells.Â  This means that any ft3 I might have can't do me a bit of good.Â  So,Â there are a few protocols out there thatÂ have been proven to clear the rt3 and allow the t3 to get into the cells.Â Â This is what I have been doing for the last several months.Â Â You mightÂ make too much rt3 if you have a ferritin issue orÂ if you have an adrenal issue and I'm sure there are more that I just can't think of now.Â  One caveat though is, if you have a rt3 problem, you need to find out what is causing it so you can fix that problem the caused it in the first place.Â  For myself, I know I had/have adrenal fatigue and I have been working on that but also in the mean time, I would like to clear the rt3 if possible.Â  STTM has a lot of good info on this.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-06T08:40:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-66540>

Patty in just on 60mg of Armour. Not sure why TSH is still trying to kicking but I think it might be b/c I've been this way for yrs w/o treatment. B/c labs seemed to be in "normal range" & my symptoms were limited (not counting problems loosing weight or being cold in summer).

Waiting on the new rx to arrive will retest in 6 weeks. Moving up to 90mg armour. Might need more. Guess we'll see where I'm at. If TSH still doesn't come down obviously we increase but will need to look at other underlying issues. The good thing. At least I now have documentation that I infact do have a thyroid problem. Which could have been a big contributor to my crazy cholesterol #s. this isn't going to fix over night. But at least I'm moving fwd.

Patty,

Interestingly, it happened with the last batch.Â  You might be on to something concerning the filler;Â although, they were supposed to be made with probiotics but who knows what really got used.Â  Anyway, yesterday wasn't as bad. 2 changes occurred, 1) new thyroid med and 2)Â I upped to 95 mcg.Â  I will hold there today and see where things go.Â  Temp is still low but higher today than yesterday.Â  Pulse seems fine.Â  Never goes too high anyway.Â  The fluttery thing seems to happen about every 3 hours.Â  I did take the t3 a couple times yesterday when it started.Â  Seemed to go away.Â  I am wondering since t3 is finally getting into my cells (sure hope that's the case) that maybe I am using more right now since my body is so starved and maybe it is helping repair things.

I would really like to know what determines how much t3 a person needs.Â  Can't be weight or I wouldn't need this much so its got to be something cellular going on.Â  If that's the case, then maybe I won't need so much down the road.Â  Hoping anyway.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-06T13:16:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-66557>

Vicki my doc told me that she has had many patients that don't like the compounded T3.Â  They feel it doesn't work as well.Â  I'm not so sure about that and have no experience myself.Â  I know when I got my Pg compounded I had to specify that I wanted the probiotics in it and not whatever else they use.Â  The pharmacy had to order my filler so I know they don't use it with anyone else and that could be why people were having issues.

Â 

I think you are on the right track.Â  Keep paying attention to what is going on inside of you and the fluttery thing will resolve itself with a few tweaks I'd bet.Â  Also if you are getting the T3 you need your body may be better at making the other hormones (LDL+T3=hormones).Â  The amount a person needs really depends on so many factors that everyone has to figure that out for themselves with symptoms and labs. I know from reading STTM that people generally are on 75-150mcg but I have seen others who are on 250mcg and are our same size.Â  Apparently they have thyroid resistance (not sure what that even is).Â  I think the cells are not taking in the T3 but I think they have other issues that need to be cleared up.Â  I'm sure they are not paleo much less epi-paleo!Â  My doc thought my 75mcg may be a little high. It could be the my meds though since my last batch I bought online. I'm not liking my doc at all these days although she did give me a script for Cytomel that I need to get filled today. So my conclusion on amount is different meds, different fillers, active ABs, and different people (plus more I'm forgetting) all make an impact when determining the dosage. That's why it's so important to track your vitals while you figure this out. Helps with the facts of what is going on inside.

### Shijin13 — 2012-12-06T13:21:34+0200

well, I'm using Dzlogic for managing the thyroid & other hormones in conjunction w/my PCP. she and I discuss labs first, then send in an update to Dzlogic - they up the rx. She signs the rx - and off we go.

obviously its not normal... but we'll get there. upping armour to 90mg... my intuition tells me I'm gonna need to go up to at least 120mg to address this.... but I'll go in little increments to get there b/c they don't want to make big moves...

> Dropped way back on the t3 as I am still getting the fluttery thing but not nearly as bad.Â  Pulse and temp are low.Â  Afraid to up it currently.Â  I am taking 10, 10 and 10 mcg thruout the day.Â  I functioning but wiped by the end.

What was your rT3 ratio?Â  I really don't know if it is a bad symptom for someone to have a very low rT3.Â  My last rT3 ratio was 40.Â  It could be even higher than that now. The more I think about your situation the more I think your problem with the flutters is your adrenals.Â  If they are weak (and we know yours are) the amount of T3 you can tolerate is minimized.Â  But that doesn't mean you don't need more.Â  Maybe a combo will work better for you: T3 in the morning and Armour in the afternoon. I cannot remember if the doc has commented on combo therapies.Â  If you have a T4 conversion issue than that won't help any.Â  Are you doing the Paul Robinson method for helping the adrenals?Â  Also I'm pretty sure you are still doing the morning sun, glasses at night, etc.Â  I cannot remember how to fix the PVN except by those items just mentioned.

Â 

I just started taking the brand Cytomel and thought my online T3 was going to be much weaker.Â  I started my dose at 12.5mcg instead of the normal 25.Â  In three hours I was wiped out! So for my evening dose I upped it to 18.75mcg.Â  But it was 7pm so I really didn't notice much but I didn't feel as bad as I did at 2pm.Â  I'm going to take that same dose today and see how I feel.

### bennyha — 2012-12-18T11:33:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-67811>

Hey guys. I had a question I just wanted to ask you for clarification while waiting for my T3 prescription to get here from Mexico. I have a High TSH (out of range), low free T3 (out of range), low but in range T4, and a Free T3/Rev T3 ratio of 11 (low and under suggested healthy range of 20). I had antibody tests done and they came back very low but weren't 0 either.Â 

I tried taking Armour for a few months this summer and it made me feel dreadfully worse and my face got really puffy, especially around my eyes. As I understand it, the Armour probably made me feels worse because of my low Free T3/Rev T3 ratio, correct? Basically, adding more T4 was just contributing to elevating my Rev T3?

So before I can consider Armour or natural thyroid or any treatment for that matter, the Rev. T3 needs to be cleared first by using a T3 only protocol?

Just trying to better understand the reasoning behind using the T3 treatment and why it is effective at helping getting rid of RT3. Â Â

### vkiernan — 2012-12-18T13:54:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-67818>

> **bennyha said**
> Hey guys. I had a question I just wanted to ask you for clarification while waiting for my T3 prescription to get here from Mexico. I have a High TSH (out of range), low free T3 (out of range), low but in range T4, and a Free T3/Rev T3 ratio of 11 (low and under suggested healthy range of 20). I had antibody tests done and they came back very low but weren't 0 either.Â 
>
> I tried taking Armour for a few months this summer and it made me feel dreadfully worse and my face got really puffy, especially around my eyes. As I understand it, the Armour probably made me feels worse because of my low Free T3/Rev T3 ratio, correct? Basically, adding more T4 was just contributing to elevating my Rev T3?
>
> So before I can consider Armour or natural thyroid or any treatment for that matter, the Rev. T3 needs to be cleared first by using a T3 only protocol?
>
> Just trying to better understand the reasoning behind using the T3 treatment and why it is effective at helping getting rid of RT3. Â Â 

There are now 2 school of thoughts (maybe more but I don't know them).Â  1. As you said above, no amt. of T4 as it is turning into RT3.Â  Over time this will clear the RT3.Â  But, you have to figure out why you have a RT3 problem in the first place.Â  Ferritin, cortisol or ???Â  You will continue to have the RT3 problem as long as you have the underlying issue going on.Â  Well, you will keep theÂ RT3 down but you might actually need T4 too and won't be able to successfully go there.Â  2. There is also a new T3 protocolÂ  by Paul Robinson but it doesn't necessarily have to be T3 only.Â  It can be NDT - as long as it contains T3.Â  You can read about this on STTM in further detail.Â  The quick and dirty with this protocol is that it will help your adrenals by raising your cortisol in the the am.Â  To do this though, you need to slowly dose early in the am and keep moving the dose back until you feel something.Â  Lot's more in the book or on STTM.Â  Helps to read the success stories on STTM.Â  One gal journals the whole experience and gives lots of details.

Patty is the expert out here.Â  Hopefully she will chime in.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-18T14:11:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-67820>

I can highly recommend Paul Robinson's Circadian Rhythm T3 method.Â  It helped my adrenals and my rT3.Â  But like Vkiernan said you really have to figure out what caused it in the first place and treat that at the same time.Â  I also recommend reading stories on his site or the STTM site to get an idea about how it works.

Also you may have had reactions to the fillers in Armour.Â  When you start the T3 go slow and notice how you feel.Â  Again it could be the fillers if you have some strange reactions. ### bennyha — 2012-12-18T18:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-67870>

Thanks Patty and vkiernan for your replies. Yes I have Paul's book and have read it and really liked the book. Very easy to read yet very detailed. My next question then is how does one figure out what is causing the Reverse T3? My actual RT3 levels aren't low, just my ratio in regards to Free T3 is. I seem to have some sort of leptin resistance that won't budge regardless of how long I do the protcol. It's weird because I am under 10% bodyfat and always have been and am pretty muscular. But a few years ago all the sudden stuff started going haywire and I started binging about once a week. Even doing the leptin reset, I have the strongest urge to binge about once a week and the urges are ridiculous. I think I got leptin resistant by overtraining/undereating/not sleeping/consuming huuuuge amounts of caffeine everyday/fasting. I think these combo of things did me in. But since I found this blog I'm following all the advice of Dr. K but still have signs of leptin resistance. I was fine until I started trying to go real low carb/ketosis levels after a month or so. I kept on with it and before I knew it I had all signs of hypothryoidism/adrenal fatigue.

I feel like if I could get my thyroid figured out then I could rid of the worst symptoms I have, which are not being able to sweat, constipation (I have maybe 2 stools a week and when I do they are just a few little nuggets), and a very severe brain fog. I have read many testimonials of people who had a number of problems reversed as soon as they got their thyroid panel normal. I guess if I have low Free T3 and T3 as well as a low Free T3/RT3 ratio and a high TSH, I have some sort of thyroid problem NOW. But fixing the thyroid won't really be fixing the problem, correct? I feel like after reading the Hormones 101 blogs hundreds of times, I keep coming back to T3 being very essential (along with Vitamin A co-factors) and at the top of the "chain" so if I am very low in Free T3 all of my other hormones are going to be low by default. I feel like a severely and chronically elevated cortisol probably caused all my problems in the first place, but now that I don't do any of the things that got me in trouble in the first place, I am confused on what else I can do short of supplementing with T3 to get things rolling and start feeling better. Even with all of Dr. K's protocols, I am gradually getting worse and not better. I'm sure they have kept me from getting really bad, but I want to feel GOOD and energetic again.

BH, Why don't you tryÂ the PR method?Â  Have you done an ASI to see where your cortisol is at currently?Â  How about your iron and ferritin level?Â  STTM has a lot of info on RT3.Â  You say your level of RT3 isn't low, actually it is supposed to be on the low side, not the high side and your FT3 is supposed to beÂ on the high side.Â Â Could you have Hashi's?Â  You really need some labs if you have not done so to tell you what is going on.Â  You are probably right that high cortisol got you into this mess.Â  Perhaps you have low cortisol now and really need PR's method.Â  T3 only is best iirc what he says about low cortisol and his protocol helping it.Â

### vkiernan — 2012-12-20T14:38:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-68072>

BH.Â  Good for you on going to try the protocol.Â  Can't help you with Hashi's as I don't understand it any better than you do.Â  I would really work on your gut if you have bad acne and constipation.Â  Maybe you can't afford the tests, but you can assume the worst and do what you can to fix it.Â  I wouldn't say T3 will completely fix the gut.Â  You probably have some bacterias, good and bad, out of whack that need other things to either kill the bad or proliferate the good.Â Â 

Do the homemade sauerkraut thing if youÂ can.Â  It is really cheap,Â easy to make and good for your gut.Â  You can do other fermented veggies too to limit the goitrogen affect from the cabbage.Â  I make homemade kombucha too.Â  I started it with 1 store bought bottle of the GT kind and it worked.Â  If you want to know how, just ask and I will help you.Â  I love this stuff with my BAB.Â  It cuts the dryness in the mouth inÂ the am and kind of refreshes it.Â  Way better than coffee and a lot cheaper.Â  Yummy stuff.Â Â 

If you eat dairy, I would cut it out completely considering the acne.Â  No butter for now either.Â  Jack told me with Hashi's - no dairy, no grains, no corn, no rice, no eggsÂ & no soy.Â  Guess what?Â  I tested and he was completely right!Â  I cannot eat these things.Â  I, like you, have only had a little antibody production so I question it too.Â  Jack says I do and I will be asking for better clarification in my consult so I can better understand.Â  I would cut out as much as possible and see if things start to change.Â  Hopefully, between your diet and the t3 things will start changing.Â 

What does your diet look like?Â  I'm guessing you are eating as much epi-paleo as you can afford.Â  He talks how that really changes and helps the gut.Â 

You do have pretty low inflammation.Â  That is a very good thing.Â  Are you CT'ing, that is supposed to raise the T.Â 

What was your RT3 number and the range?Â  Can you get any NDT?Â  You could use the t3 in the early am for the protocol and the NDT for the rest of the day.Â  The protocol is still supposed to work as long as you are getting the t3 in the early am.Â  This would help you save some money.Â  T3 only is best to clear the RT3 but sometimes you have to do what you can afford which isn't always optimal.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-20T23:01:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-68101>

I agree with everything Vickie said.Â  She is a very knowledgeable person!!!

Dr K. says that is you have any antibodies you may have Hashis. Although you also may not but he recommends to "act as if".Â  Since your thyroid numbers are pretty bad I definitely would do an AI epi-paleo eating until you can get further testing. Also your adrenals are a little whacky so that may be where your rT3 problem lies.Â  Also compare your iron labs to what STTM says are good ranges.Â  That should rule out iron.

Also remember that the lab range that is given on your lab results is not necessarily the ranges you want to go by.Â  I know I like my DHEA at around 350 and I think my range ends around 200.Â  The ranges are based on your age so the older you are the lower your range will be according to the lab.Â  I prefer a lot of my steroid hormones to be different than what the ranges say.Â  The ranges are in Dzugan's book and also Thierry Hertoghe (I think I spelled that right).

And you really will feel so much better on some kind of med with your numbers.

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-21T11:38:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-68120>

So I got labs back after 12 wks of adding T3 liothyronine to my current T4 levothyroxine. To introduce T3 med my endo cut back T4 from 112mcg to 75 and added 10mcg of T3. 3 weeks later my TSH was 6.8 and my T3 had come up a bit. Endo increased T4 to 88mcg and T3 to 15mcg. After a few weeks of not feeling great and BG high I increased T4 to 112 and T3 to 20mcg. I spread the 4 T3 5mcg doses throughou the day and chew them for sublingual absorption. T4 is one dose upon waking. So with these recent labs I have been on the 112 T4 and 20 T3 for over 8 weeks.

TSH has come down nicely to 1.4

Total T3 has dropped from 97 to sub range 70.2

Free T4 is unchanged at 1.2

T3 uptake which I don't know about is mid range at 30.2%

In summary although I have been feeling better with the T3 supp my T3 level has dropped. Mid afternoon fatigue has been virtually non existent and brain fog is much improved.

The other issue is since starting epi paleo in July my lipid profile has trended poorly. With each round of test my triglycerides have increased from sub-range to mid range, hdl /ldl ratio has trended worse not better and total cholesterol is above range 228. I keep the rest of my supp and food range very stable so as to minimize static. Any thoughts? Thanks Also my Vit D is high at 116. I have also been suppin with 100mg pregnenolone and 50mg dhea daily for the last 12 weeks. I feel great if that matters.

Mornin' BH.Â  Very good thoughts and questions.Â  As far as I know (limited) you can fix your RT3 but it depends on what is wrong with you to begin with whether you will need T3 or not.Â  Jack has mentioned in one of his CT blogs that if you get cold adapted (I'm sure this also means your hormones in balance and everything righted), you will not even need a thyroid.Â  This, of course, means a lot of "cold" work on your part to get that way.Â  I'm not really sure how low RT3 should be.Â  The Armour might be lowering it.Â  I don't know where you started.Â  My RT3 was at the high end of the range and when it finally cleared, it was under range.Â  But, I knew when it cleared so that tells me potentially RT3 should be at the bottom of the range.Â  I will dig out my STTM book and see if I can find something on it.Â  If you truly feel worse on the Armour (is the dose high enough and what were you on before?) then T3 only might be a great option or at least until you fix the conversion problem.

### fitness@home — 2013-01-06T07:18:27+0200

Just posting an update of my experience on T3 only. It has now been 12 weeks since I started the T3 only protocol. I am still on 75 mcg split into four doses daily (25, 12.5, 25, 12.5).

I have no physical indications of "clearing" as of yet and my body appears to be quite happy at this dosage. My annual physical is scheduled for January 18 and I will have her run all the thyroid labs then (including Reverse T3). Curious to know how suppressed the TSH has become and if the FT3/RT3 ratio is improved.

Will post lab results when I get them.

>

> > "Epic Post Jack. Its taken me 5 days to truly absorb the importance of it.
> > This epic has driven me to ask the following questions to you and my PCP (they can be rhetorical for you if theyâ€™re too specific).
> > Is my Low T3 really low if Iâ€™m eating an optimal Epi-Paleo RX? before this blog I would have thought having Low T3 is problematic â€“ but I think not. I think my body was doing what its designed to â€“ however, I wasnâ€™t feeding my brain what it needed (which is why Iâ€™m in the health state Iâ€™m in).
> > So eating Epi-Paleo and the increase in natural iodine consumption explains why I feel like my thyroid has turned on w/in the past 8 wks of really pushing the Epi-Paleo Rx.
> > Additionally is that Low T3 suboptimal b/c its impacting hormone production because of circadian mismatches and lack of iodine, with the end result being pregnenolone steal?
> > so, Theoretically, If this is my only problem, the Epi-Paleo Rx will fix this due to sufficient iodine, correct?
> > The next statement thatâ€™s rolling around my brain is: When you have Low T3, you produce rT3 as a workaround to prevent ROS, I think in certain situations (short term not stressed neolithic life) this could be a good thing b/c rT3 protects us from ROS in times of stress.
> > Additionally if rT3 is an oxidative protection mechanism it is also a good indicator of pregnenolone steal?
> > The final takeaway I get from this blog is that If I have Low T3, adoption of the Epi-Paleo Rx will allow my body to recover its Iodine state â€“ which will begin to push changes in the T4-> rT3 conversion, back to T4->T3, which the body can use to convert LDL w/Vit A -> pregnenolone
> > and from the pregnenolone -> progesterone -> all other steriod hormones.
> > All I can say is WOW."
>
> This was my hope too after reading BG-12, but then I kind of got a smackdown
>
> Again, I think the whole issue of context needs to come into play. Could epi-paleo be enough to improve T3, increase iodine, and have a positive trickle down effect on all hormones?!?! I think that CAN happen, but I also think it's possible some of us are too broken to have such an easy fix. I'm concerned some of us may have absorption issues or a breakdown in a critical pathway that just doesn't allow our body to optimally use the nutrients in the foods we're consuming.
> I'd much rather stick with your post Shijin and believe eating a strict epi-paleo diet is all I need, but after talking to Jack I'm pretty convinced I'm going to need more than just diet alone.

I know this is January, but I am getting what you say...I discovered over the last 5 months that diet is certainly not alone. Now I'm having all the same problems I had in the beginning, essentially I'm at sqaure one and am in pretty much the same state as I was in last year in January. Malabsorption for sure---I thought epi-paleo was the 'cure' or whatever but 6 months of no menses...then things got crappy starting end of November and they seem to be continuing that way. Now no matter how well I eat, it does not help. I've had a darkness under my eyes for months now which I kept thinking would go away---it never does. I'm always tired. My bbt is always low and also fluctuates like crazy. I have to 'happy' right now that at least for the past 4 days it's 36.5 ish around there ('C)...whereas before it was average of 36.2 and for a while in December it was 35.9! Cold hand and feet of course. Now in the last week I get a cold burning sensation on my feet and some parts of my legs. I don't get it at all...ugh. And of course gaining weight since beginning of December (so not even because of Christmas). I think I'd take the constant infection feeling that I had in Sept Oct. Nov. then being fat stupid and tired...ugh. It is what I 'fought' so hard ALL last year and then in an INSTANT it all goes to hell.

I am taking Vitex 3x/day for the last week...so I don't know if this is doing anyting bad/good...? If I get my period I guess it's good, but in December I thought I was going to get it due to massive PMS but nope...just the PMS w/o the detox.

End of Nov. I took betaine hcl + digestive aid...maybe I shouldn't have b/c I think things got worse? after that...? No clue.

I take vit D now too and very high doeses since I figured I lost some over the Fall with whatever possible infections I was fighting...but I don't know. I take 10 000 IU /day but I will soon go down to 5000 IU / day once I think it makes sense to lower it...I'm trying to boost my immune system right now.

I take zinc, potassium and b6 to help with water retention (which affects my joints) and also affects my breathing (often...as in very shallow and hard to get air).

Ii took 7keto for like...a week at the end of December but ran out and can't afford any more.

I have a green tea extract since I figured from the Adrenal Rx it could be helpful, but it DOES have rice flour as binder...does that screw me up? (Note: I had these problems before I took the green tea extract w/ rice flour...)

I was taking some vit C but then I read that it can stop progesterone?? I obviously don't want that...but on the other hand, should I take it so as to 'induce' my cycle as some people believe can happen with vit C? If it's true that my progesterone is raising maybe it's pushing my 'what I thought was under control' estrogen and hence causing me these maladies?

I have a post on IGF 1 and progesterone under: Adrenal rx and Leaky gut rx.

At the end of the day, what I'm saying is that OBVIOUSLY diet is not enough...:-( I need some help and I have not gotten any from doctors yet.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-06T13:55:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-69605>

O, like benny ha mentioned:

For Sept. Oct. and Nov. my main concerns were bloating, constipation (or little turds) with a period of diahrea for a few days during the middle of each month (in December too).

I didn't feel as tired during these months as I do no since beginning of December. My eyes feel so heavy and tired.

I gave up coffee (only 4 days ago). I do miss it and now my life is even more restrictive it seems...can't I enjoy a single cup of coffee even? I really do feel cursed somehow.

I think I'd rather have the constipation etc. from the Fall but at least feel sharp in the mind and more optimistic then weight gain, feeling like sludge is building up in my system, sleep sucks (but it really wasen't as good as I tried to convince myself over the last few months).

I have a doctors appointment tomorrow but I don't have faith since any other doctor I tried to see in the last months were not helpful at all.

### Inger — 2013-01-06T14:10:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-69607>

Zorica.. what if you have the same issues as Martin? Did you read the last posts in his journal? About the sulfur issue? So what if you try limit meat and eat no sulfur veggies, but maybe concentrate on seaweeds instead? They should be well tolerated!
Do not feel cursed Zorica. You will figure it out! Just keep your ears and eyes open! Keep looking.

Go out and look up in the sky. It always helps me when I feel I cannot take more.
Do some good things for you, like a pedicure, some beautiful music, talk to good friends or anything that has nothing to do with food pleasure. It will help when you adapts to new food I think.

Maybe the first days without coffee are worse until you adapt... or try it little by little, drink only a mini coffee every morning or so and then look how you feel. To me it is definitely an issue with the amount. A tiny bit is okay and the worse I feel the less I know it does my body good/the less I tolerate.

Try to actively get some earthing in! Might be one reason why you felt better in summer! Maybe you run barefoot a lot and you are more outside then now. I know this affects me. The more I think about it the more I am sure it has a lot to do with earthing why I feel so good in Scandinavian summer! It is also not too hot here summertime, mostly! Just perfect.

Zorica you have us all here to support you, just that you know!

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-06T17:21:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-69639>

Wow, thanks Inger for your kind words!! It means a lot!

I was under the impression recently that eating onions helps to inhibit estrogen. So I have eaten a lot of onions lately. I enjoy them to be honest. Hmmm.

When I got back from my parents I must admit that I've been eating a lot of seaweed in soups and I think maybe slowly that is helping me out. Today although I put a negative post on here I didn't feel so tired in the morning after breakfast like I have for the past few weeks and again after lunch I didn't feel as dragged down. My eyes feel tired but they feel more sharp as compared to before. I went skating today which was super fun. I feel like after the skate my eyes were even sharper. Hopefully it's a good sign in a good direction.

I can speculate that the changing light cycle from end of October to November severely impacted me negatively. I can't blame that only, but I'm not sure it helped. It's hard to look at the sun if it doesn't even show and also it's hard to sleep in until almost 8am in order to look at the sun (if it's shining). I was getting up at 6am from Sept. to Dec., but now I am making myself sleep until 7am (at least if not a little later now). I don't know if Jack thinks it a good idea, but I really think for next Fall I should definitely get a light box for the AM and/or help to minimize the effects of diminishing lights. I'm actually very sensitive to light changes like that and hence why I responded so positively to circadian timing in the summer (looking at the sun when waking at ~6-6:30).

You're right about the earthing: I wear sandals from May until October and often I am bare feet. In winter of course my feet are covered. I wonder what I should do for earthing in winter? Maybe just go in my back yard in the snow with no shoes?

If I have any modicum of self restraint I think I will hold off on the coffee for at least a few weeks. Just to see if my body needed a break; not from the coffee per se, but a break which the coffee might not have been helping with. I'm drinking green tea now instead. lol

I think when I'm at the doctors I want to check functional health of my ovaries and such and for endometriosis type stuff. I don't see how I would have that after eating healthy, but maybe it's from my previous years ever since puberty which always built up. And btw I never truly ate SUPER crappy, only a few years in university where yes, I ate crap----like chips and pop. I craved the salt and I craved the sugar. But growing up I still ate meats and veggies even if I had cereals and rice. (And no pop when growing up and I didn't like juice).

One thing which I never feel deprived of is dairy (well I hope butter doesn't count). I don't like milk or yoghurt etc. I tried some yoghurt and kefir in December (end of Nov?) and I really don't know if that seriously affected me and/or excaserbated my symptoms I was having. I do not eat these anymore...no way. Don't know what I was thinking other than GAPS and bacteria.

Again thanks for your kind words and I'll check out Martin (?) and about the non sulfer veggies and meat? In regards to meats and seafoods I'm a bit screwed financially this month so I have to make do with what I can get. So the seafood will definitely be less this month. But I have liver and heart and still SOME seafood.

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-01-08T17:44:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-69937>

> **LinD said
>
> i came across something on the STTM site that concerns me....
> _"Why does my body produce RT3 (Reverse T3)? Your body, especially the liver, can constantly be converting T4 to RT3 as a way to get rid of any unneeded T4. In any given day, it’s stated that 40% of T4 goes to T3 and 60% of T4 goes to Reverse T3.
> But in any situation where your body needs to conserve energy and focus on something else, it will change the above percentages, changing the conversion of RT3 to 50% or more, and the T3 goes down, down. Examples are emotional, physical, or biological stress, such as being chronically or acutely sick (the flu, pneumonia, etc), after surgery, after a car accident or any acute injury, chronic stress causing high cortisol, being exposed to an extremely cold environment_**_, diabetes, aging, or even being on drugs like beta blockers and amiodarone. But there’s another reason for thyroid patients."_
> [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/reverse-t3/](http://http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/reverse-t3/)
> Would the CT exacerbate this then?? or the cold pack I'm holding against my neck (right at the thyroid site! Eeek!)
> Thoughts?

Hey

Just catching up with this thread. I had wonky results for my last thyroid panel and have been trying to get everything straightened out. my t3 is off again.

i have hashi's and now have TOO HIGH T3 which is NOT the norm for me. I struggled after intitial diagnosis with getting my t3 up (V - def feel for you on the swollen part, that was my #1 complaint to the thyroid meds).

I went most of last year doing great on a combo of armour and cytomel and felt terrific. then this last month or so something felt off so i had testing done again and was surprised to see it was too high (expected to see it too low as my appetite was nonexistent, yet i was gaining weight, and i felt tired...turns out sometimes the symptons of hyper and hypo can mimick each other...in college i had graves (now in remission, but autoimmune related but hyPER) and gained which goes to show sometimes hyper = weight gain...sigh, but i digress...

anyway, I am off cytomel and will retest soon to see if its helped at all. still not 100% and feel swollen EVERYwhere...my breast are at least a cup size bigger and sore as hell (no i am not preggers).

I find this quote interesting bc i always seem to have some type of issue with my thyroid during the winter and wonder how the cold/lack of sunlight might negatively affect me. although, i guess seeing that I need less meds now is actually a good thing but still. part of me wonders if i might be better off moving to a place where the temp stays consistent bc this re-jiggering of my meds is always such a b#$ch

Is being on P causing my dependancy on thyroid meds to go down, was it the LDN? is it the fact that my anitbodies are no longer attacking it as much (this time last year my antibodies were over 1000 (off the charts) and now they are in the 200s.

so confused

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-09T10:02:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-70044>

Well that's good for you that you have a doctor that will work with you and help you. No such luck for me. I have to fly blind and things only get worse, YAY!

I'm back at sqaure one after an entire year. I thought I made all this progress then things went to hell since end of November. So, I really don't know. I tried making 'winter' work for me, but it's the same every year and now I've lost time again for the nth year. Based on my past history maybe I'll start to get better in March. Wow that's so far away from now and yes I lost 5 months.

I can't afford labs, I can't find a good doc that will do ANY tests, I can't afford JK consult etc. I can' t find a job either. So yes my life sucks flat out. 'Maybe' if things get better I can have a better 'attitude'. I did in the Fall and it got me no where things insideously crept up on me and now I'm scrambling from the bottom. :/

I did get my period after six months. I guess that's a good thing but I don't know b/c it only had PMS since basically the end of November and of course weight gain...EAT HEALTHY FOR WHAT?!!!!! While I watch all my friends enjoy life and me I get punished instead! Bollocks!

I can't take it any more!!

Here's some supplements I tried taking since end of November but not all at once and more in sequential order. (With money that was not even mine). Just to let you know.

~Nov. 20 betaine hcl and bile salts \*because I felt like I wasn't digesting food properly and bloating constipation and nasty thick coating on my tongue that was not going away after over a month. -ran out of betaine in Dec. stopped using it. digestive salts: I resumed today...I have no idea what I'm doing.
Dec. vitamin D 10 000 IU a/day (still take this)
\-Dec. 10 ish: zinc potassium and b6 (+GABA) to help with MASSIVE water retention and swelling that was affecting my joints
\-around same time: Thyrosense supplement (again because I felt so desperate and was thinking my thyroid was at issue with TSH at 2.38 which increased 'i only just discovered' from October 1.76.
End of Dec: 7ketodhea Only a week b/c it didn't last long and I can't afford it.

\-I took a lot of vit C in December but stopped since January b/c it might inhibit progesterone.
\-Dec. 28?: Vitex 3x/day (still take this and will continue until I'm finished the bleeding part) then I will go to 1 x /day
Jan: Green tea extract 3 x /day

Decemer I made the mistake (I think) of eating yoghurt and kefir. I was thinking along the lines of Campbell McBride so tried that to help with my potentially messed up gut. Maybe I ate too many dates too...god only knows...I don't know.

A little bit in December I started adding more onions because to help counteract estrogen effects. \*Didn't even know of epcot rx per se b/c I didn't have the webinar.

January: Loads of onions and I increased my seaweed intake to 3 x /day. I eat a mix of offal , bone cuts and seafood and bacon.

Anyways. I wish I could a. afford things b. get a job that is meaningful and high paying enough so that I can afford the necessary tests etc. c. Wish my body could cooperate.

I think for next Fall I have to try to do things a little differently. I am no longer an advocate of 'cold and dark'. SCREW THAT!! Not having sun in the morning (you go out and it's overcast like 9/10 times...) it just messes me up so badly. I will start vit D supplementation in September. I took colostrum in July of last year but stopped after b/c I couldn't afford it and I was concentarting on affording seafood (exclusively). Also I thought I 'fixed' myself and that the seafood would push me further to optimal....DEAD WRONG!!

I AM GOING TO USE A LIGHT BOX IN THE FALL FOR MY MORNING AM SUN. I don't care about how our species evolved. It is not working for me. I need my life and this is not it!

IF and that's a big fat IF (pretty much like how I am these days) I ever get a job (that pays) I will just buy NDT myself from online during the Fall. So as to make sure my T3 doesn't fall/cells don't get the signal properly.

From now I am going to address my liver (even MORE) if that's even possible. I eat broths, seafood, seaweed, onions etc. the whole epi-paleo. But I will stick with the Vitex, I will get some silymarin (maybe Feb. have to wait until I get a little bit more money again). I don't know if it's wise to take NAC + silymarin or if one or the other is best. I don't know maybe I'll try black cohash.

I have no clue: I fly blind b/c I can't afford things that require a large lump sum. I only end up wasting what little money I have b/c it is smaller pieces.

I took colostrum in Decemer (again with money that is basically not even mine). Ran out and apparently am getting some more from Charlotte from Germany. So I DO believe in miracles sometimes. Very kind of her to offer and send it to me.

So let's see how this goes.

I'm also thinking I will go back 'home'. I moved to Ottawa thinking it was going to be great oppurtunities etc for jobs...instead I just miss my family and friends. I do have freinds here luckily and I am living with some of them so that's good, but a. they are very busy with an actual life (unlike me) and b. they are not the friends who I consistently hang out with. Those freinds are back down south.

Long rant over. Hopefully I feel better sooner than later.

### Inger — 2013-01-09T12:33:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-70052>

Zorica.. I live in a even darker place than you. Finland. You know what I do? I try to get day light in my eyes whenever I can. At least 1 hour a day. It does not always work out but I could do it better. Jack says, to get daylight in your eyes at daytime is as important to have it dark at night. It does not need to be sun! Here the sun comes out very seldom in winter. It can go weeks in between. I do not care! I just look into the sky.
You need to go out Zorica, breath fresh air, get daylight! If you have no job you have much time to do that! I BET you will soon feel much much better! Your atitude will change! Do some earthing too.

I bet to eat the dairy and the dates was not a good idea at all.

Zorica you do the face dunks in the morning do you? They always make me feel so good! Like a mood booster really.

### vkiernan — 2013-01-09T14:31:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-70059>

**Zorica Vuletic said**

Well that's good for you that you have a doctor that will work with you and help you. No such luck for me. I have to fly blind and things only get worse, YAY!
I'm back at sqaure one after an entire year. I thought I made all this progress then things went to hell since end of November. So, I really don't know. I tried making 'winter' work for me, but it's the same every year and now I've lost time again for the nth year. Based on my past history maybe I'll start to get better in March. Wow that's so far away from now and yes I lost 5 months.
I can't afford labs, I can't find a good doc that will do ANY tests, I can't afford JK consult etc. I can' t find a job either. So yes my life sucks flat out. 'Maybe' if things get better I can have a better 'attitude'. I did in the Fall and it got me no where things insideously crept up on me and now I'm scrambling from the bottom. :/
I did get my period after six months. I guess that's a good thing but I don't know b/c it only had PMS since basically the end of November and of course weight gain...EAT HEALTHY FOR WHAT?!!!!! While I watch all my friends enjoy life and me I get punished instead! Bollocks!
I can't take it any more!!
Here's some supplements I tried taking since end of November but not all at once and more in sequential order. (With money that was not even mine). Just to let you know.
~Nov. 20 betaine hcl and bile salts \*because I felt like I wasn't digesting food properly and bloating constipation and nasty thick coating on my tongue that was not going away after over a month. -ran out of betaine in Dec. stopped using it. digestive salts: I resumed today...I have no idea what I'm doing.
Dec. vitamin D 10 000 IU a/day (still take this)
\-Dec. 10 ish: zinc potassium and b6 (+GABA) to help with MASSIVE water retention and swelling that was affecting my joints
\-around same time: Thyrosense supplement (again because I felt so desperate and was thinking my thyroid was at issue with TSH at 2.38 which increased 'i only just discovered' from October 1.76.
End of Dec: 7ketodhea Only a week b/c it didn't last long and I can't afford it.
\-I took a lot of vit C in December but stopped since January b/c it might inhibit progesterone.
\-Dec. 28?: Vitex 3x/day (still take this and will continue until I'm finished the bleeding part) then I will go to 1 x /day
Jan: Green tea extract 3 x /day
Decemer I made the mistake (I think) of eating yoghurt and kefir. I was thinking along the lines of Campbell McBride so tried that to help with my potentially messed up gut. Maybe I ate too many dates too...god only knows...I don't know.
A little bit in December I started adding more onions because to help counteract estrogen effects. \*Didn't even know of epcot rx per se b/c I didn't have the webinar.
January: Loads of onions and I increased my seaweed intake to 3 x /day. I eat a mix of offal , bone cuts and seafood and bacon.
Anyways. I wish I could a. afford things b. get a job that is meaningful and high paying enough so that I can afford the necessary tests etc. c. Wish my body could cooperate.
I think for next Fall I have to try to do things a little differently. I am no longer an advocate of 'cold and dark'. SCREW THAT!! Not having sun in the morning (you go out and it's overcast like 9/10 times...) it just messes me up so badly. I will start vit D supplementation in September. I took colostrum in July of last year but stopped after b/c I couldn't afford it and I was concentarting on affording seafood (exclusively). Also I thought I 'fixed' myself and that the seafood would push me further to optimal....DEAD WRONG!!
I AM GOING TO USE A LIGHT BOX IN THE FALL FOR MY MORNING AM SUN. I don't care about how our species evolved. It is not working for me. I need my life and this is not it!r />IF and that's a big fat IF (pretty much like how I am these days) I ever get a job (that pays) I will just buy NDT myself from online during the Fall. So as to make sure my T3 doesn't fall/cells don't get the signal properly.
From now I am going to address my liver (even MORE) if that's even possible. I eat broths, seafood, seaweed, onions etc. the whole epi-paleo. But I will stick with the Vitex, I will get some silymarin (maybe Feb. have to wait until I get a little bit more money again). I don't know if it's wise to take NAC + silymarin or if one or the other is best. I don't know maybe I'll try black cohash.
I have no clue: I fly blind b/c I can't afford things that require a large lump sum. I only end up wasting what little money I have b/c it is smaller pieces.
I took colostrum in Decemer (again with money that is basically not even mine). Ran out and apparently am getting some more from Charlotte from Germany. So I DO believe in miracles sometimes. Very kind of her to offer and send it to me.
So let's see how this goes.
I'm also thinking I will go back 'home'. I moved to Ottawa thinking it was going to be great oppurtunities etc for jobs...instead I just miss my family and friends. I do have freinds here luckily and I am living with some of them so that's good, but a. they are very busy with an actual life (unlike me) and b. they are not the friends who I consistently hang out with. Those freinds are back down south.
Long rant over. Hopefully I feel better sooner than later.

Zorica, pm me your e-mail if you want and I will do what I can to help you.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-01-09T16:04:32+0200

Yes I have stopped the dairy and the dates since December for sure.

Yes I do face dunks in the morning and mostly in the evening when it's sunset (as recommended so only just started doing this for a few days).

I do go outside luckily. I probably need to go without wearing my glasses then just like I was doing in the summer.

I hope at least with this cycle finally arriving things can be better now but based on my past history I'm scared that it won't. I really don't want to not fit in my clothes which are all tight now. { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. I'm not willing to buy new clothes just for that.

Yes, it's hard to have a good attitude when so many things are going wrong. If I could at least blame ice cream and pop or something like that, that would be great. > Was wondering about your t3 being high, if you aren't supplementing, then perhaps your hashi's is going hyper instead of hypo. High t3 with no meds is graves or hashi's isn't it?

Thanks - i wonder that as well. Sometimes I get confused what causes what. It seems too much estrogen can cause big boobs, but then so can too much progesterone...oh well, I guess that is why we have blood work done! I put the update through and asked about oral progesterone. Based on some of what I'm hearing regarding cream P, i think I might prefer the oral anyways (cream BHRT might build up in your fat tissue according to Chris Kressor...not sure what Jack thinks).

I WAS supplementing when the T3 got too high which is why I backed off. I had been taking both armour and cytomel for a while now and when I saw my T3 was too high (a first for me), it made my Dr suggest backing off the cytomel (still take a grain and a half armour though so still getting a combor of T4/T3. Now I'm curious to see if it will help. I will be asking for another thryoid test soon

thanks for the feedback on the gut stuff. I'm working on it! Jacks answer just confused me some! I feel like I am doing so much to heal myself, yet based on what was said, I feel like a ticking time bomb watiing to go off

### Charlotte — 2013-01-10T07:10:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-70134>

Hey Zorica, I would not say that you get punished. I would say that you made a mistake and you learn from it. Eating dates in December is probably not a good choice, when you should be in deep ketosis. So get back into ketosis. I found it very healing.
I always find that being out in nature helps in any weather. I can spend a day in the forest, get soaking wet, or in the snow and get ice cold and feel so good.
For me, Inger ist such a good example, how you can still make the most of it all, even when you have no money. So do not get desparate. I know that sometimes it all gets really trying, but don't make the mistake to think that you have to survive the next few months and then get better. It is always now in this moment. And you can do it.
Parcel arrived in Mount Hope CA this morning, so it is on its way.

### SeaJane — 2013-01-10T10:47:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-70152>

I am looking for some input on my situation. I have Graves and Hashi's antibodies and am hypothyroid with RT3 issue. I am trying to get my FT3 and FT4 up higher and reduce my RT3. I had been on 1/4 grain Naturethroid before doing labs and consulting with Jack (where I learned about RT3). My ND then put me on T3 SR (sustained release) 7.5mcg in addition to NT. After a month, my labs were improved (before FT4 and FT3 were below range) although still low and RT3 had gone down slightly. She then doubled my dosage to T3 SR 7.5 mcg, one in a.m. and one in p.m. I also started BHRT (low estrogen, low progesterone and bad ratio) during this time and mitochondria support per a consult with Jack. I got my labs back yesterday and there is hardly any change and my RT3 is back where I started.

Nov. labs TSH 0.7, FT4 1.2 (0.8 - 1.2), FT3 2.3 (2.3 - 4.2), RT3 30 (11-32)
Dec labs TSH 0.7, FT4 0.8 (0.8 - 1.2), FT3 2.7 (2.3 - 4.2), RT3 33 (11-32)

My ND is hesitant to up my dosage until I have been on BHRT at least a month since they are all related. I am trying to figure out whether this makes sense in my case. I likely have damage to thyroid from the antibodies so I am trying to understand whether it is reasonable to think getting my estrogen and progesterone levels up and in proper ratio will improve my thyroid or whether it is more reasonable to be tackling this from both angles (thyroid and sex hormones). Thoughts?

In addition, I have started to have some Raynaud type symptoms in my hands when cold that I have never had before. I also woke up last night in the middle of the night with cold feet and this weird sensation of feeling chilled even though I wasn't really cold. I am trying to figure out if I have a higher demand for thyroid hormone with adding in the hormones and doing the mitochondrial Rx .

### Claudia — 2013-01-15T21:47:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-70965>

**SeaJane said**

I am looking for some input on my situation. I have Graves and Hashi's antibodies and am hypothyroid with RT3 issue. I am trying to get my FT3 and FT4 up higher and reduce my RT3. I had been on 1/4 grain Naturethroid before doing labs and consulting with Jack (where I learned about RT3). My ND then put me on T3 SR (sustained release) 7.5mcg in addition to NT. After a month, my labs were improved (before FT4 and FT3 were below range) although still low and RT3 had gone down slightly. She then doubled my dosage to T3 SR 7.5 mcg, one in a.m. and one in p.m. I also started BHRT (low estrogen, low progesterone and bad ratio) during this time and mitochondria support per a consult with Jack.

What are you taking for mitochondria support? Jack suggested I had this problem on the forum. I can't afford a consult right now. I have been reading this entire thread. I have the hypo symptoms, high rt3, low t3, t4/t3 conversion issues as well. I have been taking first generic Cytomel, 5mcg started in July. I felt great in the summer, was at a good weight, I always feel best in the summer. I stayed at 5mcg and I was feeling better on it. In the early fall, started feeling worse hypo symptoms (doctor raised my dose to 10mcg) and I gained 10 lbs, 5 of which were due to trying a progesterone cream. My Pg was low but not crazy. I supplement with DIM so my estrogen isn't that high either...I think the DIM helped it come down but not sure. Dr. put my on compounded Pg cream, 200 mg. Which turns out to be a very high dose. I FELT HORRIBLE, gained 10 lbs, couldn't function, wanted to crawl in a hole and stay there, couldn't take care of the kids, brain fog, inability to concentrated, so tired, no energy. Bloated, depressed, swollen, just horrible. I got my period 3 days earlly, stopped the cream. Symptoms continued that whole week into next week. Doctor's response was to stop taking that cream if I didin't like it. THat's it.

I am not taking Cynomel from Mexico, 12.5, 2 hours before waking. Sometimes 1.5, sometimes 2.5 hours before. I don't have Paul's book, but thinking of getting it. My doc. won't support me going any higher than 25mcg. I feel good on the 12.5 right now and my free t3 is up close to high of the range. My TSH is very low in the range, and my free t4 is low, too, reverse t3 was not tested this last draw, don't know why. I have low iron, and can't budge the weight gained at all, I am 10-15 lbs overweight. I do Paleo but not quite Epi. I was doing CT in the spring but stopped in the summer and didn't resume. RIght now can't stand the thought of it, can't bring myself to do it.

I could post labs. I'm waiting on results of saliva adrenal cortisol test. My last lab work also indicated low DHEA. I picked up 7-KETO DHEA today. I have very high sex binding globulin and I'm taking nettleroot for that. And low iron, just under the range at 71. I have chelated iron, 18 mg, and told to take it 3 times per week, but I'm constipated so I stopped taking it. Took extra magnesium and then, get diarrhea, so I seem to be going in between both of those now.

Prog. is low...don't know what to do now. One of my docs gave me oral Rx Prog. 100mg. I did take it but I heard it's hard on the liver to take oral. I preferred a cream, but not sure which one to take and afraid of more weight gain.

Also, started taking betain and pepsin HCI because the doc thinks I need it. He is pretty good, super thorough, reminds me a little of JK but my insurance covers it mostly. It was a diff. doc that prescribed the 200mg cream Pg that caused me so many problem. That doc also found a MTHMR gene mutation so now I supplement with metylfolate and inject b12 (methylcobalamin) once weekly.

Wow! I wrote a lot. I feel for those of you who are struggling. I am, too. Can't get a handle on all of this, limited funds, too. And I eat so much better than all these people around me. My family eats Paleo, too, so that's great. I mean all the other people who are eating SAD, major junk food, soda, ugh. I have eaten so much healthier for 10 years now and 3 on Paleo. I just don't understand. Sometimes they seems better off. Why aren't they gaining 10 lbs in a month? So frustrating. And I am so strict with my diet. The only treat I have is dark chocolate, 90% or higher, or I make my own with coconut oil and cacao and stevia. I don't have sugar or grains.

Not sure about Hashi's. Everyone says I dont' have it. I do have <20 antibodies, though. AI runs in my family. My mom had rheumatoid arthritis, severe.

Thanks for reading. Appreciate the support. Don't know if I should get Paul RObinson's book and go it alone. Scares me a bit not to do it with a doctor. I did up my t3 dose to 12.5 Cynomel, easier to cut the tablet in half and take. I didn't tell the doc. I suspected the generic wasn't as potent. I do feel better. My hypo symptoms are better lately in the last month or so.

And what is mitochondrial Rx? Is anyone doing it? I just can't seem to keep up with everything JK

.

I have very high sex binding globulin and I'm taking nettleroot for that. And low iron, just under the range at 71. I have chelated iron, 18 mg, and told to take it 3 times per week, but I'm constipated so I stopped taking it. Took extra magnesium and then, get diarrhea, so I seem to be going in between both of those now.

Prog. is lowâ€¦don't know what to do now. One of my docs gave me oral Rx Prog. 100mg. I did take it but I heard it's hard on the liver to take oral. I preferred a cream, but not sure which one to take and afraid of more weight gain.

Also, started taking betain and pepsin HCI because the doc thinks I need it. He is pretty good, super thorough, reminds me a little of JK but my insurance covers it mostly. It was a diff. doc that prescribed the 200mg cream Pg that caused me so many problem. That doc also found a MTHMR gene mutation so now I supplement with metylfolate and inject b12 (methylcobalamin) once weekly.

Wow! I wrote a lot. I feel for those of you who are struggling. I am, too. Can't get a handle on all of this, limited funds, too. And I eat so much better than all these people around me. My family eats Paleo, too, so that's great. I mean all the other people who are eating SAD, major junk food, soda, ugh. I have eaten so much healthier for 10 years now and 3 on Paleo. I just don't understand. Sometimes they seems better off. Why aren't they gaining 10 lbs in a month? So frustrating. And I am so strict with my diet. The only treat I have is dark chocolate, 90% or higher, or I make my own with coconut oil and cacao and stevia. I don't have sugar or grains.

Not sure about Hashi's. Everyone says I dont' have it. I do have <20 antibodies, though. AI runs in my family. My mom had rheumatoid arthritis, severe.

Thanks for reading. Appreciate the support. Don't know if I should get Paul RObinson's book and go it alone. Scares me a bit not to do it with a doctor. I did up my t3 dose to 12.5 Cynomel, easier to cut the tablet in half and take. I didn't tell the doc. I suspected the generic wasn't as potent. I do feel better. My hypo symptoms are better lately in the last month or so.

And what is mitochondrial Rx? Is anyone doing it? I just can't seem to keep up with everything JK .

### vkiernan — 2013-01-15T22:46:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-t3.3783/post-70976>

Hi CD. My experience with prog. is oral and cream. I have overdosed with both. I like the oral personally better though. And, it doesn't stay in your system that long. Takes a lot longer to get rid of the cream if you use too much. Start with the oral every other day if you are scared and see how you do. Or, use 1/4th of the cream or less. Sometimes you have to figure this out for yourself. You should try to get a doc on board to test so you know if you are getting the right pg/e2 ratio ultimately. If you have options, go for it but go slow. Your body will most likely tell you when you have gone overboard. Sore breasts, bloating, etc.

STTM has lots of info on the t3 protocol but obviously the book is great. I used it myself to clear my rt3.

Don't waste your time comparing yourself to others, can't change that anyway. Just worry about you and fixing yourself. You stay the course and you will be great. Also, this does not happen overnight so give it a lot of time. The body changes but at its own pace, not ours.

>
> Just in case the above is confusing: I was responding to SeaJane at first, wondering about the mitochondria support she is taking? The first paragraph above is SeaJane's. Then, I start here: What are you taking for mitochondria support? Jack suggested I had this problem on the forum. I can't afford a consult right now. I have been reading this entire thread. I have the hypo symptoms, high rt3, low t3, t4/t3 conversion issues as well. I have been taking first generic Cytomel, 5mcg started in July. I felt great in the summer, was at a good weight, I always feel best in the summer. I stayed at 5mcg and I was feeling better on it. In the early fall, started feeling worse hypo symptoms (doctor raised my dose to 10mcg) and I gained 10 lbs, 5 of which were due to trying a progesterone cream. My Pg was low but not crazy. I supplement with DIM so my estrogen isn't that high eitherâ€¦I think the DIM helped it come down but not sure. Dr. put my on compounded Pg cream, 200 mg. Which turns out to be a very high dose. I FELT HORRIBLE, gained 10 lbs, couldn't function, wanted to crawl in a hole and stay there, couldn't take care of the kids, brain fog, inability to concentrated, so tired, no energy. Bloated, depressed, swollen, just horrible. I got my period 3 days earlly, stopped the cream. Symptoms continued that whole week into next week. Doctor's response was to stop taking that cream if I didin't like it. THat's it.

As someone said, I am not sure you want to decide what to do based on someone else. Each of our context's is different. And, I am not sure that I have the right mix at all for myself. I just started BHRT this month and I am experiencing weight gain although I am not sure if this is due to hormones alone or low FT3 or both. I just doubled my FT3 levels the last 2 days (up from 7.5mcg twice a day to 15 mcg twice a day) and am feeling much better. I am no longer constantly cold and the Raynaud's symptoms have gone way down. For mito support, I am taking d-ribose, coQ10, acetly-carnitine, and magnesium.

Is weight gain associated with progesterone, estrogen, or the ratio? When I just did progesterone with vaginal troches, I did not gain weight. Also, when I did progesterone cream at about 200mg/day for second half of cycle I did not get weight gain. Only this cycle where I am cycling estrongen and progesterone the whole time and the progesterone levels are much higher than earlier. I don't have any other symptoms (i.e., tender breasts, depressed, brain fog) and my sleep has been good. Anyways, as I write this out, I realize this is questions for the BHRT thread probably and will post it over there as well.

I thought I would share my new lab results and the effect that T3 can have if you need to clear Rt3. I've listed my August labs to the right where applicable.

Ok, so here are new test results:

Serum Iron 119mcg/dl (40-150)
TIBC(total IBC) 235 L (240-450)
%saturation 51 (15-55)
Ferritin 105 ng/ml (10-291) 93 in December 2011

FT4 .61 ng/ml (.89-17.6) 1.26 August 2012
FT3 3.88 pg/ml (2.30-4.20) 2.58 August 2012
RT3 5.9 ng/dl L (9.2-24.1) 34.9 August 2012
TSH .18 (.36-4.5). 2.55 August 2012
Rt3 ratio in August was 7.4 now 66
